input.body:
<!-- Type: feature-request -->

## Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

## VS Code Version: 1.126
## OS Version: Windows 11 Enterprise (26200)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. On Windows, install VS Code (Stable), then install a fork such as Cursor or VSCodium that ships its own `code` / `code.cmd` shim on `PATH`.
2. The fork's `code.cmd` ends up earlier on `PATH` (Cursor's installer prepends its `bin` dir).
3. From any terminal, run:
   ```
   code --install-extension ms-python.python
   ```
4. The extension installs into the **fork's** extension directory (`%USERPROFILE%\.cursor\extensions\`), not VS Code's (`%USERPROFILE%\.vscode\extensions\`). Same command was intended to target VS Code.

## The problem

`code` on Windows is a `code.cmd` shim that unconditionally invokes `Code.exe --ms-enable-electron-run-as-node <cli.js>` from the directory it lives in. When multiple VS Code-family editors are installed, the CLI has no way to say **"install into VS Code Stable's extension host specifically"** other than fully qualifying the path to the correct `code.cmd`.

This bites in three real workflows:

1. **Automation scripts** (dotfiles, `install-extensions.ps1`, CI runners) that assume `code --install-extension` targets VS Code ΓÇö they silently install to whichever fork's shim won the `PATH` race.
2. **Corp-managed machines** where VS Code was installed by SCCM/Intune and a user later installed Cursor ΓÇö the fork wins because it prepends `PATH` in user scope.
3. **Fresh laptop bootstrap** ΓÇö `code --list-extensions > extensions.txt` on the old machine, `Get-Content extensions.txt | ForEach-Object { code --install-extension $_ }` on the new one, and the extensions land in the wrong editor.

There is no diagnostic ΓÇö the fork's CLI happily accepts VS Code extension IDs. The user only notices when the extensions don't appear in VS Code.

## Proposal

Add one or more of:

**(a) `--target` flag** on the CLI (`code --install-extension <id> --target stable | insiders | <absolute path>`) that resolves to the correct extension host regardless of which shim was invoked. Default = the shim's own host (today's behaviour).

**(b) Print a one-line banner** when the invoked `code.cmd` belongs to a non-Microsoft fork, e.g. `note: 'code' resolved to Cursor at C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\Programs\cursor\bin\code.cmd`. Users could then re-run with a fully qualified path. Low-risk, no config surface added.

**(c) Environment variable** `VSCODE_CLI_TARGET=stable|insiders|/abs/path` that a wrapper script or user profile can set to disambiguate. Same effect as (a) without a new flag.

Even just (b) ΓÇö printing the resolved shim path when a fork owns `code` ΓÇö would save hours of "why isn't my extension loading" debugging.

## Related but not duplicate

- #40239 asks for a way to enable/disable extensions from a config file, not CLI targeting across forks.
- #5595 asks for platform-scoped settings, not CLI target selection.

Searched issue tracker for `code CLI install-extension fork`, `code shim PATH conflict`, `code --install-extension wrong binary` ΓÇö no existing report. Happy to close if I missed one.
input.title: Feature Request: 'code --install-extension' should disambiguate target when multiple VS Code forks are on PATH (Windows)

input.body:
<!-- Type: feature-request -->

## Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

## VS Code Version: 1.126
## OS Version: Windows 11 Enterprise (26200) ΓÇö corporate managed

## Steps to Reproduce

1. In a project workspace that handles customer/regulated data, open .vscode/settings.json.
2. Add "telemetry.telemetryLevel": "off".
3. Observe the setting is ignored ΓÇö value only takes effect when placed in User (application) settings.

## The problem

telemetry.telemetryLevel is registered with ConfigurationScope.APPLICATION and restricted: true (see [src/vs/platform/telemetry/common/telemetryService.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/telemetry/common/telemetryService.ts)). This means:

- A developer working on multiple projects on a corporate-managed machine cannot say "telemetry off for **this** workspace (which contains customer data under NDA) but leave the org-wide policy value in place for other projects."
- The only escape hatches today are the org-wide GPO/enterprise policy (TelemetryLevel), or flipping the user setting each time you switch projects ΓÇö both are heavy hammers.

Peer editors solve this: JetBrains IDEs allow project-level opt-out of Data Sharing that stacks over the application default. VS Code's own http.proxy, git.enabled, and other sensitive settings support workspace scope with the restricted flag gating trusted-workspace behaviour ΓÇö so there is precedent for this pattern.

## Proposal

Either:

**(a) Widen the scope** of telemetry.telemetryLevel to ConfigurationScope.WINDOW (or RESOURCE), keeping restricted: true so only trusted workspaces can lower the level. The value would monotonically **decrease** telemetry (workspace can only set Γëñ application level) ΓÇö never raise it above what the user/policy allows.

**(b) Add a new sibling setting** e.g. telemetry.workspaceOverride (enum: inherit | off, default inherit, scope WINDOW, restricted: true). off clamps the effective level to off for the window; anything else inherits the app-level value.

Option (a) is cleaner if the monotonic-decrease guarantee is acceptable. Option (b) is safer if you want to keep the primary setting application-scoped and add opt-in workspace clamp.

## Why this matters for corp/enterprise use

Enterprise VS Code users with mixed public/regulated projects on the same laptop currently either:
- Accept org-wide telemetry level for all workspaces (may leak metadata about regulated project structure).
- Manually toggle the user setting each context switch (error-prone).

A workspace-scoped clamp gives per-project rigor without disturbing the org-wide baseline.

## Not a duplicate of

Searched for telemetry workspace scope, telemetry.telemetryLevel workspace, telemetry per workspace project ΓÇö no existing issue captures this ask. #260375 covers **tool** enablement, not telemetry scope. If I missed one, happy to close.
input.title: Feature Request: Allow telemetry.telemetryLevel to be overridden at workspace scope (or new workspace-scope setting)

input.body:
# Renderer crashes with V8 heap OOM (~4 GB cap) ΓÇö regression introduced in 1.125 (runtime unchanged from 1.124)

<!-- Paste into https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new/choose ΓåÆ "Bug report" -->

## Type: Performance Issue / Crash

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled? ΓÇö Not yet fully confirmed.**
The OOM is in the **renderer** process; my heaviest extension (Sapling SCM) runs in the
separate **extension host**, so disabling extensions may not be conclusive. However, the
regression is reproducible on the *same machine and same workspace* purely by changing the
VS Code version (see A/B below), which points at workbench renderer code, not an extension.

## VS Code Version

- Version: **1.126.0** (also reproduced on 1.125.x)
- Commit: `7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a`
- Date: 2026-06-23
- Electron: 42.3.0 / Chromium: 148.0.7778.180 / Node: 24.15.0
- OS: macOS 26.5.1 (25F80), Apple M4 Pro, **48 GB RAM**

**Last known good: 1.124.2** (identical Electron/Chromium/Node ΓÇö see "Runtime is unchanged").

## Summary

The workbench **renderer process's V8 JavaScript heap grows until it hits V8's ~4 GB
per-isolate ceiling, then aborts**. The window dies with:

```
[error] CodeWindow: renderer process gone (reason: crashed, code: 5)
```

`code: 5` is how Electron surfaces a V8 fatal OOM abort at the window level ΓÇö the Crashpad
minidump confirms it is an out-of-memory (annotations below).

Physical RAM is irrelevant (48 GB free headroom); V8 caps each renderer heap at 4 GB. GPU
acceleration is **disabled**, so this is not a GPU/WebGL issue. Large integrated-terminal
output (e.g. `ls -la` on a big directory) reliably triggers the final crash, but the heap is
already sitting near 4 GB ΓÇö the terminal output is the last allocation, not the root cause.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a large multi-root / multi-window workspace (I run ~8 windows; heavy SCM activity).
2. Work normally for a while so the main renderer heap climbs (watch it in
   **Developer: Open Process Explorer** ΓÇö the window renderer approaches ~4000 MB).
3. Produce a burst of integrated-terminal output (e.g. `ls -la ~/Library`).
4. The renderer crashes; the extension host then exits cleanly ("renderer closed the MessagePort").

Repeats reliably: **20 of 20** recent Crashpad dumps carry `v8-oom` annotations.

## Runtime is unchanged ΓÇö so this is a VS Code JS regression

Per the community version tracker (ewanharris/vscode-versions), **1.124, 1.125 and 1.126 all
ship the identical runtime**: Electron 42.3.0, Chromium 148.0.7778.180, Node 24.15.0.

Combined with the controlled A/B below, the increase in renderer heap consumption is in
**VS Code's own workbench JavaScript shipped in 1.125**, not in Chromium/V8.

## Controlled A/B (same machine, same workspace, same synced settings)

| VS Code | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 1.124.2 | Stable, no crashes |
| 1.125.x / 1.126.0 | Renderer OOM-crashes; recurs within a session |

A second laptop with the **same synced settings/extensions** does not crash ΓÇö consistent with
Settings Sync not replicating open windows/folders/panels or on-disk workload, i.e. the second
machine's renderer simply never accumulates to 4 GB.

## Evidence ΓÇö Crashpad minidump heap annotations (renderer, main isolate)

Extracted from `~/Library/Application Support/Code/Crashpad/completed/*.dmp`:

```
--type=renderer  (Code Helper (Renderer))
v8-oom-is-main-isolate:            true
electron.v8-oom.heap.limit:        4294967296   (4.00 GiB ΓÇö V8 hard cap)
electron.v8-oom.heap.total:        4168581120   (3.88 GiB)
electron.v8-oom.heap.used:         4162167016   (3.88 GiB ΓÇö at the ceiling)
electron.v8-oom.heap.external_memory: 25657430  (~24 MB)
electron.v8-oom.heap.malloced_memory: 1069084   (~1 MB)
electron.v8-oom.heap.detached_contexts: 0
electron.v8-oom.heap.native_contexts:   3
v8-oom-global-handle-count:        3072
```

Embedded V8 GC log at the moment of death (GC freeing nothing, efficiency ΓåÆ 0):

```
10383 ms: Mark-Compact 3993.3 (4000.5) -> 3993.3 (4000.7) MB ... allocation failure; scavenge might not succeed
10771 ms: Mark-Compact 3993.3 (4000.7) -> 3993.3 (4000.7) MB ... (current mu = 0.000) allocation failure; scavenge might not succeed
```

## Interpretation (what to look at)

- **`detached_contexts: 0`, `native_contexts: 3`** ΓåÆ not a leaked-webview / detached-DOM leak.
- **`external_memory: ~24 MB`, `malloced: ~1 MB`** ΓåÆ not ArrayBuffers / external buffers.
- Therefore ~3.88 GB is **ordinary JS objects on the managed V8 heap in the main workbench
  renderer** ΓÇö a growing in-heap data structure in workbench JS. Given the terminal trigger,
  the integrated-terminal buffer/scrollback or an accumulating workbench cache are prime
  suspects. A live heap snapshot (retainer graph) is needed to name the exact structure; the
  minidump doesn't carry the object graph.

## What I ruled out

- GPU / WebGL ΓÇö `terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration` already off; crash is JS-heap.
- Physical memory ΓÇö 48 GB, crash is at V8's 4 GB per-renderer cap, not OS OOM.
- Settings / extensions config ΓÇö identical (synced) on an unaffected machine.
- Chromium/Electron/Node ΓÇö byte-identical across 1.124ΓÇô1.126.

## Ask

Please investigate what in the 1.125 workbench renderer increased steady-state / peak JS heap
usage enough to reach the 4 GB V8 ceiling. Happy to attach a `.heapsnapshot` and full Crashpad
dumps on request.
input.title: Renderer crashes with V8 heap OOM (~4 GB cap) ΓÇö regression introduced in 1.125 (runtime unchanged from 1.124)

input.body:
Version: 1.126.0

OS:
Kubuntu 26.04
KDE Plasma 6.6.4
Wayland (also reproducible when forcing X11 via --ozone-platform=x11)

Issue:

When using:

"window.menuStyle": "native"

the context menus are rendered at a much larger scale than the rest of the VS Code UI.

The editor, tabs and explorer use one scale, while native context menus use a noticeably larger font and spacing.

If I change:

"window.menuStyle": "custom"

the scaling becomes correct immediately, but the KDE Global Menu no longer works.

Expected:
Native menus should have the same scale as the rest of the UI while still supporting KDE Global Menu.

Additional information:
- Display scale: 100%
- GTK theme: Breeze
- Plasma theme: Breeze
- No QT_SCALE_FACTOR or GDK_SCALE set
- Reproduced with a clean VS Code profile (--user-data-dir)
- Reproduced with both Wayland and X11
input.title: Native menus are incorrectly scaled on KDE Plasma 6.6 with Global Menu enabled

input.body:
### Problem

The `agentHost.turnCompleted` telemetry event (`IAgentHostTurnCompletedEvent` / `IAgentHostTurnCompletedClassification` in `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentHostTelemetryReporter.ts`) includes a `permissionLevel` field. Unlike the rest of that event (`timeToFirstProgress`, `totalTime`, `result`, `model`), **`permissionLevel` is not generic across agent-host providers** ΓÇö it is only ever populated for Copilot AH and is empty for Claude and Codex AH.

### Root cause

The value is sourced in shared code from the platform `autoApprove` config key:

```ts
// src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentSideEffects.ts  (_getTurnTelemetryContext)
const permissionValue = state?.config?.values[SessionConfigKey.AutoApprove];
```

Only the Copilot provider writes `SessionConfigKey.AutoApprove`. The other harnesses model permissions/approvals with their own, namespaced config keys:

- **Claude** ΓÇö `ClaudeSessionConfigKey.PermissionMode` (`permissionMode`): `default` / `acceptEdits` / `bypassPermissions` / `plan` / `auto`
- **Codex** ΓÇö `CodexSessionConfigKey.ApprovalPolicy` (`codex.approvalPolicy`), plus `codex.sandboxMode`, `codex.networkAccessEnabled`, `codex.webSearchMode`, etc.

So the shared turn-completed path bakes in a Copilot-specific assumption (`autoApprove`), and `permissionLevel` comes back empty for every Claude/Codex turn.

### Why it doesn't belong on the generic event

`agentHost.turnCompleted` is otherwise a harness-agnostic *turn performance* event. `permissionLevel` is a *provider-specific config snapshot*, and there is no lossless normalization across harnesses ΓÇö Codex's `sandboxMode ├ù networkAccess` axes and Claude's single `permissionMode` axis don't map cleanly onto Copilot's `default` / `autoApprove` / `autopilot`. Putting one provider's config field on the shared classification makes the metric look generic when it isn't, and silently yields empty data for 2 of 3 providers.

### Proposed direction

- Remove `permissionLevel` from the shared `agentHost.turnCompleted` event so it stays purely harness-agnostic (timing / result / model).
- Emit permission/config telemetry from **provider-specific code** instead (e.g. `agentHost.copilot.*`), so each harness reports its own config faithfully and the values are accurate per provider. This mirrors the provider-namespaced pattern (e.g. the proposed `agentHost.claude.subagentResolved`).

Internal context / scorecard gap: microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#8205.

### Code references

- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentHostTelemetryReporter.ts` ΓÇö `permissionLevel` on `IAgentHostTurnCompletedEvent` + classification
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentSideEffects.ts` ΓÇö `_getTurnTelemetryContext` reads `SessionConfigKey.AutoApprove`
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentHostTurnTracker.ts` ΓÇö plumbs `permissionLevel` through `turnStarted` ΓåÆ `turnCompleted`
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/common/sessionConfigKeys.ts`, `common/claudeSessionConfigKeys.ts`, `node/codex/codexSessionConfigKeys.ts` ΓÇö the divergent per-provider key namespaces
input.title: Telemetry: `agentHost.turnCompleted.permissionLevel` is provider-specific and shouldn't live on the generic turn event

input.body: The `/btw` command on the CLI let's you ask quick questions about what your agent is working on without interrupting/steering the main loop. I've found this massively helpful as the agent sessions run longer and longer and miss it in Agents Window compared to the CLI.
input.title: expose /btw from the Copilot CLI

input.body:
Polish item tracking the chat tab bar visibility behavior in the Agents window session header.

## Context

Addressed in #323789. Previously the chat tab strip disappeared once only a single open chat remained, hiding closed-but-reopenable chats and any chat whose title had diverged from the session title.

## Behavior after the PR

The tab strip (a single shared observable `IActiveSession.shouldShowChatTabs`) is shown when:
- the session has more than one chat (counting closed, non-tool chats), or
- its single remaining chat's title diverged from the session title.

Chat-to-chat navigation (next/previous chat, the `Ctrl+Tab` switcher) is gated separately on `SessionHasMultipleOpenChatsContext` (more than one *open* tab), so those chords stay a no-op / fall back to session navigation when only a single open chat remains.

## Possible follow-up polish

- [ ] Validate the visual transition when the strip appears/disappears (no layout jump in the header).
- [ ] Confirm the trailing **New Chat** / **Conversations** controls move cleanly between the header and the strip across all the visibility transitions (closed chats, diverged title, drafts).
- [ ] Double-check keyboard navigation precedence (`Ctrl+Tab`, `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+]`/`[`) in the single-open-chat edge cases.
- [ ] Verify behavior with tool-origin (subagent) chats present.

Ref: #323789
input.title: Polish: chat tab bar visibility for closed chats and diverged chat title
issue #1000

input.title: Auto discount does not show in Copilot CLI model picker

input.body: In v1.126 supposedly there is a [unified model customization picker](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_126#_unified-model-customization-picker) that allows you configure thinking effort and context window size in the same panel. But the corresponding [simplified model hover](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_126#_simplified-model-hover) does not show any indication of context window size. **Thus for models that don't allow customization, there is no longer any indication of context window size.** 1. Click the model selector. 2. Hover over "Claude Haiku 4.5". **Expected:** Some indication of the context window size of "Claude Haiku 4.5". **Actual:** No indication of the context window size of "Claude Haiku 4.5". I personally happen to remember the context window size of Claude Haiku 4.5, but I can't (or shouldn't need to) have all of them memorized. This is especially important if I'm working with another more powerful model and I want to "drop down" to a lesser model; I need to know its context window size to know if it can support the current context! ``` Version: 1.126.0 (user setup) Commit: 7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a Date: 2026-06-23T01:46:13Z Electron: 42.2.0 ElectronBuildId: 14159160 Chromium: 148.0.7778.97 Node.js: 24.15.0 V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 ```
input.title: GitHub Copilot model selector doesn't indicate model context window size if unconfigurable in v1.126

input.body:
Version: 1.128.0-insider (user setup)
Commit: c4632cac4f0a71ef09ccc2b6847c7ad19e98fcb6
Date: 2026-06-30T16:18:14Z
Electron: 42.5.0
ElectronBuildId: 14500164
Chromium: 148.0.7778.271
Node.js: 24.17.0
V8: 14.8.178.33-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT arm64 10.0.28000
Feature: Chat
Selected Model: Any

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure a custom Chat model with editor-scoped/per-model configuration, such as a custom endpoint model with reasoning effort or other model-specific settings.
2. Start a Chat request using that custom model and confirm that the initial request uses the expected selected model configuration.
3. Trigger a resend/follow-up path such as ΓÇ£Try AgainΓÇ¥, a confirmation response button, a sample request, slash command sample, or another Chat UI flow that sends using a different widget model.
4. Observe that the UI still appears to have the same model selected, but the follow-up request is not routed to the newly-selected model.

## User story

I use VS Code Chat with custom language models configured through `chatLanguageModels.json`. Some of these models require per-model settings, such as reasoning effort or other model-specific options.

While testing a custom endpoint, I found that a normal first request used the expected model configuration. But after using retry/follow-up flows, the request no longer behaved as if the selected model configuration was being honored. From the userΓÇÖs perspective, the Chat UI still appeared to have the correct model selected, but the request sent by the retry/follow-up path was not equivalent to a fresh send with that same visible model selection.

This is confusing because the model picker state suggests one thing, while the request options used by some resend/follow-up paths can behave differently.

## Expected behavior

Any Chat flow that sends or resends using the currently selected model should preserve both:

- the selected model ID
- the editor-scoped model configuration associated with that model

The visible model picker state should match the request options used for retry/resend/confirmation/follow-up flows.

## Actual behavior

Some call sites pass only `userSelectedModelId` from the widgetΓÇÖs selected model state. They do not also pass `userSelectedModelConfiguration`.

That can drop editor-scoped configuration and cause follow-up requests to use configuration that does not match the visible model picker state.

## Technical notes

`IChatSendRequestOptions` supports both `userSelectedModelId` and `userSelectedModelConfiguration`.

The service layer can fall back to global model configuration when only a model ID is provided, but that is not equivalent to preserving editor-scoped configuration from the active Chat widget.

Several widget-backed resend/confirmation paths construct request options independently. This makes it easy for some paths to pass the model ID without the associated scoped configuration.

A safer pattern is to centralize construction of selected model request options so the model ID and scoped configuration are always passed together.

## Impact

This affects custom model providers and any flow where per-model configuration matters. It is especially visible with custom endpoints where configuration controls important runtime behavior such as reasoning effort, context/output settings, or provider-specific options.
input.title: "Try Again" in Chat does not allow selection of new model

input.body:
Symptoms:

A session stops and says it's waiting for an async subagent to complete. The subagent never completes. If you check the events.jsonl "Open Copilot CLI State File", the last entry is a `permission.requested` that is never resolved.

The bug involves
- Main session is unsubscribed by client
- It goes idle while waiting for the async subagent
- It's evicted from AH memory
- Subagent completes and tries to wake up the main agent again
- This doesn't work when it has been evicted

This looks really similar to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/323738 but they are not the same
input.title: Async subagents can be blocked

input.body:
Type: Bug ## Summary In VS Code Insiders, deselecting built-in agent tools in the Configure Tools picker does not persist. After deselecting all tools or reducing the selection to Codebase only, confirming with OK, and reopening the picker, the default built-in tools are active again. Adding additional tools is remembered. Removing default tools is not. ## Expected behavior The tools selected or deselected in Configure Tools should determine which tools are available for the current request, as documented. ## Actual behavior The tool picker restores a large built-in default set after deselection. Chat Debug View confirms that the actual request still contains 28 tools, including unrelated notebook, browser, task, edit, terminal, and extension tools. ## Reproduction 1. Open Chat and select Agent. 2. Open Configure Tools. 3. Deselect all default tools, or leave only Codebase selected. 4. Confirm with OK. 5. Reopen Configure Tools. 6. Observe that the default tools are active again. 7. Send a request and inspect it in Chat Debug View. ## Observed request impact The Chat Debug View showed: - `tools (28)` - `prompt_tokens: 10180` - `maxPromptTokens: 10240` - model: `qwen3-coder:30b` - endpoint: `http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions` This leaves almost no input budget for a local model and makes a controlled single-tool test impossible. The same behavior also affects cloud-model workflows, where unexpectedly retained tool definitions and context can increase input-token and AI-credit consumption. ## Environment - VS Code Insiders: `1.127.0-insider` - Commit: `4fe60c8b1cdac1c4c174f2fb180d0d758272d713` - Windows: `10.0.26200` - Workspace size: more than 20,000 files - Custom Endpoint model: local Ollama `qwen3-coder:30b` - Copilot Chat file logging enabled ## Notes A direct Ollama OpenAI-compatible API request with one simple tool returned valid structured `tool_calls`. The issue appears to be in tool-selection persistence or request construction inside VS Code/Copilot Chat, not in OllamaΓÇÖs basic tool-calling support. I can provide redacted Chat Debug View exports and screenshots. input.body:
## Bug Report

**VS Code version:** 1.123.0
**Copilot Chat version:** 0.51.0
**OS:** Windows

---

### Describe the bug

When the workspace repository is in detached HEAD state, starting a new Copilot CLI agent session fails to create an isolated worktree. The session silently falls back to `isolationEnabled: false` with no visible error to the user.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a git repository where the main working directory is in detached HEAD (e.g. `git checkout <commit-sha>`)
2. Start a new Copilot agent session that triggers worktree creation

### Expected behavior

The worktree is created successfully (the underlying `git worktree add` command succeeds), and the session runs with isolation enabled.

### Actual behavior

The Copilot Chat extension log shows:
```
[ChatSessionWorktreeService][_createWorktree] Failed to create worktree for isolation.
```
ΓÇªand the session starts with `isolationEnabled: false, worktreePath: undefined`.

Notably, the `git worktree add` command itself **succeeds** ΓÇö the worktree directory is created on disk ΓÇö but the result is discarded.

### Root cause

In `ChatSessionWorktreeService._createWorktree`, after `gitService.createWorktree()` succeeds, the result is only returned if `activeRepository.headBranchName` is truthy:

```typescript
if (worktreePath && activeRepository.headCommitHash && activeRepository.headBranchName) {
    // build and return properties
}
// falls through ΓåÆ logs error, returns undefined
```

In detached HEAD, `headBranchName` is `undefined`, so the condition fails even though the worktree was created successfully. However, `headBranchName` is only actually used as a fallback when `baseBranch` is not provided:

```typescript
const baseBranchName = baseBranch ?? activeRepository.headBranchName;
```

The guard is overly strict ΓÇö it should not require `headBranchName` when `baseBranch` is already supplied by the caller, or when the worktree was successfully created regardless.

### Suggested fix

```typescript
if (worktreePath && activeRepository.headCommitHash && (activeRepository.headBranchName || baseBranch)) {
```

Or more robustly, separate the guard from the fallback so that a missing `headBranchName` only affects `baseBranchName` computation rather than discarding the entire result.
input.title: Copilot agent session worktree creation silently fails when repository is in detached HEAD state

input.body:
## Describe the bug

In the Agents window, a single session can contain multiple peer chats (tabs). Changing the **agent** or **model** in one chat also changes it in the other chats of the same session. These decisions should be scoped per chat.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the Agents window and start an agent-host session that supports multiple chats.
2. Add a second chat to the session (so the session has 2+ chat tabs).
3. In chat A, change the model (or the agent).
4. Switch to chat B.

**Expected:** Chat B keeps its own model/agent selection, independent of chat A.
**Actual:** The model/agent picker reflects (and is driven by) a single shared selection, so changing it in one chat appears to change it in the others.

## Root cause

The provider's write path (`setModel`/`setAgent`) already targets the active chat's resource, and each `IChat` carries its own `modelId`/`mode` observables. However, `VisibleSession` (the `IActiveSession` implementation that the Agents window model/agent pickers bind to) exposed the **session/default chat's** `modelId`/`mode` rather than the active chat's. So the read path ignored which chat tab was active, making the selection appear shared.

## Area

- Agents window (`src/vs/sessions`)
input.title: Agents window: model/agent selection is shared across chats in a session instead of per chat

$ cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="24.04"
VERSION="24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat)"
VERSION_CODENAME=noble
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code from WSL (Ubuntu 24.04).
2. Copy and paste a file inside File Explorer

Error Message:

```
The file(s) to paste have been deleted or moved since you copied them. Unable to move/copy 'c:\Users\foobar\AppData\Roaming\Code\remote-clipboard\293be1dc-df26-4767-824e-6dcd322c2534\4f3c7168-7417-4a82-9db7-e3b49c3f0763\baz' because target 'vscode-remote://wsl+ubuntu-24.04/home/foobar/baz' already exists at destination.
```

## Expected Behavior

The file should be pasted successfully without any errors, even if the target file already exists, in which case it creates a copy with a different name (e.g., `baz copy`) like previously.
input.title: [Bug] Copy/Cut and Paste File Operations Fail with Errors

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ≡ƒ¬ô If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ≡ƒôú Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.127
- OS Version: windows

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Enable claude agent host related settings (specifically around `/code-review`, agent host needed because of [related](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/322818#issuecomment-4793882732), although appears to be with `/review` etc. as well
2. open copilot chat window, select Claude Agent SDK
3. (had Sonnet 4.6 high effort selected, although I don't think this matters)
4. try to execute `/code-review` slash command (Enter key, manually click send button), nothing happens and is blocked
5. workaround is to run "review `/code-review`", although should not need workaround  (additional workaround also seems to be can send to new chat with Ctrl+Shift+Enter)
input.title: slash command blocks input - Copilot Chat with Agent Host, Claude Agent SDK

input.body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.127 - OS Version: windows Steps to Reproduce: 1. launch Agents host session window (https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agents/agents-window#_open-the-agents-window) 2. switch to claude agent sdk in local agent host dropdown 3. type `/code-review` (popup-will be correct built-in claude sdk command), 5. enter but then switches to "wrong" agents built-in skill instead (https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_126#_agentic-code-feedback-with-agent-host-harnesses), verified when running review it does not use the full claude code-review flow version
input.title: Agents, /code-review command conflicts with Claude Agent SDK

input.body:
Type: Bug In the context of editing or viewing a jupyter notebook file (ipynb), I cannot insert a new line in the local file search box (Ctrl+F), the classic shortcut `ctrl` + `enter` won't work ([shortcut described here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/30351529/find-and-replace-with-a-newline-in-visual-studio-code/30775857#30775857)). This neither work in the "Find" field nor in the "Replace" field. This seems specifically related to the local file search, as it does work in the global "Search" pane (Ctrl+Shift+F). Also, non jupyter files are note affected. Tested with the insider version as well:

input.body: ### Related issue Related to #316581, but this report is specifically about **Vision capability being lost/ignored** for GitHub-managed custom models. ### What happened? I have **three different setups** for effectively the same Qwen 3.7 Plus vision-capable model family: 1. **GitHub-managed custom model -> LiteLLM proxy** 2. **GitHub-managed custom model -> Alibaba Cloud directly** 3. **Direct custom endpoint in VS Code** The issue reproduces with the first **two** setups, and the third setup works. In practice: - the **GitHub-managed LiteLLM-backed model** is exposed as **Tools** only - the **GitHub-managed Alibaba-direct model** is also exposed as **Tools** only - but the **same model configured directly as a custom endpoint in VS Code** shows **Tools + Vision** and works with image input inside of vscode So this does **not** appear to be specific to LiteLLM. ### Repro steps 1. Register a custom model in GitHub custom models preview against either: - an OpenAI-compatible LiteLLM endpoint, or - Alibaba Cloud directly. 2. Enable **Vision** in the model configuration. 3. Use the model from a GitHub-managed Copilot surface with an image attachment. 4. Observe that the image is rejected with messages like: - `The current model couldn't process this image's format.` - `Removed image attachment(s) from conversation history because the model rejected them (vision unavailable for this model or organization).` ### Expected behavior If the model is configured with **Vision** and the backing endpoint supports image input, Copilot clients should expose and honor the Vision capability consistently. ### Actual behavior The GitHub-managed model path appears to drop or ignore the Vision capability: - GitHub custom model configuration shows **Vision enabled** - VS Code model catalog shows the GitHub-managed models as **Tools** only - the same model added directly in VS Code as a custom endpoint shows **Tools + Vision** - image requests fail before they appear to reach the provider/backend ### Why this seems like a GitHub/Copilot integration issue This does not look like a backend/provider issue: - direct requests to the LiteLLM endpoint succeed with image input - the same model configured directly in VS Code as a custom endpoint also succeeds with image input - the issue reproduces for **both**: - GitHub-managed model backed by LiteLLM - GitHub-managed model backed directly by Alibaba Cloud So this looks like a capability propagation/runtime bug in the GitHub custom model integration rather than a LiteLLM-specific or provider-specific issue. ### Screenshots #### 1. GitHub custom model configuration shows Vision enabled Description: - Model examples include: - `qwen3.7-plus (alibaba_cloud)` - `qwen-3.7-plus (litellm)` - Endpoint type: `Chat completions API` - Capabilities enabled in the GitHub UI: - `Tool calling` - `Streaming` - `Vision` - `Thinking` #### 2. Copilot CLI / chat rejection after image attachment Description: - After attaching an image, Copilot reports: - `The current model couldn't process this image's format.` - `Removed image attachment(s) from conversation history because the model rejected them (vision unavailable for this model or organization).` #### 3. VS Code capability mismatch between GitHub-managed models and direct endpoint Description: - The GitHub-managed custom model entries appear as: - `qwen-3.7-plus-litellm` -> **Tools** only - `qwen3.7-plus` -> **Tools** only - A direct custom endpoint entry for the same backing model appears with **Tools + Vision**. - Same model family and capability, but different Copilot capability exposure depending on whether the model is routed through the GitHub-managed custom model path or added directly as a custom endpoint.
input.title: GitHub-managed custom model with Vision enabled is exposed as Tools-only and rejects image attachments

input.title: Unable to use GitHub Copilot Chat in Visual Studio Code

input.body:
## Bug Description

The Copilot CLI/SDK session loader (`@github/copilot` SDK, used by the Copilot Chat extension's Agents window) rejects an entire session file when an event's `data.tokensRemoved` value is **negative**, with:

```
Session file is corrupted (line <N>: data.tokensRemoved: Number must be greater than or equal to 0)
```

The schema enforces `tokensRemoved >= 0`, but the SDK itself can write **negative** `tokensRemoved` deltas into `events.jsonl` during context compaction/token accounting. The session is then unloadable ΓÇö the whole conversation history is lost over a single accounting field.

This is the same root-cause family as #312793 (`data.compactionTokensUsed.input: Required`) and #319244 (`batchSize: Number must be greater than 0`): strict Zod validation on **token-accounting metadata** rejects the entire session rather than the conversation turns. #315692 is the user-visible "session file is corrupted" symptom.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run a long Copilot CLI / Agents session that goes through one or more context compactions (large sessions, tens of thousands of events).
2. The SDK writes one or more events with a negative `data.tokensRemoved` value into the session `events.jsonl`.
3. Open/resume that session in the VS Code Agents window (or via the Copilot Chat extension).
4. Loading fails: `Session file is corrupted (line N: data.tokensRemoved: Number must be greater than or equal to 0)`.

## Actual Behavior

- The session cannot be opened.
- Because of the fallback path described in #319244, the user may instead silently get an empty (0-turn) session ΓÇö all history lost.

## Expected Behavior

- `tokensRemoved` should never be persisted as a negative number, OR
- The loader should floor it at `0` (clamp) rather than reject the whole session, since this is non-essential accounting metadata, not conversation content.

## Root Cause / Suggested Fix

Two layers, either fixes it:

1. **SDK serialization (preferred):** clamp `tokensRemoved` (and sibling token-accounting fields) to `>= 0` when writing events, so corrupt records are never produced. The delta going negative looks like an off-by accounting bug in compaction.
2. **SDK loader / Zod schema:** make token-accounting fields tolerant ΓÇö `.catch(0)` / `.transform(v => Math.max(0, v))` instead of a hard `min(0)` rejection ΓÇö so a metadata glitch can't make an entire multi-turn session unloadable. This also resolves the broader family in #312793 / #319244.

## Workaround (for already-corrupted files)

Floor the negative values to `0` in the session file (`~/.copilot/session-state/<id>/events.jsonl`), back up first:

```bash
f=~/.copilot/session-state/<SESSION_ID>/events.jsonl
cp "$f" "$f.bak"
perl -i -pe 's/("tokensRemoved":)-\d+/${1}0/g' "$f"
```

The session then loads normally. (In one real 60k-line session this affected 12 records.)

## Environment

- VS Code Agents window + GitHub Copilot Chat extension, Copilot CLI sessions (`@github/copilot` SDK).
- macOS (Darwin); the field/schema is platform-independent.

## Related

- #312793 ΓÇö `compactionTokensUsed.input: Required` (same family)
- #319244 ΓÇö corrupted session silently loads as empty instead of surfacing the error
- #315692 ΓÇö "session file is corrupted" symptom in Agents window
input.title: Session file corrupted: negative data.tokensRemoved rejected by SDK ("Number must be greater than or equal to 0")

input.body:
### Type
Bug / performance + cost regression (volatile per-session value inside the cache-controlled prefix).

### Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?
Yes ΓÇö Copilot Chat agent-mode prompt assembly with a BYOK Anthropic model.

### Version
- VS Code: 1.126.0 (commit 7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a)
- GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.54.0
- OS: macOS (expected on all platforms)
- Model path: BYOK Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.8) via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`

### Summary
Copilot appends a "template variables" section to the **end of the single cached system block** so the model can resolve `{{VSCODE_*}}` references:

```
The following template variables are available for this session:
- VSCODE_USER_PROMPTS_FOLDER: /Users/<user>/.../User/prompts
- VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG: /Users/<user>/.../GitHub.copilot-chat/debug-logs/<UUID>/main.jsonl
When a skill or instruction references {{VSCODE_VARIABLE_NAME}}, substitute the corresponding value above.
```

`VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG` resolves to a **per-conversation** debug-log path whose `<UUID>` changes per conversation ΓÇö and, in practice, **multiple times within a single conversation**. Because it sits **before the system block's lone `cache_control` breakpoint**, every UUID change invalidates the entire ~227K-char cached prefix, forcing a full Anthropic **cache write** (1.25x) instead of a **read** (0.1x).

### Impact
On a real BYOK Anthropic workload (~227K-char cached system prefix):
- **29.5% of turns** were full-prefix cache writes; those accounted for **~95% of all `cache_creation` tokens**.
- The UUID rotated **2-9x within single conversations** (25 distinct UUIDs across the capture window).
- Reuse factor (cache_read / cache_creation) sat at **~2.2x** vs the 20-25x a well-tuned Anthropic prefix achieves.

Representative consecutive turns (only the UUID changed; system prefix byte-identical after masking it):
```
turn N-1: cache_creation=  1,744  cache_read=209,349  uuid=d4d6ce54-...   (healthy read)
turn N  : cache_creation=112,949  cache_read=      0  uuid=10fa7f5b-...   (cold re-write)
```

### Steps to reproduce
1. Use Copilot agent mode with a BYOK Anthropic model and a sizeable cached prefix.
2. Capture consecutive raw `/v1/messages` request bodies (e.g. a transparent local forward proxy on `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`).
3. Find a turn where `cache_creation_input_tokens` spikes and `cache_read_input_tokens` is 0.
4. Diff the system block against the previous turn: the only divergence is the `debug-logs/<UUID>` segment in the `VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG` line.

### Root cause (from decompiling the shipped extension, 1.126.0)
`buildTemplateVariablesContext` appends the block to the cached system text. The resolver:
```js
["VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG",(o,a)=>{ ... let s=this.chatDebugFileLoggerService.getSessionDir(o);
  if(s) return this.promptPathRepresentationService.getFilePath(s) }]
```
`getSessionDir -> _getDebugLogsDir` computes the path **unconditionally** from `storageUri ?? globalStorageUri`; it is **not** gated by `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.fileLogging.enabled` (that setting only changes the logger's flush/buffer setup). The value is also **not** a `process.env` override despite the `VSCODE_` name (it is a resolver Map key; zero `process.env['VSCODE_*']` reads in the bundle). So no setting or env var removes it.

### Why this is questionable beyond cost
- **Eager, not lazy:** declared on every request even when no loaded skill references `{{VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG}}`.
- **Info leak:** a per-machine absolute filesystem path is sent to the model provider for no functional benefit when unused.
- **Volatile-in-cached-region:** the actual defect ΓÇö a value that changes per conversation placed inside the static cached prefix.

### Requested fix (any one of)
1. **Only inject a template variable when a loaded skill/instruction actually references it** (lazy resolution), or
2. **Place the template-variables block AFTER the `cache_control` breakpoint** (or in a separate uncached block), or
3. **Keep `{{VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG}}` as a stable logical token and resolve it client-side** after the response rather than baking the resolved per-session path into the request, or
4. Make the session-log path **stable for the workspace** rather than per-conversation when it must be sent.

Option 2 fixes the cost issue with no loss of capability.

### Related
Same byte-stability principle as microsoft/vscode#313920 (cache_control sentinel must not change the byte-exact prefix across turns), different trigger. This is one of three independent BYOK Anthropic cache-busters we found (workspace-tree injection and non-additive breakpoint re-anchoring are the other two).
input.title: Per-session VSCODE_TARGET_SESSION_LOG template variable sits inside the cached system prefix and cold-busts BYOK Anthropic prompt caching

Type: Bug During an active agent-mode session reviewing a large PHP file (~13,000 lines), Copilot Chat's output stream began repeating a single unrelated word ("court") dozens of times in a row, interleaved with brief narrative text claiming the repetition was a benign "output glitch" and instructing itself to "ignore it and continue." The agent did continue issuing tool calls (file reads) between repetition bursts, but the chat transcript became unreadable and unreliable as a record of what the agent was actually doing. This closely matches prior reports of agent-mode repetition loops but in this case the model also generated self-referential commentary dismissing the artifact rather than stalling outright, making it harder to tell whether underlying reasoning was still intact. input.body: Updating a context key that affects TreeView item context menu visibility causes TreeView selection corruption. A minimal reproduction extension is attached. In this reproduction, a context key is updated when `selection.length === 5`. The update is intentionally delayed to separate it from the original selection change event and demonstrate that the issue is triggered by the context update itself rather than by selection processing. When the context key changes and the context menu contribution visibility changes by `when` expression: - The visual selection becomes corrupted (only one item stays selected). - `onDidChangeSelection` is not fired for this change. - Internally, `TreeView.selection` still reports the correct items. - Subsequent selection changes are no longer reliably reflected. - The original selection is eventually lost. [treeview-selection-contextchange-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29488333/treeview-selection-contextchange-repro.zip)
input.title: bug: TreeView selection becomes corrupted when a context key update changes item context menu visibility

input.body:
∩╗┐## Feature Request: Three enhancements for BYOK (`chatLanguageModels.json`) configuration experience

### Suggestion 1: Auto-detect and auto-encrypt plaintext `apiKey` on file save / Provide a command to bulk-set encrypted `apiKey`

**Problem:**

When users manually edit `chatLanguageModels.json` to configure custom providers, they may write the `apiKey` in plaintext (e.g., when migrating from third-party tools or batch-importing many providers). Currently, VS Code's BYOK system only encrypts `apiKey` via `SecretStorageService` when provider groups are created through the **UI wizard or programmatic API** ΓÇö the `_resolveLanguageModelProviderGroup` method handles this, but it is never triggered on file save.

For users managing many custom endpoints, manually clicking through the UI for each API key is impractical.

**Desired improvement ΓÇö auto-encrypt on file save (plaintext in file ΓåÆ auto encrypted):**

```json
[
    {
        "name": "deepseek",
        "vendor": "customendpoint",
        // User writes plaintext apiKey manually in JSON file
        "apiKey": "sk-01234...abcdef",
        "apiType": "messages"
    }
]
```

After saving, the system should automatically:
1. Detect plaintext `apiKey` values in provider group configurations.
2. Migrate them to `SecretStorageService` (as it already does in `_resolveLanguageModelProviderGroup`).
3. Rewrite the file with encrypted placeholder references (e.g., `"${input:chat.lm.secret.<hash>}"`).

Additionally, exposing a **command** (e.g., `workbench.action.providers.setApiKey`) accessible via `Ctrl+P` would allow external tooling and batch scripts to programmatically set encrypted API keys.

**Relevant code:**
- `_resolveLanguageModelProviderGroup` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts`
- `LanguageModelsConfigurationService` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/languageModelsConfigurationService.ts` ΓÇö file watcher exists but no auto-encryption.
- `updateLanguageModelsProviderGroupApiKey` ΓÇö existing UI-only command.

---

### Suggestion 2: Add `apiUrl` and `defaults` at the provider group level to avoid per-model duplication

**Problem:**

When multiple models share the same base endpoint and capabilities under one provider group, each model must still repeat `url`, `maxInputTokens`, `maxOutputTokens`, `toolCalling`, `vision`, etc. This creates excessive boilerplate:

```json
[
    {
        "name": "deepseek",
        "vendor": "customendpoint",
        "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.-f1cf4fa}",
        "apiType": "messages",
        "models": [
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-flash",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-flash",
                "url": "https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
                "toolCalling": true,
                "vision": false,
                "maxInputTokens": 128000,
                "maxOutputTokens": 128000
            },
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                "url": "https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
                "toolCalling": true,
                "vision": false,
                "maxInputTokens": 128000,
                "maxOutputTokens": 128000
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

**Desired improvement ΓÇö inherited defaults at group level:**

```json
[
    {
        "name": "deepseek",
        "vendor": "customendpoint",
        "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.-f1cf4fa}",
        "apiType": "messages",
        // Base URL for all models in this group
        "apiUrl": "https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
        // Default capability params ΓÇö models inherit unless overridden
        "defaults": {
            "toolCalling": true,
            "vision": false,
            "maxInputTokens": 128000,
            "maxOutputTokens": 128000
        },
        "models": [
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-flash",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-flash"
            },
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                // Per-model overrides when defaults don't apply
                "maxInputTokens": 256000,
                "maxOutputTokens": 256000
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

The resolution order should be: **per-model field > `defaults` at group level > provider-level fallback**. This would dramatically reduce boilerplate and make bulk editing much easier, especially for self-hosted endpoints with many models.

**Relevant code:**
- `CustomEndpointModelProviderConfig` / `_CustomOAIModelConfig` in `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/customEndpointProvider.ts` and `customOAIProvider.ts` ΓÇö per-model `url` is currently required for each entry.
- `getAllModels` in those same files ΓÇö builds per-model info from `configuration.models`.

---

### Suggestion 3: Add `apiListModels` endpoint to auto-discover and sync available models

**Problem:**

Currently, when a user configures a new provider (especially a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint), they must manually enumerate every model ID in the `models` array. For endpoints that support model listing (e.g., `GET /v1/models`), this is redundant. The user has to keep the list in sync manually whenever the upstream adds or removes models.

**Desired improvement ΓÇö auto-fetch from model list endpoint:**

```json
[
    {
        "name": "deepseek",
        "vendor": "customendpoint",
        "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.-f1cf4fa}",
        "apiType": "messages",
        "apiUrl": "https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
        // Optional: auto-fetch available models from this discovery endpoint
        "apiListModels": "/v1/models",
        "defaults": {
            "toolCalling": true,
            "vision": false,
            "maxInputTokens": 128000,
            "maxOutputTokens": 128000
        },
        "models": [
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-flash",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-flash",
                // Not listed as hidden ΓåÆ visible in model picker
                "hidden": false
            },
            {
                "id": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                "name": "deepseek-v4-pro",
                "hidden": false
            },
            {
                "id": "deepseek-reasoner",
                "name": "DeepSeek Reasoner",
                // Hide this model from the picker by default
                "hidden": true
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

When `apiListModels` is specified, the system would:
1. Fetch available models from the endpoint (similar to how `AbstractOpenAICompatibleLMProvider.getModelsFromEndpoint` works).
2. Merge the server-side model list with the user's local `models` array.
3. Use the user-configured `hidden` property (or a new `show`/`hide` toggle in the model picker UI) to control visibility, rather than requiring the user to maintain a complete list.

This approach ΓÇö **maintain model list automatically, let the user just toggle show/hide** ΓÇö would be far more efficient and user-friendly for self-hosted providers that frequently add or update models.

**Relevant code:**
- `AbstractOpenAICompatibleLMProvider.getModelsFromEndpoint` in `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/abstractLanguageModelChatProvider.ts` ΓÇö already fetches models from a discovery URL; could be extended to support user-provided `apiListModels`.
- `LanguageModelsService.setModelHidden` / `setGroupHidden` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts` ΓÇö existing show/hide infrastructure for models.
- `getAllModels` in `customEndpointProvider.ts` ΓÇö when `configuration.url` is set, falls back to `super.getAllModels()` which fetches from endpoint; this pattern could be extended.
input.title: Feature Request: Auto-encrypt apiKey on save, group-level defaults, and auto-discover models for BYOK (chatLanguageModels.json)

Type: Bug I'm currently running a virtualization lab on RHEL 9.8, using VSCode and Cascadia Code for the font on linux, certain zoom modes force the font to look super skinny and warped. I could provide screenshots, but this issue reporter does not appear to have that in here anywhere. So, steps to reproduce: 1) install VSCode on linux (would hope u guys know how) 2) install Cascadia code fonts sudo curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft/cascadia-code/releases/latest | grep browser_download_url | grep zip | cut -d '"' -f 4 | xargs sudo curl -L -o /tmp/cascadia.zip sudo unzip /tmp/cascadia.zip -d /usr/share/fonts/cascadia sudo fc-cache -f -v fc-list | grep -i cascadia 3) update settings.json to target Cascadia Code "editor.fontFamily": "Cascadia Code" 4) use CTRL + and CTRL - to cycle through zoom settings. I asked copilot what it thinks and it gave me a bunch of useless suggestions that either didn't work, or were trying to resolve it elsewhere using DPI type commands. It's not an XRDP issue, nor an issue with the font itself, the renderer for the editor is squeezing the font by like 25-33%? I can't even tell what zoom setting it's on, but the font issue occurs over Hyper-V window, OR using RDP window. I like this font, I always use it, I always wind up installing a bunch of Microsoft stuff on linux, edge, vscode, extensions... would be nice to have a solution for this particular issue. input.body:
Several terminal agent sandbox settings are restricted preview settings but do not all have enterprise policy registrations. This makes it harder for administrators to centrally disable or lock down sandbox-related behavior, especially when chat preview features are disabled.

This issue tracks adding policy metadata/default restricted values for the terminal agent sandbox settings, including:

- Windows sandbox enablement
- Network and unsandboxed-command allowances
- Retry-with-network behavior
- Auto-approval behavior
- Linux/macOS/Windows filesystem sandbox settings
- Advanced sandbox runtime settings

The policy values should default to disabling or emptying these settings when `chat_preview_features_enabled` is false.
input.title: Add enterprise policies for terminal agent sandbox settings

input.body:
### Type
Bug ΓÇö cost regression (prompt-cache write churn from non-additive breakpoint movement) on the BYOK Anthropic path.

### Environment
- VS Code: 1.126.0 (commit 7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a)
- GitHub Copilot Chat: <your Extensions ΓåÆ GitHub Copilot Chat version>
- OS: macOS (expected on all platforms)
- Model: BYOK Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.8) via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`
- Occurs with all other extensions disabled: yes

### Summary
Anthropic prompt caching is explicit and capped at **4 `cache_control` breakpoints** per request.
As an agent conversation grows, the Copilot prompt renderer re-distributes those 4 breakpoints ΓÇö
generally moving the last one toward the end so the bulk of accumulated history stays cached. That
intent is correct. The **implementation is not byte-stable**: when the breakpoint re-anchors, the
prefix bytes *before* the new anchor change, so Anthropic treats it as a **cache write of the entire
prefix** (`cache_creation_input_tokens` spikes, `cache_read_input_tokens` drops to 0) instead of an
**incremental cache extension** of the already-cached region.

The specific trigger observed is a **synthetic whitespace anchor block** appearing/disappearing. The
renderer injects `{"type":"text","text":" ","cache_control":{"type":"ephemeral"}}` when the last
content block isn't a valid cache anchor, and removes/relocates it on a later turn. That single
structural change to the cached region forces a full re-write.

### Impact
On a real BYOK Anthropic workload (Claude Opus 4.8), over a clean-slate window of 155 consecutive
captured requests:
- ~8.4% of turns were full-prefix cache **writes** (`cache_creation` > `cache_read`), distinct from
  a separate workspace-tree bust (filed as the companion issue).
- These re-write turns appeared on **long conversations** (request bodies ~1.8 MB) and cost a full
  `cache_creation` of the entire prefix (observed up to ~560K tokens at 1.25├ù input rate) where an
  incremental extension would have cost almost nothing.
- Reuse factor (cache_read ├╖ cache_creation) on the affected window sat at ~7.9├ù versus the 20ΓÇô25├ù a
  well-tuned Anthropic prefix achieves.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Use agent mode with a BYOK Anthropic model and a long-running conversation (many turns, large
   accumulated history).
2. Capture consecutive raw `/v1/messages` request bodies (e.g. via a transparent local forward proxy
   on `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`).
3. Find a turn where `cache_creation_input_tokens` spikes and `cache_read_input_tokens` is 0.
4. Diff that body against the previous turn. The first divergence is **inside the message history**
   (not the system prompt), at a `cache_control` marker ΓÇö typically a
   `{"type":"text","text":" ","cache_control":...}` block present in one turn and absent/moved in the
   next.

### Root cause (from the shipped extension, 1.126.0)
The Anthropic wire-format renderer places `cache_control` on the last cacheable block and walks
**from the end backward** to position the (capped) breakpoints:

// place the breakpoint on the last non-deferred block
for (let a = n.length - 1; a >= 0; a--) if (!n[a].defer_loading) { n[a].cache_control = r; break }
// ΓÇªand a synthetic whitespace anchor when the tail isn't cacheable:
r && c1n(r) ? r.cache_control = {type:"ephemeral"}
: e.push({ type:"text", text:" ", cache_control:{type:"ephemeral"} })


Because the 4-breakpoint cap forces re-distribution as history grows, and the synthetic `" "` anchor
is added/removed depending on the tail block type, the **bytes of the cached prefix change between
turns**. Anthropic caches the longest *identical* prefix, so any change before the anchor
invalidates everything after it ΓåÆ a full re-write rather than an additive extension.

### Why "just pin the breakpoint early" is not the fix
Anthropic only caches *up to* the last breakpoint; everything after it is billed at full input rate
every turn. Pinning the breakpoint early would keep the small static prefix cached but leave the
entire **growing** history uncached and re-billed at 1├ù every turn ΓÇö worse than current behavior in
long sessions. The correct behavior is to move the breakpoint **forward additively**, preserving the
already-cached prefix bytes, so re-anchoring extends the cache instead of rewriting it.

### Requested fix
1. Make breakpoint re-anchoring **byte-stable / additive**: when moving the trailing breakpoint
   forward, do not alter any bytes before the previously-cached anchor, so Anthropic extends the
   existing cache rather than re-creating it.
2. Specifically, stop inserting/removing the synthetic `{"type":"text","text":" "}` anchor in a way
   that mutates the cached prefix across turns; use a stable anchor position.
3. Consider pinning one of the 4 breakpoints permanently at the end of the **static** system/tools
   prefix (that region never changes) and using the remaining breakpoints for the growing tail ΓÇö so
   the large static prefix is never re-written even when the tail re-anchors.

### Workaround
Keep sessions short (start a fresh chat per task). A shorter conversation has a smaller tail and
fewer message blocks competing for the 4 breakpoints, so re-anchoring happens less often and
invalidates less when it does.

### Related
- **#313920** ("BYOK chat providers receive an internal cache_control LanguageModelDataPart sentinel
  that's not in the public API", Open, `model-byok`) documents the **same `cache_control` /
  `"ephemeral"` sentinel** from a different trigger (the sentinel leaking into serialized tool-result
  text). It states the same root principle this issue relies on:
  > "A leaked `{"$mid":24,...}` substring on one turn changes the byte-exact prefix and misses every
  > subsequent turn's cache ΓÇö defeating the whole point of the sentinel."
  Same sentinel, same byte-stability failure class, two distinct triggers. Relevant first-party
  converter: `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/common/anthropicMessageConverter.ts`.
- Companion issue (workspace-structure tree injected ahead of the cached prefix) ΓÇö the other
  independent cache-busting cause on the BYOK Anthropic path: <paste ticket 1 URL>.
input.title: Cache breakpoint re-anchoring rewrites (instead of extends) the BYOK Anthropic prompt cache in long agent sessions

input.body:
### Type
Bug ΓÇö performance/cost regression on the BYOK Anthropic path.

### Environment
- VS Code: 1.126.0 (commit 7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a)
- GitHub Copilot Chat: <your Extensions ΓåÆ GitHub Copilot Chat version>
- OS: macOS (expected on all platforms)
- Model: BYOK Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.8) via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`
- Occurs with all other extensions disabled: yes

### Summary
In agent mode the prompt includes an injected workspace **directory structure tree**
("I am working in a workspace that has the following structure:") placed **near the head of the
request**, ahead of the large otherwise-static cached prefix (instructions / `AGENTS.md` / tool
definitions).

That tree is regenerated each turn and reflects the live workspace at **depth Γëñ 2**. When any
directory at depth Γëñ 2 of a workspace/repo root is **created or removed mid-session** (very common ΓÇö
per-ticket scratch dirs, screenshot folders, test output, downloads), the tree text changes.
Because it sits **before** the cached prefix, the byte change invalidates the entire downstream
prefix, forcing a full Anthropic **cache write** (1.25├ù input rate) instead of a **cache read**
(0.1├ù).

There is no setting to disable or stabilize this injection.

### Impact
On a real BYOK Anthropic workload with a ~270K-token cached prefix:
- Measured reuse factor fell to **~4├ù** (well-tuned Anthropic caching is 20ΓÇô25├ù).
- **~22% of turns** were full-prefix re-writes, and **94% of those re-writes happened within 5
  minutes of the prior turn** (i.e. not TTL expiry ΓÇö a structural bust).
- Captured the exact moving byte: a single `artifacts/p12-NNNN/` directory appearing/disappearing
  at **byte ~800** of a ~669KB request body, cold-rewriting the ~270K-token prefix after it.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Use agent mode with a BYOK Anthropic model and a sizeable cached prefix (instructions +
   `AGENTS.md` + tools).
2. Capture two consecutive raw `/v1/messages` request bodies (e.g. via a transparent local forward
   proxy on `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`).
3. Between the two turns, create or remove a directory at depth Γëñ 2 of a workspace root
   (e.g. `mkdir artifacts/some-dir`).
4. Diff the two bodies: the first divergence is inside the "following structure:" tree near the
   head; `cache_creation_input_tokens` spikes and `cache_read_input_tokens` drops to 0 on the
   second turn.

### Root cause (from the shipped extension, 1.126.0)
The tree is emitted by an unconditional `PromptElement` (minified class `LP`):
input.title: Agent-mode workspace directory tree is injected ahead of the cached prefix and breaks BYOK Anthropic prompt caching

input.body:
Summary

Add Multi-Chat support to the Codex harness so it matches the Copilot experience.

Background

- The Multi-Chat feature (previously called Sub-Sessions) is currently available for the Copilot harness and works in AHP sessions for Copilot.
- Team feedback and user studies have shown confusion around the feature and its name; the UX should be consistent across harnesses rather than Copilot-only.
- Codex AHP sessions do not currently show the Multi-Chat UI.

Proposal

- Implement Multi-Chat in the Codex harness with parity to the Copilot implementation and UX.
- Ensure Multi-Chat is available in AHP sessions for Codex as it is for Copilot.
- Minimize agent integration effort (sessions should be transparent to agents where possible), and document any agent-facing changes required.
- Update tests, e2e scenarios, and relevant documentation to reflect Multi-Chat availability in the Codex harness.

Acceptance criteria

- Multi-Chat UI appears and behaves the same in the Codex harness as it does in Copilot.
- Multi-Chat is available and functional in AHP sessions for Codex.
- Agent/handler code for Codex either requires no changes or any required changes are documented and minimal.
- Relevant tests and documentation updated.

Implementation notes

- Verify whether sessions need to be linked or if the agent surface can remain unchanged (transparent sessions).
- Coordinate UX changes with the Copilot harness team to ensure consistency and avoid repeating prior confusion around naming/behavior.
- Add testing steps to validate in manual and automated tests across the Codex harness and AHP sessions.

---

Split from #322776 (Claude). This issue tracks the Codex harness.

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09MVCZKLP4/p1782230045706259?thread_ts=1782230045.706259&cid=C09MVCZKLP4)
input.title: Support Multi-Chat in Codex harness

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Problem
When using comprehensive keyboard layout extensions or modal editing wrappers like [Master Key](https://haberdashpi.github.io/vscode-master-key/), the `keybindings.json` file size can drastically increase due to the sheer volume of generated shortcuts for various modes and contexts. 

Currently, VS Code strictly enforces a 100kb limit on the keyboard shortcuts file for Settings Sync, throwing the following error and disabling keyboard settings synchronization:
> Disabled syncing keyboard shortcuts because size of the keyboard shortcuts file to sync is larger than 100kb. Please open the file and reduce the size and enable sync

This hard limit penalizes power users who rely on heavy keybinding configurations or ecosystem-altering extensions.

### Describe the solution you'd like
I would like VS Code to increase the maximum allowed file size for `keybindings.json` in Settings Sync from 100kb to 150kb (or higher). This slight overhead would easily accommodate complex, extension-generated keybinding maps without risking server performance.

### Describe alternatives you've considered
* **Manually stripping keybindings:** Not viable as it breaks the functionality provided by key-mapping extensions.
* **Disabling keybindings sync:** A frustrating workaround since keyboard shortcuts are a core part of an engineer's workflow synchronization across multiple machines.

### Additional context
#86178
input.title: Increase Settings Sync limit for `keybindings.json` to 150kb

input.body:
## Type
Feature request / behavior gap

## Environment
- VS Code: 1.126
- Copilot Chat: latest
- Permission level: Autopilot (`chat.permissions.default: "autopilot"`, `chat.autopilot.enabled: true`)
- Also set: `chat.tools.global.autoApprove: true`, terminal `/.*/` catch-all auto-approve, `chat.tools.terminal.ignoreDefaultAutoApproveRules: true`

## Summary
Even at the **most permissive** documented configuration (Autopilot), the agent **silently skips** irreversible/destructive tool calls that I *explicitly asked it to perform* (e.g. removing a file, removing a database row). The agent returns:

> Autopilot skipped this tool call because it was automatically assessed as high-risk: ... The action was not performed.

There is **no documented setting** to opt out of this secondary risk gate.

## Why this is confusing (docs vs. behavior)
The [approvals docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agents/approvals) state that Autopilot and Bypass Approvals:

> "...bypass manual approval prompts, **including for potentially destructive actions like file edits, terminal commands, and external tool calls.**"

But in practice a *separate* risk classifier re-blocks destructive actions **after** the permission level is applied. So the documented "approve everything" promise is not honored, and there is no setting (documented or surfaced) to disable this second layer.

## Repro
1. Set permission level to **Autopilot** (or Bypass Approvals).
2. Set `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` to a `/.*/` catch-all and `chat.tools.terminal.ignoreDefaultAutoApproveRules: true`.
3. Ask the agent to remove a file (e.g. "remove scripts/foo.py"), or to remove a row from a database via an MCP tool.
4. Observe: the tool call is auto-skipped as "high-risk" and never executes, despite the explicit request and the most permissive settings.

Notably, the classifier appears to key partly on surface tokens: a command literally containing the removal keyword gets blocked, while a functionally-identical script with a neutral name slips through, so the gate is inconsistent rather than a reliable safety guarantee.

## Expected
One of:
- Autopilot/Bypass honor an **explicit** user request to perform a destructive action (matching the documented behavior), **or**
- Expose a setting (e.g. `chat.tools.allowHighRiskActions` or per-tool `eligibleForAutoApproval`) to opt out of the high-risk gate, **or**
- At minimum, fall back to a **single confirmation prompt** instead of silently skipping with no way to proceed.

## Related
- #313210: "Copilot easily breaking out of sandbox mode" (the inverse concern; shows the team is actively tuning this line)
- #303174: "autopilot mode should allow curl in terminal" (same theme: autopilot more restrictive than expected)
input.title: Autopilot/Bypass silently skips user-requested high-risk actions with no opt-out

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

IΓÇÖd like to propose a feature for the integrated browser that lets a repo or page provide custom context when a user selects an element and chooses ΓÇ£Add Element to ChatΓÇ¥.

In short, it would enable [react-grab](https://www.react-grab.com/) like functionality through a hook with the code defined in repo. There are a lot of "grab" libraries designed for different web frameworks: [react](https://www.react-grab.com), [angular](https://angular-grab.com/), [svelte](https://meshkore.com/agent/heicg-svelte-grab), [solidjs](https://github.com/omniaura/solid-grab), [vue](https://vue-grab.vercel.app/), etc... This feature would enable these tools to work inside the integrated browser directly. Each framework would have its own npm package to enable the hook on the browser side.

Today, the flow in `browserEditorChatFeatures.ts` builds a standardized snippet from the selected DOM element (HTML, path, dimensions, computed styles, etc.) and sends that into chat. That is useful, but it is not flexible enough for repos with specialized needs.

As a frontend developer, I want to be able to customize the text snippet that gets attached to chat so I can provide richer, repo-specific context to agents. This should make agent-driven edits more accurate and reduce the need for manual follow-up.

Example use case:
- In a React repo, I want the browser selection flow to call custom code that inspects the React virtual DOM / component tree and returns a structured component-context summary.
- I also want to customize CSS output, for example to de-transform CSS module class names before they are attached to chat, or to remove hashed griffel class names.

Proposal:
- When an element is selected in the integrated browser, VS Code should attempt to invoke a page-provided hook that can customize the context payload.
- The hook should be available for both:
  - the manual ΓÇ£Add Element to ChatΓÇ¥ flow from the integrated browser UI, and
  - the browser-tool / agent-driven selection path, so the same repo-specific context can be provided regardless of how the selection was triggered.
- The hook could be exposed as an optional global function on the page, for example:
  - `window.__vscodeIntegratedBrowserGetContext?.(elementData)`
- The hook should be able to return either:
  - a string snippet to append to the existing context, or
  - a richer structured payload that VS Code can render into the chat attachment.
- If the hook is not provided, the current built-in behavior remains unchanged.
- For discoverability, we could add a small docs/help entry or info popover near the element-selection button in the integrated browser UI.

Why this is valuable:
- Different repos need different context
- Frontend teams often want framework-specific summaries
- Better context should lead to better agent suggestions and edits

Possible follow-up work:
- Support repo-specific injection via a script already loaded in the page
- Provide a documented API shape and examples
- Add a UI hint/docs link near the selection action
- Add some default formatters for common libraries like react

---

You can contact me directly on Teams at my Microsoft alias
input.title: Feature request: React-Grab-like customizable element-selection context for the integrated browser

input.body:
### Problem

Extension webviews currently can't use `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` at all ΓÇö the webview iframe carries no `allow="camera; microphone"`, and the Electron permission handler denies `media` for `vscode-webview://` origins. That's intentional ("media must not be silently accessible", #113916), but it leaves **no opt-in path** for legitimate, user-initiated scenarios: live device/camera preview, audio/video capture, QR scanning, in-editor calls, voice/accessibility input, hardware diagnostics.

Today the only workaround is to spawn `ffmpeg` in the extension host and stream frames back into the webview ΓÇö heavy and platform-specific, just to reach hardware the OS already exposes.

### Precedent

There's now an active precedent: consent-gated, per-origin, revocable camera/mic/location permissions for the integrated browser (#299521 / PR #322639), **currently in Insiders**. This proposes extending that same safety model to extension webviews.

### Proposed shape (designed to answer the "no silent access" concern)

- Extension opts in via a proposed API (`webviewMediaDevices`) and declares devices on the webview ΓÇö `mediaDevices: ['camera', 'microphone']` ΓÇö visible at install time, off by default.
- The iframe `allow` is injected **only** for declaring webviews (per-device).
- First `getUserMedia` triggers a **one-time consent prompt**; the grant is stored **per extension** and is revocable.
- No declaration ΓåÆ no `allow`; no consent ΓåÆ no access.

### Working PoC (desktop), behind the proposed API

Verified end-to-end (declare ΓåÆ consent prompt ΓåÆ `getUserMedia` ΓåÆ live camera/mic in the webview). Deliberately minimal; happy to rework to your conventions, not asking to merge as-is:
https://github.com/chrisdp/vscode/commit/5b9064c626cbc87554c65f0639fa9062d86b72e6

The net-new piece vs the browser work is mainly the iframe `allow` injection (on both the outer webview iframe and the inner content frame, for Permissions-Policy delegation) plus the per-extension grant; the consent UI/store could be reused.

### The ask

Would the team be open to this direction? If so, we'll productionize (persisted/revocable grants + management UI, renderer-brokered consent, remote/web/WSL hosts).

### Related
- #250568 ΓÇö Permissions-policy violation report (microphone not allowed in a webview)
- #113916 ΓÇö earlier decision that webview media should be blocked without a prompt
- #299521 / PR #322639 ΓÇö consent-gated browser permissions (in Insiders), the model this builds on
input.title: Proposed API: opt-in, consent-gated camera/microphone access for extension webviews
