input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
As we have seen from multiple studies, participants frequently run into difficulties managing parallel streams of work in the Agents window. Participants uniformly preferred serial work and struggled when pushed into parallelism. The tool did not make parallel work visible enough to manage easily or to entice participants to reduce their reluctance to adopt parallel workflows.

Key friction points:

- Cannot watch multiple streams simultaneously
- Lose track of which stream made which changes
- Cannot see what each agent is doing while it runs
- No summary of what each stream accomplished

Proposal
A design exploration to make parallel work manageable enough that the reluctance has a chance to soften.

Areas to explore:

- Side-by-side activity view ΓÇö Show concurrent streams' progress simultaneously rather than requiring tab-switching
- Activity summaries ΓÇö Replace wall-of-transcript with per-stream summaries of what happened and what changed
- Automatic worktree isolation ΓÇö Create git worktrees per stream as an implementation detail, so parallel work cannot conflict at the file level
- Progress notifications ΓÇö Show stream completion/error as toast or status-bar events so users don't need to watch constantly

User evidence

- 5/5 participants preferred serial work when left to their own devices
- When pushed into parallelism, all struggled with visibility and attribution
- Fear of streams conflicting was explicitly cited as a reason to avoid parallelism
- One participant proposed branch/worktree isolation as the solution
input.title: Design the optimal experience for parallel work in the Agents window

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
During the June 2026 UX study participants universally wanted to see what the agent changed, quickly and directly. Getting to a clear, current diff was harder than it should have been. Participants frequently inspected the files view because they could see the color highlighting indicating where changes had been applied. But clicking on the files only showed the content of those files, not the diffs that had been applied. 

The desire to watch the work is strong, and the diff is part of the approval decision ΓÇö not just a post-hoc review.

Proposal

- Make the diff one click away from any chat or agent activity ΓÇö no hunting through transcript
- Provide more detail about proposed changes before approval when the agent requests permission to perform an action such as editing a sensitive file, so the user can make an informed allow/deny decision
- Ensure the diff is always current ΓÇö if the agent makes additional changes, the view should update without requiring a manual refresh

User evidence

- 5/5 participants wanted to see what the agent changed
- Multiple participants hit bugs where changes did not display correctly
- Another wanted to see the diff before allowing an edit, not after
- The desire was universal across experience levels

Success criteria
[ ] Diff view reliably shows all new and modified files (no missing files)
[ ] Diff is reachable in one click from the active chat
[ ] Pending approval requests show a preview of the proposed change
[ ] Diff view updates in real-time as the agent makes additional changes
input.title: Make the changes that an agent has made immediately obvious

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In the June 2026 UX study, participants found it difficult to keep track of what was happening across multiple sessions and within any chat that launched two or more sub-agents/sub-chats. When multiple agents share a transcript or run concurrently, their activity appears as an undifferentiated wall of text. Participants could not quickly determine which agent did what, or whether one stalled. When agents broke silently mid-task the failure was only discoverable by parsing the log manually.

Proposal
Give each active agent a distinct visual identity so that concurrent work is legible and attributable at a glance.

Design directions to explore:

- Per-session and/or per-agent colour coding or iconography in the chat transcript
- Visual separation (cards, borders, swimlanes) between sessions, agents and agent outputs
- Per-session and per agent status indicators showing: running, waiting, completed, errored
- Notifications when an agent stalls or errors, rather than requiring manual log inspection

User evidence

- 2/5 participants had agents break silently mid-task with no visible indication
- Participants struggled to parse interleaved output from multiple agents
- One participant explicitly requested colour-coded separation by agent role
- Another wanted to "click in and see what they're doing" for each agent
input.title: Give parallel sessions and agents more prominent visual identity

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
From the recent June 2026 UX study, when multiple chats run within a session, participants expected to see what this chat changed, rather than what changed across the full workspace. This was the most consistently cited expectation (5/5 participants). However, the strength of this signal may partly reflect the study tasks, which deliberately pushed participants into parallel work. In practice, many developers work one stream at a time, and the pain of an unscoped changes view surfaces mainly when parallelism is in play.

"If I have five chats I don't know which chat made what changesΓÇª I would have to review 300 lines, and I don't know which chat did what." ΓÇö Research participant

"I also can understand the technical limitations, so I could live with a diff just showing the whole [tree]ΓÇª but maybe if the session view has a button to display the changes, I would expect it to be at the session level." ΓÇö Research participant

Participants didn't necessarily ask for the changes view to change to show only changes per chat/session (many equated chat and session), they requested a way to see the changes that had been made by the agent in a chat/session.

It would be worth exploring ways in which we can show and summarise changes made by the agent in the visible chat or session and exploring whether changes made by other means, such as an agent working inside VS Code, or the developer making manual edits need to be summarised in the same way.

For example:

- could the existing Changes view panel be filtered/toggled by stream?
- Inline in the chat conversation at the end of each agent turn (summary of what just changed)?
- Both ΓÇö a lightweight inline summary plus a detailed view on demand?

Other things to consider during the exploration are that in this study, participants were pushed into parallelism. We know from other studies that this not the default behaviour. Would developers who work serially (the observed default) encounter this pain naturally?

If not, should this be optimised for the parallel case specifically, rather than changing the default single-stream experience?
What's the right fidelity for an inline summary?

Full diff inline?
File-list with change counts (e.g., "Modified: api/tags.ts (+12 ΓêÆ3), Created: tests/tags.test.ts")?
Expandable summary that links to the full diff?

Design directions worth prototyping

- Inline turn summary: At the end of each agent turn, show a collapsible "Changes made" card listing files modified/created/deleted, with a "View diff" link to the detailed view
- Scoped Changes view toggle: Add a filter to the existing Changes panel to scope by chat/stream
- Per-chat changes tab: Each chat gets its own changes section (separate from the workspace-wide panel)

User evidence

- 5/5 participants expected changes to be attributable to a chat/session
- Experienced participants understood git constraints and proposed workable seams
- One participant proposed a side-panel (whole project) + session-control (scoped view) split
- Another reached for separate branches/worktrees as file-level isolation
-
input.title: Study participants wanted to see changes that were made per chat/session

input.body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

When using BYOK models and logged into GitHub, if making too many requests to the BYOK models too quickly (which can occur when there are multiple subagents running or running chats in multiple workspaces), it can somehow trigger GitHub API rate limit. Furthermore, it causes all chats to instantly fail showing the model ID as the error message.

This does not happen when not logged into GitHub.

This is annoying and unexpected because it causes interruptions to my ongoing chats unexpectedly and had to restart the chat (either after waiting for the API rate limit to clear or logging out of GitHub in the extension), and restarting the chat might cause the model to get lost of the current progress easily. This is especially true when running multiple subagents, since the main agent can only see the subagent reporting an agent error and not seeing its work at all.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.52.2026060505
- VS Code Version: 1.124.0-insider (0035c783eccf8a2efbdb972dfe62becb51f11fc6)
- OS Version: macOS 26.4.1
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): BYOK
- Logs:

GitHub Copilot Chat output:

	at async i.$invokeAgent (file:///Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code%20-%20Insiders.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/workbench/api/node/extensionHostProcess.js:562:20882)

```

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Log into GitHub in the extension.
2. Trigger GitHub API rate limit by running many subagents at once or running many chats in many windows.
3. All ongoing chats will then fail with the model ID as the error message.
input.title: Chat: BYOK models can trigger GitHub API rate limit and cause BYOK model requests to fail

```
# ≡ƒç¿≡ƒç¡ Γ₧£ ≡ƒç½≡ƒç╖ Trip Plan: Zurich to France

A flexible itinerary and planning guide for traveling from Zurich, Switzerland to France.

---

## 1. Overview

| Detail | Info |
| --- | --- |
| **Start** | Zurich, Switzerland |
| **Destination(s)** | France (e.g. Paris, Lyon, Annecy, Strasbourg, Nice) |
| **Suggested duration** | 5ΓÇô7 days |
| **Best seasons** | Late spring (MayΓÇôJun) & early autumn (SepΓÇôOct) |
| **Currency** | Switzerland: CHF ┬╖ France: EUR (Γé¼) |
| **Languages** | German (Zurich) ┬╖ French (France) |

---

## 2. Choosing Your Destination

France offers very different experiences depending on the region. Pick based on your vibe:

- **Paris** ≡ƒù╝ ΓÇö Iconic city break: museums, caf├⌐s, architecture. (~4h by train)
- **Lyon** ≡ƒì╜∩╕Å ΓÇö Gastronomy capital, charming old town. (~4h by train)
- **Annecy** ≡ƒÅö∩╕Å ΓÇö Alpine lake town, very close to Switzerland. (~3.5h)
- **Strasbourg** ≡ƒÑ¿ ΓÇö Alsace charm, FrenchΓÇôGerman blend. (~3h)
- **French Riviera (Nice)** ≡ƒîè ΓÇö Mediterranean beaches & glamour. (~7h by train / 1.5h flight)

> Tip: For a short trip, **Annecy** or **Strasbourg** minimize travel time. For a classic experience, choose **Paris** or **Lyon**.

---

## 3. Getting There

### ≡ƒÜå By Train (recommended)
- **Zurich HB ΓåÆ Paris Gare de Lyon**: ~4h direct on **TGV Lyria** (high-speed).
- **Zurich HB ΓåÆ Lyon**: ~4h via TGV Lyria.
- **Zurich ΓåÆ Strasbourg**: ~3h (change in Basel).
- **Zurich ΓåÆ Annecy**: ~3.5ΓÇô4h (change in Geneva).
- Book early via **SBB**, **TGV Lyria**, or **Rail Europe** for the best fares.
- Scenic, city-center to city-center, no airport hassle.

### Γ£ê∩╕Å By Plane
- Direct flights from **Zurich (ZRH)** to Paris, Lyon, Nice, Marseille (~1ΓÇô1.5h).
- Best for the Riviera or far-south destinations.
- Factor in airport transfer + security time (~3h total door-to-door).

### ≡ƒÜù By Car
- Zurich ΓåÆ Paris: ~6.5h (650 km).
- Zurich ΓåÆ Annecy: ~3h (250 km).
- Flexible for the countryside; remember the **Swiss motorway vignette** and French **tolls (p├⌐ages)**.
- Carry an EU-compliant warning triangle & reflective vest.

---

## 4. Sample Itinerary (Paris, 5 Days)

### Day 1 ΓÇö Travel & Arrival
- Morning TGV Lyria from Zurich HB.
- Check in, evening stroll along the **Seine**, dinner in a local bistro.

### Day 2 ΓÇö Classic Paris
- **Eiffel Tower** & Champ de Mars.
- **Louvre** or **Mus├⌐e d'Orsay**.
- Sunset from **Trocad├⌐ro** or a Seine river cruise.

### Day 3 ΓÇö Neighborhoods
- **Montmartre** & Sacr├⌐-C┼ôur.
- **Le Marais** for shopping and caf├⌐s.
- Evening cabaret or a quiet wine bar.

### Day 4 ΓÇö Day Trip
- **Versailles** palace & gardens, or
- **Giverny** (Monet's gardens, seasonal).

### Day 5 ΓÇö Departure
- Morning at a caf├⌐ / last-minute shopping.
- Afternoon TGV back to Zurich.

> Swap Paris for Lyon/Annecy by reusing the same day structure (arrival ΓåÆ highlights ΓåÆ neighborhoods ΓåÆ day trip ΓåÆ departure).

---

## 5. Budget Estimate (per person, mid-range, 5 days)

| Category | Estimate |
| --- | --- |
| Round-trip train (booked early) | Γé¼120ΓÇô250 |
| Accommodation (4 nights) | Γé¼400ΓÇô700 |
| Food & drink | Γé¼200ΓÇô350 |
| Attractions & museums | Γé¼80ΓÇô150 |
| Local transport | Γé¼30ΓÇô60 |
| **Total** | **Γé¼830ΓÇô1,510** |

> Save with: rail advance fares, museum passes (e.g. **Paris Museum Pass**), and apartment stays.

---

## 6. Packing Checklist

- [ ] Passport / national ID (both valid for Schengen)
- [ ] Bank/credit cards + some EUR cash
- [ ] Phone, charger, EU plug (Type C/E works in both countries)
- [ ] Comfortable walking shoes
- [ ] Weather-appropriate layers + light rain jacket
- [ ] Reusable water bottle
- [ ] Travel insurance details
- [ ] Train tickets / boarding passes (digital + backup)
- [ ] Small day bag

---

## 7. Practical Tips

- **Money**: Switzerland uses CHF, France uses EUR ΓÇö withdraw/exchange before crossing.
- **Trains**: Validate reservations; TGV requires a seat reservation.
- **Language**: A few French phrases (*bonjour, merci, s'il vous pla├«t*) go a long way.
- **Tipping**: Service is included in France; round up for good service.
- **Connectivity**: An EU/Swiss-inclusive eSIM avoids roaming surprises.
- **Safety**: Watch for pickpockets in busy tourist areas and on transit.
- **Opening hours**: Many shops/museums close on Mondays or Sundays ΓÇö check ahead.

---

## 8. Pre-Trip To-Do

- [ ] Decide destination & dates
- [ ] Book transport (train/flight)
- [ ] Reserve accommodation
- [ ] Plan must-see attractions & book tickets
- [ ] Arrange travel insurance
- [ ] Check passport validity
- [ ] Notify bank of travel / set up travel card
- [ ] Download offline maps & translation app

---

*Happy travels! Bon voyage! ≡ƒÜä*

```

</details>

In Outline view, I need a filter that "Show only exported members"(functions, constants or whatever), as if I were using a developer tool to inspect a library from a caller's perspective.

+ For module js, the filter should find members modified by `export` keyword.
+ For classic js (if supported), the filter should find function/variables exposed to global scope, those including top-level functions/vars. consider also eslint `/* exported xxx */` and jsdoc `/** @global */`.
+ For wasm/wat (if supported), the filter should find exports; see example https://beta.tourofdreamland.net/wasm-inspector/
+ For lib files of other languages, this filter may find members that are public, public static or modified by other keyword of the same concept.

input.body: I tried to configure the settings `extensions.autoUpdate` to `on` and `extensions.autoUpdateDelay` to `48` from a GPO for my organization. The `extensions.autoUpdate` is correctly configured and set to `Managed by organization`, but the `extensions.autoUpdateDelay` doesn't have the `Managed by organization` and is still set to the default value: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.126.0 - OS Version: Windows11 Enterprise 25H2 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Close VSCode on the managed computer 2. Download the default policies from VSCode 1.126.0 zip, and install them 3. Configure the `ExtensionsAutoUpdate` to `Enabled` + `Auto update` 4. Configure the `ExtensionsAutoUpdateDelay` to `Enabled` + `48` 5. Run `gpupdate.exe /force /target:computer` on the managed computer 6. Re-open VSCode on the managed computer 7. Verify the extensions settings

Type: Feature Request ## Feature Request: Allow extensions to embed native remote desktop sessions inside an editor tab ### Summary I would like VS Code to support embedding a native Remote Desktop / RDP session directly inside an editor tab, in the same area where files such as `runTest.js` are displayed. Currently, VS Code extensions can open Webviews, terminals, custom editors, and external applications, but there does not seem to be an official API that allows an extension to host a native OS window, such as `mstsc.exe`, inside a VS Code editor tab. ### Use case I am building a VS Code extension for remote development and automation. The goal is to let users manage SSH, macros, terminal automation, and remote desktop connections from a single VS Code workspace. A useful workflow would be: 1. Open VS Code. 2. Select a saved remote machine. 3. Open an RDP session. 4. Display the remote desktop directly inside a VS Code editor tab, next to source files, terminals, and extension views. This would make VS Code a unified remote operations workspace rather than forcing users to switch between VS Code and a separate Remote Desktop window. ### Current limitations At the moment, the available options are: * Launch `mstsc.exe` or another RDP client externally. * Use a Webview with an HTML5 remote desktop gateway such as Apache Guacamole. * Open a browser-based remote desktop interface inside VS CodeΓÇÖs Simple Browser or a custom Webview. These workarounds are useful, but they are not equivalent to embedding a native RDP client directly in VS Code. Launching an external RDP client breaks the integrated workflow, while using a web gateway requires extra infrastructure and is more complex for users to install. ### Requested capability It would be helpful if VS Code provided an official API that allows extensions to embed or host native external application windows inside an editor tab or custom editor. For example, an extension could start a process such as: ```text mstsc.exe ``` and attach its window to a VS Code editor area. A possible API could look conceptually like: ```ts vscode.window.createNativeWindowPanel({ title: 'Remote Desktop', executable: 'mstsc.exe', args: ['connection.rdp'], viewColumn: vscode.ViewColumn.One }); ``` This does not need to be specific to RDP. The general capability would be useful for tools that need to display native UI inside VS Code while remaining part of the workspace. ### Security considerations I understand that embedding native windows inside VS Code may have security, portability, and sandboxing implications. A safe version of this feature could include: * Explicit user confirmation before launching or embedding a native application. * Workspace Trust restrictions. * Extension permission declarations. * Platform-specific support only where feasible. * Clear separation between Webviews and native embedded windows. * No automatic access to credentials or system resources beyond the launched process. ### Why this would be valuable This would enable extensions to provide a much more integrated experience for: * Remote desktop / RDP tools. * VM consoles. * Embedded device consoles. * Legacy enterprise tools. * Remote administration workflows. * Development environments that require both code and graphical remote access. For users, the benefit would be a single VS Code workspace containing code, terminals, logs, automation tools, and remote desktop sessions. ### Alternative If embedding arbitrary native windows is not feasible, another useful option would be an official remote desktop view API or a recommended extension API pattern for building integrated RDP/VNC/remote display clients inside VS Code. Thank you for considering this feature request. input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Currently, when using the GitHub Copilot chat interface, each coding agent's answer concludes with a small string of metadata/statistics, such as:

> `MAI-Code-1-Flash ΓÇó 2.5 credits`

While knowing the model name and credit consumption is highly useful, there is currently no visibility into the actual processing time or latency of the request. Users have to manually guess or time how long a generation takes.

### Γ₧ò Describe the solution you'd like

I would love to see the total duration of the generationΓÇömeasured from the exact moment the prompt is submitted to when the response completesΓÇöappended to that same stats line.

For example, it could look something like this:

* `MAI-Code-1-Flash ΓÇó 2.5 credits ΓÇó 3.4s`
* `MAI-Code-1-Flash ΓÇó 2.5 credits ΓÇó 0:03`

### ≡ƒöä Describe alternatives you've considered

The only current alternative is manually tracking the clock or opening developer tools to inspect network request timings, both of which are cumbersome for standard daily use.

### ≡ƒôï Additional context

* This will help developers better evaluate model performance and responsiveness directly within their workflow.

---

≡ƒñû *Drafted with care by Gemini, your AI collaborator.*
input.title: ≡ƒÜÇ Feature Request: Include response duration in Copilot Chat statistics

input.body: ## Summary Since June 25, 2026, the new GitHub Copilot Agent in VS Code has stopped working for my account. The Agent UI shows **"No models available"**, there is no **Auto** option, and submitting any prompt does nothing. This started immediately after the recent Free/Student model selection change. I understand that Student plans now use Auto model selection instead of manual model selection. However, this appears to be a different issue because Copilot itself is authenticated correctly, model metadata is successfully retrieved, and internal model requests succeed. --- ## Environment VS Code ``` Version: 1.126.0 (User Setup) Commit: 7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a Electron: 42.2.0 Node.js: 24.15.0 Windows 11 ``` Copilot Chat ``` Built-in Version: 0.54.0 ``` Remote ``` WSL Ubuntu Linux 6.18.33.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 ``` GitHub Copilot Plan ``` free_educational_quota (Student) ``` --- ## Expected behavior Although Student plans now use Auto model selection, the Agent should still work. I expected either: - Auto model selection - or another supported default model The Agent should accept prompts and execute normally. --- ## Actual behavior The Agent UI shows ``` No models available ``` There is no Auto option. Submitting a prompt does nothing. --- ## Runtime Status GitHub Copilot Chat Runtime Status reports ``` TypeError: e is not iterable ``` Triggered by ``` onChatParticipant:github.copilot.editsAgent ``` --- ## Copilot Output Authentication succeeds. ``` Logged in as myoinbong Got Copilot token copilot token sku: free_educational_quota ``` Model metadata is successfully fetched. ``` Fetched model metadata ``` Language model access succeeds. ``` activationBlocker from 'languageModelAccess' ``` BYOK models are also loaded successfully. ``` BYOK: Copilot Chat known models list fetched successfully. ConversationFeature: BYOK models available ``` Copilot can successfully execute internal model requests. Example: ``` success | gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18 ``` However, the Agent still reports ``` No models available ``` --- ## Additional log messages The following errors also appear. ``` Error fetching partner agents: PermissiveAuthRequiredError: Permissive authentication is required ``` Developer Console also reports ``` TypeError: e is not iterable ``` --- ## Notes This issue started immediately after the recent Student / Free model selection changes. The problem does **not** appear to be authentication or model entitlement because: - Copilot authentication succeeds - Student plan is detected correctly - Model metadata is retrieved successfully - Internal GPT requests succeed The failure appears to happen while initializing the new Agent UI, which ultimately reports "No models available".
input.title: Copilot Agent shows "No models available" despite successful model metadata retrieval (Student plan)

input.body:
* Copilot Chat Extension Version: Built in
* VS Code Version: 1.126.0
* OS Version:
  * Windows 11
  * WSL: Fedora 43
* Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent Mode (Claude Agent)
* Selected model: Tried all models
* Logs:

  ```text
  2026-06-25 13:11:51.225 [info] [ClaudeCodeSession] Passing 9 plugin(s) from skill locations
  2026-06-25 13:11:56.179 [error] Error: Error during execution
  	at q8o (/home/mukund/.vscode-server/bin/7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:2908:480)
  	at ebr (/home/mukund/.vscode-server/bin/7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:2908:691)
  	at n.invokeFunction (/home/mukund/.vscode-server/bin/7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:29:944)
  	at oV._processMessages (/home/mukund/.vscode-server/bin/7e7950df89d055b5a378379db9ee14290772148a/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:2915:17122)
  	at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:104:5)
  ```

## Description

Claude Code is configured in our enterprise environment to use Anthropic models through Amazon Bedrock and works correctly when used directly.

However, when attempting to use the Claude Agent from GitHub Copilot Chat, the agent consistently fails during execution. The same issue occurs in both WSL and native Windows environments, which suggests this is not specific to WSL.

The main difficulty is that the failure does not expose enough diagnostic information to determine whether the issue originates from:

* GitHub Copilot Chat
* Claude Agent integration
* Claude Code
* Amazon Bedrock
* Authentication/authorization
* Model compatibility

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure Claude Code to use the enterprise-provided Bedrock model:
   `/model/us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0/invoke-with-response-stream`
2. Verify Claude Code works successfully.
3. Open VS Code with GitHub Copilot Chat.
4. Select Claude Agent / Agent Mode.
5. Execute any task that requires agent execution.
6. Observe that the agent fails during execution.

## Expected Behavior

Claude Agent should successfully execute using the same enterprise Claude model configuration that Claude Code uses.

## Actual Behavior

The agent fails during execution. The surfaced error information is insufficient to identify the root cause or determine which component is failing.

## Additional Information

* Reproduced on both Windows and WSL.
* Claude Code itself is functional against the same Bedrock configuration.
* The issue appears specific to the Claude Agent integration path.
* I have been unable to locate logs that indicate:

  * Whether the request reaches Bedrock
  * Whether model invocation succeeds
  * Whether the failure occurs before or after agent initialization

## Questions

1. Is the above Bedrock-hosted Claude model supported by Claude Agent in Copilot Chat?
2. Are there known limitations with enterprise Claude Code configurations that use Amazon Bedrock?
3. Is there a way to enable verbose/debug logging for Claude Agent execution?
4. Where can detailed logs be found to determine exactly where the failure occurs?
input.title: Claude Agent fails with "Error during execution" when Claude Code is configured via Amazon Bedrock

- VS Code Version: Insiders commit `fcff1a779c023e586c6d44a18c04a04166b59f37`
- OS Version: Windows 11, WSL2 Ubuntu

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update to the latest VS Code Insiders build (commit `fcff1a779c023e586c6d44a18c04a04166b59f37`)
2. Open any WSL remote folder (`wsl+ubuntu`)
3. VS Code attempts to download the server binary, both from the Windows side and from inside WSL. and both return HTTP 404
4. Remote session fails with "WSL Server closed unexpectedly"

Error from WSL extension log
```
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 404 Not Found
ERROR: Failed to download https://update.code.visualstudio.com/commit:fcff1a779c023e586c6d44a18c04a04166b59f37/server-linux-x64/insider
```

Expected:
WSL connection succeeds after updating.

Workaround: 
Reverted to previous Insiders build (`2858f2c0200dc2f4f87aca248c69daaf85f3ce69`).
input.title: WSL Server returns 404 after Insiders update

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Currently, the VS Code integrated browser appears to be available only to Copilot CLI. It would be extremely useful if the DOM from the integrated browser could also be made available to Claude Code during chat sessions.

This would significantly improve automation workflows, especially when working with tools like Playwright. Claude could inspect the live DOM, understand the current page state, generate more accurate locators, debug failing tests, and suggest reliable automation code without requiring users to manually copy HTML or screenshots.

Enabling Claude Code to access the integrated browser DOM would make browser automation development much more efficient and bring feature parity with the existing Copilot CLI integration.
input.title: Expose the VS Code integrated browser DOM to Claude Code chat

input.body:
# Bug: reasoningEffort setting written to wrong vendor section in chatLanguageModels.json

## Bug Description

When setting `reasoningEffort` for a model in the Chat Language Models configuration, the setting is written to the wrong vendor section in `chatLanguageModels.json`.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure multiple custom endpoint vendors in `chatLanguageModels.json` (e.g., DeepSeek, MiMo, MyCustom)
2. Each vendor has different models defined
3. Select a model (e.g., `gpt-5.5` under MyCustom vendor) and set its `reasoningEffort` to `"high"` via the VS Code UI
4. Check the `chatLanguageModels.json` file

## Expected Behavior

The `settings` object with `reasoningEffort` should be added under the **MyCustom** vendor section where the `gpt-5.5` model is defined:

```json
{
    "name": "MyCustom",
    "vendor": "customendpoint",
    "models": [
        {
            "id": "gpt-5.5",
            "name": "gpt-5.5",
            "url": "http://127.0.0.1:15721",
            "toolCalling": true,
            "vision": true,
            "streaming": true,
            "thinking": true,
            "reasoningEffortFormat": "responses",
            "supportsReasoningEffort": [
                "none",
                "minimal",
                "low",
                "medium",
                "high",
                "xhigh"
            ],
            "maxInputTokens": 1050000,
            "maxOutputTokens": 128000
        }
    ],
    "settings": {
        "gpt-5.5": {
            "reasoningEffort": "high"
        }
    }
}
```

## Actual Behavior

The `settings` object is incorrectly written under the **DeepSeek** vendor section, which doesn't even contain a `gpt-5.5` model:

```json
{
    "name": "DeepSeek",
    "vendor": "customendpoint",
    "models": [
        {
            "id": "deepseek-v4-pro",
            "name": "deepseek-v4-pro",
            "url": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
            "toolCalling": true,
            "vision": true,
            "streaming": true,
            "thinking": true,
            "supportsReasoningEffort": [
                "high",
                "max"
            ],
            "maxInputTokens": 1000000,
            "maxOutputTokens": 384000
        }
    ],
    "settings": {
        "gpt-5.5": {
            "reasoningEffort": "high"
        }
    }
}
```

## Configuration File

File path: `%APPDATA%\Code\User\chatLanguageModels.json`

## Environment

- VS Code Version: 1.126.0
- OS: Windows
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat

## Suggested Labels

- `chat` or `copilot-chat`
- `bug`
- `settings`
input.title: Bug: reasoningEffort setting written to wrong vendor section in chatLanguageModels.json

input.body: The old issue reporter used to support extensions and now does not. Disabling with `issueReporter.wizard.enabled` brings back the classic issue reporter with this functionality. Several extension authors raised they weren't seeing issues roll in via reporter with this change.
input.title: Issue reporter no longer supports extensions

input.body:
Type: Bug Actual Behavior Every time I open the Chat panel (click the Chat icon or press Ctrl+Shift+I), VS Code's memory usage spikes from ~500MB to 8-10GB within 10-30 seconds. The entire editor becomes completely unresponsive (UI freezes, no input accepted), and I have to force-kill the process via Task Manager. Expected Behavior Opening Chat panel should not cause abnormal memory growth. Memory usage should remain stable or increase moderately. Steps to Reproduce Close VS Code completely, ensure all Code.exe processes are terminated. Launch VS Code (with any project, or even an empty folder). Open Task Manager and observe baseline memory (~500MB-1GB). Click the Chat icon in the activity bar (or press Ctrl+Shift+I). Watch memory usage climb to 8-10GB within seconds, then UI freezes. What I've Already Tried (Did NOT Help) Deleted all caches: %APPDATA%\Code\Cache, GPUCache, workspaceStorage Started with --disable-gpu Started with --disable-extensions (issue still occurs when opening Chat) Started with a fresh user-data-dir (--user-data-dir) Removed all extensions physically Additional Notes Project folder size is only ~500MB, not a large monorepo. The issue also reproduces in an empty folder, ruling out project indexing as the cause. The problem started recently and is 100% reproducible every time. <!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒò« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ≡ƒöÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ≡ƒº¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒÆí Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ≡ƒöº Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ≡ƒ¬ô If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ≡ƒôú Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.126.0
- OS Version: 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a 3-level nested Git repository structure. You can run the following bash script:
    ```bash
    # Level 1: Root repo
    mkdir root-repo && cd root-repo && git init
    
    # Level 2: 'modules' repo
    mkdir modules && cd modules && git init
    
    # Level 3: Nested repos inside 'modules'
    mkdir sub_repo_A && cd sub_repo_A && git init && echo "A" > a.txt && cd ..
    mkdir sub_repo_B && cd sub_repo_B && git init && echo "B" > b.txt && cd ..
    cd ..
    ```
2. Open `root-repo` in VS Code.
3. **Actual Behavior 1:** Look at the Source Control view. Only `root-repo` and `modules` are detected. The 3rd-level repositories (`sub_repo_A`, `sub_repo_B`) are completely missing.
4. **The "Lazy Load" Trigger:** Open the file `modules/sub_repo_A/a.txt` in the editor, and then run `Developer: Reload Window` from the Command Palette.
5. **Actual Behavior 2:** Now `sub_repo_A` magically appears in the Source Control view.
6. **Expected Behavior:** VS Code should proactively traverse and detect the 3rd-level nested repositories upon workspace initialization, without requiring the user to manually open files inside them and reload the window.
input.title: Git: 3rd-level nested repositories fail to discover automatically in Source Control until file opened and window reloaded

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
## Feature Request

The VS Code Integrated Browser is an excellent feature for development and testing workflows. It allows engineers to interact with web applications without opening a separate browser window, making the development experience much smoother.

However, for QA engineers and teams using modern automation frameworks such as Playwright, the integrated browser currently does not support Playwright CLI functionality.

### Current Limitation

When using Playwright CLI, features such as:

- Automated navigation across pages
- Snapshot/screenshot capture during test execution
- End-to-end test debugging and inspection

are not supported within the Integrated Browser.

### Expected Behavior

It would be extremely useful if the Integrated Browser could support Playwright CLI interactions, allowing QA engineers to:

- Run Playwright tests directly against the Integrated Browser
- Capture snapshots/screenshots during navigation
- Debug and inspect automated test flows without launching an external browser

### Benefits

- Improves the testing and debugging experience for QA engineers.
- Reduces the need to open separate Chrome/Edge instances.
- Provides a more unified development and testing workflow inside VS Code.

Thank you for considering this enhancement. I believe this would be a valuable addition for teams using Playwright-based automation frameworks.
input.title: Add Playwright CLI support to the Integrated Browser

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Summary

Please expose the Claude `/code-review` slash command (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/code-review#review-a-diff-locally) through the normal `claude-code` chat session contribution path, so it is available in the Copilot Chat panel when using Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK without requiring the Agent Host implementation.

## Background

Currently, Claude commands such as `/review` and `/simplify` are statically exposed via `extensions/copilot/package.json` under `contributes.chatSessions` for the `claude-code` session type.

However, `/code-review` appears to be surfaced only through the Agent Host sessions/customizations path, specifically via:

- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/claude/customizations/claudeBuiltinCommands.ts`
- `src/vs/sessions/skills/code-review/SKILL.md`
- the Sessions ΓÇ£Run Code ReviewΓÇ¥ toolbar action, which sends `/code-review`

This means `/code-review` is available in Agent Host / Sessions contexts, but not in the normal Claude Code chat-session command list.

## Request

Add `/code-review` to the `claude-code` chat session command contribution in `extensions/copilot/package.json`, alongside existing commands like `/review`, `/security-review`, and `/simplify`.

Suggested command entry:

```json
{
  "name": "code-review",
  "description": "Review the current diff for correctness bugs, regressions, missing tests, and actionable issues"
}
```

## Motivation

`/code-review` is already a recognized built-in skill/command in the Agent Host Claude path and has clearer semantics than `/review` or `/simplify`:

- `/review` appears oriented toward PR review or general review.
- `/simplify` focuses on reuse, simplification, and efficiency cleanup.
- `/code-review` is specifically intended for reviewing current changes for correctness issues and actionable code comments.

Users working in the normal Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK chat path should be able to discover and invoke this command without switching to or depending on Agent Host.

## Expected Behavior

When using the normal Claude Code chat session in Copilot Chat:

1. Typing `/` should show `/code-review` in the slash-command list.
2. Running `/code-review` should route to the Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK command if supported by the installed SDK/runtime.
3. The behavior should align with the Agent Host Sessions ΓÇ£Run Code ReviewΓÇ¥ action, which already sends `/code-review`.

## Current Behavior

`/code-review` is not exposed in the static `contributes.chatSessions[type="claude-code"].commands` list, even though related commands such as `/review`, `/security-review`, and `/simplify` are exposed.

## Notes

This request is not asking to change the Agent Host implementation. It is asking to make the existing `/code-review` command discoverable and usable through the normal Claude Code chat-session command contribution path as well.
input.title: Expose `/code-review` in Claude Code chat session commands without requiring Agent Host

# System/Tooling Context - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.41 - VS Code Version: 1.126.0 - OS Version: Window 11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent Customization - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): NA (model does not matter in this case) - Notes: - For this bug I am using **rwoll/markdown-review** as an example as that is what is being used in the [VS docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agent-customization/agent-plugins#_install-a-plugin-from-source) - I think this could be easily fixed by having uninstalled plugins getting removed from the chat.plugins.marketplaces section in %APPDATA%/Code/User/settings.json - This is largely just an annoying user experience, but also raises security risks as you can be downloading from repos/branches you don't want # Steps to Reproduce (simple but not realistic): 1. Open VS Code settings (%APPDATA%/Code/User/settings.json) 2. Add a link to a branch that no longer exists `"chat.plugins.marketplaces": [ "rwoll/markdown-review#BAD"]`
3. `Chat: Install Plugin From Source` from the Command Palette 4. Enter the sub url (**rwoll/markdown-review** for example) 5. Note that you get an error message
# Steps to Reproduce (realistic but much harder to setup): 1. Create a simple agent plugin project in a new GitHub repo and [setup to distribute any skills or actions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agent-customization/agent-plugins) 2. Get plugin code in main 3. Create a temp branch off of main 4. Install the temp branch of the plugin using `Chat: Install Plugin From Source` from the Command Palette
*You will need to follow this format to add from a branch `ORG/PROJECT#BRANCH` 5. Open `Chat: Open Customizations` from the Command Palette and go to the Plugins page 6. Uninstall the plugin 7. Delete the temp branch in git 8. Delete the local cached downloaded code: `%USERPROFILE%/.vscode/agent-plugins/github.com`
*This folder doesn't often get deleted, but it can for various reason 9. Install the plugin (without specifying the branch) using `Chat: Install Plugin From Source` from the Command Palette 10. Note that you get an "Failed to install plugin" error message for the deleted branch # Workaround: Manually delete old marketplace values from %APPDATA%/Code/User/settings.json
input.title: Agent customization for local continues to attempt to download old plugins from source after they have been uninstalled

input.body:
Follow-up from #322774 and related to #322112.

## Context
#322774 makes Agent Host consume the real `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` setting by forwarding normalized terminal auto-approve rules through Agent Host root config.

As part of that PR, `CommandAutoApprover` still keeps a bundled hard-coded default rule table as a compatibility fallback for clients that do not forward the setting yet. A TODO was added near that fallback:

> Remove this fallback once all agent-host clients are guaranteed to forward `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` before shell approvals run.

## Work
- Audit Agent Host clients and startup ordering to confirm terminal auto-approve rules are always forwarded before shell approval checks can run.
- Remove the bundled hard-coded fallback rule table from `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/commandAutoApprover.ts` once forwarding is guaranteed.
- Keep or adjust the source-of-truth tests so forwarded `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` rules remain authoritative over any fallback behavior.
- Confirm local and remote Agent Host paths still respect `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove` and `chat.tools.terminal.ignoreDefaultAutoApproveRules` after the fallback is removed.

## Links
- Follow-up PR: #322774
- Original tracking issue: #322112
input.title: Remove Agent Host terminal auto-approve fallback rules

input.body:
**Priority:** P0

The Tools Grouping Service ("virtual tools"), built by @connor4312, keeps large tool sets from inflating prompt size and degrading tool selection. Once the active tool count crosses a threshold, related tools are collapsed into onΓÇædemand **virtual tool groups** so the model sees a compact set instead of a long flat list ΓÇö a COGS win that grows with the number of MCP/extension tools. We need this behavior to carry over as CLI/AHP becomes the default agent.

## How it works

- Grouping kicks in once the tool count passes `START_GROUPING_AFTER_TOOL_COUNT` (currently `HARD_TOOL_LIMIT / 2 = 64`), keeping the request under the 128 hard tool limit. The threshold is experimentΓÇæbased (`VirtualToolThreshold`).
- `IToolGroupingService.create(sessionId, tools)` returns an `IToolGrouping` that:
  - `compute(query)` ΓÇö returns the (possibly grouped) tool list for the request;
  - expands a group on demand when the model calls into it (`didCall` / `ensureExpanded`), and collapses tools by recency across turns (`didTakeTurn`);
  - is backed by a persisted grouping cache (`ToolGroupingCache`).
- Categorization uses the clientΓÇæside embeddings service to cluster related tools.

Lives in the Copilot extension under `extension/tools/common/virtualTools/` (`toolGroupingService.ts`, `toolGrouping.ts`, `virtualToolGrouper.ts`, `virtualToolGroupCache.ts`).

## Why it needs attention for AHP/CLI

- The service runs in the Copilot extension and depends on the clientΓÇæside embeddings service, so it does **not** automatically apply to CLI / agentΓÇæhost (SDK) sessions.
- As with Client Side Tool Search, we need SDK/CLI buyΓÇæin to let the client supply tool grouping ΓÇö or an equivalent path ΓÇö so the token savings hold when CLI is the default. The client would keep the embeddings index updated against the SDK tool set.

## References

- Umbrella: #321462 ΓÇö Client Side Tool Search & Tools Grouping Service
- Original implementation by @connor4312 (virtual tools: initial implementation, autoΓÇætrigger at the 128 tool limit, enablement simplification, lifecycle fixes).

---
**Context:** Part of the CLI/AHPΓÇæasΓÇædefault migration. Tracking given the COGS impact of tool grouping on large tool sets.
input.title: [AHP/CLI:COGS] Tools Grouping Service (Virtual Tools)

input.body:
Tracking the experimental, versionΓÇæspecific alternate system prompts for Claude Opus 4.7 and 4.8. Both are optΓÇæin (default off) and layer modelΓÇæspecific tuning on top of the base prompt, following Anthropic's perΓÇæversion prompting guides.

## Opus 4.7 ΓÇö Copilot extension

A standalone `Claude47OpusPrompt` (copy of `Claude46OpusPrompt`) gated by the experimentΓÇæbased setting `github.copilot.chat.claude47OpusPrompt.enabled` (default `false`). When enabled, `claude-opus-4.7*` traffic routes to it; otherwise it falls back to the 4.6 Opus prompt.

Highlights vs 4.6 (per the Opus 4.7 prompting guide): refined toolΓÇætriggering and subagent fanΓÇæout, responseΓÇæshape/verbosity wording, full operationalSafety, plus targeted lessons ΓÇö no internal narration between tool calls, endΓÇæofΓÇæturn summary cap, comment discipline, "don't duplicate inΓÇæflight subagent work", "verify subagent output before reporting done", and a `<skillUsage>` primer.

- PR: #313916 (cherryΓÇæpick #314193)

## Opus 4.8 ΓÇö Agent host (CLI)

Delivered through the new perΓÇæmodel systemΓÇæprompt registry for Copilot agentΓÇæhost sessions. `Claude48OpusPromptResolver` matches Opus 4.8 and is gated by the VS Code setting `chat.agentHost.opus48Prompt.enabled` (agentΓÇæhost config `opus48Prompt`, default off). When off it returns `undefined` and the session uses the default SDK system message.

Per Anthropic's Opus 4.8 guide the overrides are intentionally minimal ΓÇö the model performs well out of the box ΓÇö keeping the SDK foundation (tool/safety sections) intact and only nudging two behaviors:
- **Tone:** concise, focused responses; minimal examples; sparing emoji.
- **Subagents:** don't spawn for work doable in a single response; fan out multiple subagents in one turn when reading multiple files / iterating across items.

No interimΓÇæprogress scaffolding is added (the guide warns against forcing "summarize after every N tool calls").

- PR: #321864

## Reference

Opus 4.7 tracking, eval runs, and experiment: microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#7600
input.title: [AHP/CLI] Claude Opus 4.7 / 4.8 Alt Prompt

input.body:
Summary

Add Multi-Chat support to the Claude harness so it matches the Copilot experience.

Background

- The Multi-Chat feature (previously called Sub-Sessions) is currently available for the Copilot harness and works in AHP sessions for Copilot.
- Team feedback and user studies have shown confusion around the feature and its name; the UX should be consistent across harnesses rather than Copilot-only.
- Claude AHP sessions do not currently show the Multi-Chat UI.

Proposal

- Implement Multi-Chat in the Claude harness with parity to the Copilot implementation and UX.
- Ensure Multi-Chat is available in AHP sessions for Claude as it is for Copilot.
- Minimize agent integration effort (sessions should be transparent to agents where possible), and document any agent-facing changes required.
- Update tests, e2e scenarios, and relevant documentation to reflect Multi-Chat availability in the Claude harness.

Acceptance criteria

- Multi-Chat UI appears and behaves the same in the Claude harness as it does in Copilot.
- Multi-Chat is available and functional in AHP sessions for Claude.
- Agent/handler code for Claude either requires no changes or any required changes are documented and minimal.
- Relevant tests and documentation updated.

Implementation notes

- Verify whether sessions need to be linked or if the agent surface can remain unchanged (transparent sessions).
- Coordinate UX changes with the Copilot harness team to ensure consistency and avoid repeating prior confusion around naming/behavior.
- Add testing steps to validate in manual and automated tests across the Claude harness and AHP sessions.

---

Split from the combined Claude & Codex issue. Codex tracked separately in #323624.

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09MVCZKLP4/p1782230045706259?thread_ts=1782230045.706259&cid=C09MVCZKLP4)
input.title: Support Multi-Chat in Claude harness

input.body:
**Priority:** P0

Anthropic supports an extended **1ΓÇæhour cache TTL** for prompt caching, versus the default 5ΓÇæminute TTL. LongerΓÇælived cache entries can raise cacheΓÇæhit rates across longer agent sessions ΓÇö and across summarization/compaction boundaries ΓÇö which reduces the uncached input billed per turn. The catch is that 1h cache **writes** cost more, so the net COGS impact depends on how much reuse each workload actually gets.

## The tradeoff

- **5ΓÇæminute TTL (default):** cache write = **1.25├ù** base input.
- **1ΓÇæhour TTL (extended):** cache write = **2.0├ù** base input.
- Applies only to Anthropic extendedΓÇæTTLΓÇæeligible models (per `modelSupportsExtendedCacheTtl`); all other models are unaffected.

For COGS modeling, the extendedΓÇæTTL path prices uncached input at **base ├ù (2.0 / 1.25) = ├ù1.6** for eligible Anthropic models, so the write premium is reflected when comparing against the 5ΓÇæminute baseline. The win is only realized when the higher cacheΓÇæhit lift outweighs that 1.6├ù write premium.

## Open questions / followΓÇæups

- **Telemetry gap:** Anthropic returns `cache_creation.ephemeral_5m_input_tokens` and `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens` separately, but we only capture the combined total. A hybridΓÇæTTL strategy (e.g. system + tools on 1h, messages on 5m) can't be validated without a telemetry change to split these.
- Identify which models/workloads recoup the 1.6├ù write premium through higher cache reuse, and whether a hybridΓÇæTTL allocation is worth pursuing.

## Reference

Detailed COGS accounting and perΓÇæmodel breakdown: microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#7825

---
**Context:** Part of the CLI/AHPΓÇæasΓÇædefault migration. Tracking given the direct COGS impact of cacheΓÇæTTL selection.
input.title: [AHP/CLI:COGS] Anthropic Extended Cache (1hr TTL)

input.body:
The agent `grep_search` (`findTextInFiles`) tool was returning verbose, XMLΓÇætagged results that dominated its toolΓÇæcall token usage. PRs #321601 and #321938 introduce a compact, grepΓÇæstyle output format behind an experiment. Early results show the new format roughly **halves** the tokens returned by the tool with no loss of useful signal ΓÇö a direct COGS win as we move CLI/AHP to the default agent.

## What changed

- **New compact output format** ΓÇö A `grep`-style result (plain `path` followed by `line:text`, blankΓÇæline separated) replaces the previous tagΓÇæwrapped output. Gated by the experimentΓÇæbased setting `github.copilot.chat.tools.grepSearch.outputFormat` (`tag` | `grep`, default `tag`).
  - #321601 ΓÇö Optimize grep output and enhance configuration
- **TokenΓÇæbudgetΓÇæaware rendering + longΓÇæline handling** ΓÇö Results render through promptΓÇætsx, so they participate in tokenΓÇæbudget pruning (earlier files prioritized) and carry editor references instead of a single unprunable blob. Long lines are truncated to a max length with context kept around the match.
  - #321938 ΓÇö Fix long line rendering for grep style output
- **Smarter result capping** ΓÇö The `maxResults` budget is distributed proportionally across matching files (largestΓÇæremainder, min one match per file), and paths are returned workspaceΓÇærelative.

## Experiment

[`agent.tool.responselength`](https://exp.microsoft.com/features/30687fdb-eb7c-dbe8-06c4-02c5ece0671a/progressions?workspaceId=dbd12384-5001-4e90-a863-526321eaf233) ΓÇö treatment enables `grepSearch.outputFormat = grep` via the `github.copilot-chat/agent.tool.responselength` flag.

### Tokens returned by `grep_search` (control = `tag`, treatment = `grep`)

| Group | Events | Total Tokens | Avg | P50 | P95 | P99 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| control | 363,400 | 421,529,887 | 1,160 | 321.2 | 5,275.4 | 13,091.6 |
| treatment | 389,097 | 200,998,321 | 516.6 | 137.7 | 2,123.6 | 6,380.8 |

- **~55% fewer tokens per call** on average (1,160 ΓåÆ 517); P95 down ~60%, P99 down ~51%.
- Total tokens dropped from **421.5M ΓåÆ 201.0M**, despite treatment serving *more* calls (389K vs 363K events).
- `PValue = 0` ΓÇö statistically significant.

---
**Context:** Part of the CLI/AHPΓÇæasΓÇædefault migration. Tracking given the direct COGS impact of tool response length.
input.title: [AHP/CLI:COGS] Grep Search Tool Output Optimization

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->

I have a cluster of remote servers and I sometimes want to edit the same file on all servers.  The current option is to edit the file locally and scp the local copy to each server.  This is usually for files which are not committed to a git repository, such as environment files with sensitive data but it could also apply if I want to push the output of a frontend build to a set of servers.

It might be a cool feature if I can open a local folder and the same folder on a set of remote hosts and if they are all identical then show a single view so that an edit to one of the local files is copied automatically to all hosts in the set.  

If there are any differences these could be highlighted in some way and click to compare as in the Source Control view.  Binary files would be excluded from the diff check.  A nice addition would be a flag that all tests must pass before the local file is copied to all remote hosts in the set and/or a button to push the changed file to the hosts in the set if auto sync is not enabled.

Issue #200524 discusses grouping remote hosts which would be useful for this feature.

The paths on the local host and the remote hosts may differ so in grouping the remote hosts a path to the remote folder could be defined for all hosts in the group.
input.title: ssh sessions to multiple remote hosts with synced view

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No / not applicable. This reproduces in VS Code's built-in MCP enterprise-managed auth path; disabling MCP/Copilot removes the feature being tested.

- VS Code Version: Visual Studio Code - Insiders 1.126.0-insider
- OS Version: macOS Darwin 25.5.0

## Summary

With MCP enterprise-managed auth / XAA enabled, the first enterprise-managed HTTP MCP server can authenticate and run successfully. A later/new enterprise-managed MCP server using the same configured IdP issuer fails with:

```text
Error getting token from server metadata: Error: No accounts available
Connection state: Error 401 status sending message ... {"detail":"Missing or invalid access token"}
```

The resource authorization server logs show that the XAA token flow for Server B already succeeded: ID-JAG exchange succeeded and jwt-bearer redemption succeeded. VS Code appears to mint/redeem the resource token, but then fails before attaching that token to the MCP initialize request.

Related XAA tracking issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/316625

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure an enterprise-managed MCP IdP:

```jsonc
"mcp.enterpriseManagedAuth.idp": {
  "issuer": "http://localhost:3100",
  "clientId": "<redacted>",
  "clientSecret": "<redacted>"
}
```

2. Configure two HTTP MCP servers with enterprise-managed auth:

```jsonc
{
  "servers": {
    "server-a": {
      "url": "http://localhost:3100/api/v1/proxy/<server-a-id>/mcp",
      "type": "http",
      "oauth": {
        "enterpriseManaged": true,
        "clientId": "<resource-client-id>"
      }
    },
    "server-b": {
      "url": "http://localhost:3100/api/v1/proxy/<server-b-id>/mcp",
      "type": "http",
      "oauth": {
        "enterpriseManaged": true,
        "clientId": "<resource-client-id>"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

3. Start Server A.
4. Complete the IdP login / XAA flow.
5. Confirm Server A discovers tools successfully.
6. Start Server B.

## Expected

After Server A creates the IdP session, Server B should reuse that IdP session, silently mint/redeem its resource token, and send the token to Server B's `/mcp` endpoint.

## Actual

Server B fails with `No accounts available`. The MCP initialize request is retried without a bearer token and the server returns the normal initial 401 challenge.

MCP log for Server B:

```text
Discovered resource metadata at http://localhost:3100/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/api/v1/proxy/<server-b-id>/mcp
Using auth server metadata url: http://localhost:3100
Discovered authorization server metadata at http://localhost:3100/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
Error getting token from server metadata: Error: No accounts available
Connection state: Error 401 status sending message to http://localhost:3100/api/v1/proxy/<server-b-id>/mcp: {"detail":"Missing or invalid access token"}
```

VS Code XAA auth-provider log shows the IdP session exists and the Server B token exchange/redemption calls are made:

```text
Getting sessions for scopes: openid offline_access
Found 1 sessions for scopes: openid offline_access
[XAA] POST http://localhost:3100/api/v1/oauth/token (ID-JAG exchange) audience=http://localhost:3100 resource=http://localhost:3100/api/v1/proxy/<server-b-id>/mcp scope=mcp:proxy
[XAA] POST http://localhost:3100/api/v1/oauth/token (ID-JAG redemption) client_id=<redacted> resource=http://localhost:3100/api/v1/proxy/<server-b-id>/mcp scope=mcp:proxy
```

Resource server audit logs also confirm success for Server B:

```text
action=id_jag_issued server_name=Server B
action=proxy_oauth_token_issued grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer server_name=Server B
```

There is no subsequent tool-list call for Server B, which suggests the redeemed token is not attached to the MCP request.

## Source-Level Hypothesis

This appears to happen in the account-selection path for a subsequent enterprise-managed server:

1. `mainThreadMcp.ts` calls `_getSessionForProvider(...)`.
2. For Server B, there is no existing MCP access grant/preference yet, so `_getSessionForProvider(...)` reaches account selection.
3. `AuthenticationMcpService.selectSession(...)` calls `this._authenticationService.getAccounts(providerId)`.
4. `AuthenticationService.getAccounts(...)` calls `getSessions(id)` without XAA-specific `resource`, `audience`, or scopes.
5. The XAA provider's `getSessions(...)` returns `[]` when those XAA options are missing.
6. `selectSession(...)` throws `No accounts available`, even though the XAA provider already minted/redeemed a valid Server B resource token.

## Possible Fix Direction

For XAA providers, avoid generic account enumeration in `selectSession()` when a resource-scoped session is already available, or make XAA expose the underlying IdP account(s) for account enumeration without requiring `resource` / `audience` options.
input.title: MCP XAA auth fails for subsequent enterprise-managed server with No accounts available

input.body:
When the user's main agent is a BYOK model, and the selection is `default` for a GitHub copilot utility model, currently a user's AI credits are used unless the utility models are switched to BYOK models. 
This default behavior needs to change so that if the main model is a BYOK model, unless explicitly configured, utility models must not use GitHub copilot models
input.title: Copilot Utility Models should default to none when BYOK is selected

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

This is similar to another item as linked below, but this request is slightly different in that I think it would be really useful to have the ability to right-click at any particular location in the agent's response and have the option to 'read aloud from that location' to the end.

In a lot of cases, the responses that I'm getting take a lot of time for read aloud to get through as they have a lot of the 'read' actions, analysis and all the info that I'm not really that interested in. I'm more interested in the distilled findings from the agent, which are at the end of the response. 

If there was the ability to right-click and read from here, I think that'd be really useful and a big time saver for those of us who avail of that feature. It's great for accessibility, but I mainly find it useful in allowing me to step away from my laptop while listening to say the detailed findings of an architectural review or a meaty security strategy etc and start digesting that via my headphones.

Other similar feature request I mentioned: [302997](https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/issues/302997)

Please Speed, I need this ≡ƒÖÅ
input.title: VS Code Speech: Right click to read aloud FROM SPECIFIC LOCATION in the response

input.body:
We recently added telemetry for the quota notification banners but we group all of the quota approaching banners into one. This prevents us from being able to see which ones are most impactful in terms of the user taking an action. Can we make the `RawEventsVSCode
| where EventName ==("monacoworkbench/chatinputnotificationshown")
| summarize by tostring(Properties["telemetryid"])` more granular so that each notification checkpoint is classified separately?
input.title: Add finer grain telemetry for checkpoint banners

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

I would like to request a feature that allows developers to work with multiple independent projects inside a single VS Code window.

Currently, when developers work on separate projects at the same time, such as a React frontend and a .NET backend stored in different repositories, they must open multiple VS Code windows. This becomes difficult to manage, especially for full-stack developers.

**Although Multi-root Workspaces already exist, they still behave as one workspace and do not provide fully isolated project environments.**

### Example Scenario

> Frontend project: React application
> Backend project: .NET API

**Both projects need to:**

> Run simultaneously.
> Have separate terminals.
> Maintain independent debugging configurations.
> Keep separate tasks and settings.
> Allow quick switching between projects.
> Currently, this requires opening two separate VS Code windows.
> Proposed Solution

### Add a new option such as:

> Open New Project
> Add Project to Window
> This option could be available from the Explorer panel menu (three-dot menu) or the File menu.
> When opening another project, VS Code would create a new project tab inside the same window.

### Example Explorer layout:

> Project 1
> Project 2
> Outline
> Timeline

### Today, the sidebar typically contains:

> Workspace
> Outline
> Timeline
> With this feature, each project would appear as its own top-level section, allowing developers to switch between projects without opening additional windows.

### Expected Behavior

> Each project maintains its own terminals.
> Each project has independent debugging sessions.
> Tasks and launch configurations remain isolated.
> Projects can run simultaneously.
> Developers can quickly switch between projects within one window.

### Benefits

> Less desktop and window clutter.
> Better experience for full-stack developers.
> Easier frontend and backend development workflows.
> Improved productivity for microservices and multi-repository projects.
> Better use of screen space, especially on laptops and smaller monitors.

This would provide a tab-based multi-project experience while preserving the existing VS Code workflow and keeping projects logically separated within a single application window.
input.title: Single Window Multi-Project Workspaces with Project Tabs

input.body:
I had an email today from Microsoft:

> Action required: Azure DevOps global PATs are retiring
>
> Global PATs allow a single token to access all Azure DevOps organizations you belong to. To reduce risk from broadly scoped credentials and support regional data requirements, this token type is being retired.
>
> YouΓÇÖre receiving this email because one or more global personal access token(s) associated with your account was used in 1 organization(s) in the last 30 days.

The only global PAT I know of is the one used to publish extensions. I believe it's currently a requirement for this to be global, and the docs at https://code.visualstudio.com/api/working-with-extensions/publishing-extension do say to make it global:

> In the Create a new personal access token modal, select the following details for the token:
> Organization: **All accessible organizations**

So this is a request to support organization-specific tokens before December 1st 2026. If it's already supported (I haven't tested it recently), then just the docs need updating.
input.title: Support publishing extensions with organization-scoped PATs due to global PATs being retired

- VS Code Version: 1.125.1
- OS Version: 
Edition	Windows 11 Enterprise
Version	25H2
Installed on	ΓÇÄ3/ΓÇÄ14/ΓÇÄ2025
OS build	26200.8655
Experience	Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.26100.315.0

When using the Python REPL inside the VS Code Integrated Terminal, output from executed commands is not displayed consistently. For example, running print(0) repeatedly shows the input prompts, but the printed output does not appear after each command.

The REPL accepts input and continues to show the >>> prompt, but stdout appears to be swallowed, hidden, or not rendered. This makes the terminal unusable for debugging because printed statements and errors are not visible.

Python REPL should display:
>>> print(0)
0
>>> print(0)
0
>>> print(0)
0

Actual behavior:
>>> print(0)
>>> print(0)
>>> print(0)
>>> print(0 causes hang and no error is shown until enter is pressed multiple times. 

Troubleshooting already tried
Reinstalled Anaconda
Reinstalled VS Code
Removed/disabled all extensions
Opened a new terminal session
Issue still occurs in the VS Code Integrated Terminal

Steps to Reproduce:
Open VS Code.
Open Integrated Terminal.
Start Python REPL using Anaconda Python.
Run:
print(0)
print(0)
print(0)
input.title: VS Code Integrated Terminal Python REPL not showing stdout / output disappears

Enable the TypeScript native preview extension 6. Run the npm: watch marker task. Observe: you get one error in the problems view 7. Switch to the problems view 8. Open greet.ts: Observe: you get two errors in the problems view 9. Fix the code by putting export in front of the `foo` function. Observe: the error is still shown in the problems view and the code still shows the error as well The watch task doesn't report any errors

input.body: I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate. Related closed issues: #255934, #253126 - Copilot Chat Extension Version: Built-in (0.53.1) - VS Code Version: 1.125.1 (x64) - OS Version: Windows 11 - Feature: Ask mode - Selected model: Custom BYOK endpoint ΓÇö moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6 via chatLanguageModels.json - Logs: ``` 2026-06-24 09:02:16.869 [info] ccreq:ed060600.copilotmd | badRequest | moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6 | 1126ms | [copilotLanguageModelWrapper] 2026-06-24 09:02:16.953 [info] [ToolCallingLoop] Stop hook result: shouldContinue=false, reasons=undefined ``` Steps to Reproduce: 1. Configure a custom endpoint in chatLanguageModels.json with toolCalling: true pointing to a vLLM-backed server (identified via system_fingerprint: "vllm-0.22.1" in responses) 2. Send any prompt in VS Code chat Expected: Response content is displayed. Actual: Model reasoning steps appear in the UI but no response content is shown ΓÇö "Sorry, no response was returned." Root cause: vLLM emits "tool_calls": [] (empty array) on every content delta even when no tool call is being made. VS Code treats any tool_calls field presence as a tool invocation and discards the content, logging the request as badRequest. Confirmed by running a local proxy that strips empty tool_calls arrays ΓÇö with the proxy in place requests log as success and respond correctly. Backends returning null instead of [] work fine. Example offending SSE chunk from the server: ``` {"choices":[{"index":0,"delta":{"content":"Hello","tool_calls":[]},"finish_reason":null}]} ``` Previously reported in #255934 and #253126 (both closed). Suggested fix: Only process tool_calls deltas when the array is non-empty (tool_calls.length > 0).
input.title: Custom BYOK endpoint: "Sorry, no response was returned" when empty tool_calls: [] on content deltas

input.body:
When a bash script contains a numeric left shift e.g.

```sh
b=5
a=$(( b << 1 ))
```

then the Shell Script highlighting becomes messed up for all subsequent lines.

I think the highlighter wrongly assumes that `<<` begins a [heredoc](https://tldp.org/LDP/abs/html/here-docs.html), but it does not do so inside numeric calculation brackets `$(())`.
input.title: Bash script highlighting error when using bit-shift operator

input.body:
### Describe the bug
I am a paying GitHub Copilot Pro subscriber, and I am experiencing a critical issue where my chat history completely disappears after restarting my computer or closing VS Code. 

When I return to work the next day and click on the chat icon or look for previous conversations, the chat area is completely blank, and clicking on past sessions does nothing. No previous conversation is loaded or displayed.

### To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open VS Code and start a chat session with GitHub Copilot Chat.
2. Ask questions and get responses.
3. Shut down the computer (or close VS Code) and restart it later/the next day.
4. Open VS Code and open the Copilot Chat panel.
5. Click on the past chat history/sessions.
6. **Result:** Nothing happens. The conversation is gone, and the panel remains blank.

### Expected behavior
As a paid service, Copilot Chat should reliably persist and reload past conversations across VS Code restarts and system reboots, allowing users to resume their work context.

### Environment details
- OS: Windows 11
- VS Code Version: 1.126.0
input.title: Chat history disappears after system restart / VS Code close (Paid Copilot Pro User)

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
I often use multiple agents working on different parts of the code base. But I regularly lose track and find it difficult to context switch between them or my mind wanders onto other things whilst I'm waiting.
Essentially, a little more context than renaming tab name

I've often thought that a scratchpad style note pad feature allowing me to write small notes about what I'm planning with an agent, what to do next, things I want to ask it and so on.
I don't know if the agent needs to be aware of it, but it could be attached to the agents window.

-----------     ------------
| agent 1   |     | agent 2    |
----------     ------------
| scratch 1 |.  | scratch 2 |
------------  ------------
input.title: Agent adjacent scratchpad.

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

When resolving merge conflicts, Copilot inline completions often create visual and cognitive noise. During conflict resolution I typically want to focus on comparing and choosing changes rather than receiving code suggestions.

Please add a setting to automatically suppress Copilot inline completions when merge conflicts are active.

Suggested Behavior:

* Suppress inline completions in files containing unresolved conflict markers.
* Suppress inline completions in the Merge Editor / Conflict Resolution Editor.
* Restore normal behavior automatically once conflicts are resolved.

Possible setting names:

* `github.copilot.inlineCompletions.suppressDuringMergeConflicts`
* `github.copilot.inlineCompletions.disableInMergeEditor`

This would reduce distraction and help keep merge resolution focused on the task at hand.

(previously asked in Copilot Conversations in 2024 in https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/140818)
input.title: Add option to automatically suppress Copilot inline completions during merge conflict resolution

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
When using the copilot chat, it would be great to have a proxy setting to allow the chat requests to be sent to token compression tools like https://github.com/headroomlabs-ai/headroom

I watched #320867 but this request is more general.

But I realised copilot specific proxying for this use case hasn't been requested from searching.
input.title: Allow Copilot Chat to be proxied by endpoint.
