Open the Sessions view containing an agent/session with a pending approval. 2. Hover over the session row so the pending approval pop-over appears. 3. Move the mouse from the session row toward the approval controls in the pop-over, such as `Allow` or `Skip`. 4. Observe that the pop-over disappears before the button can be clicked. Actual Result: The approval pop-over is dismissed when the mouse leaves the session row, making it impossible or very difficult to click `Allow` or `Skip`. Expected Result: The pop-over should remain open while the pointer moves from the session row into the pop-over, so the user can click the approval controls. The pop-over should behave as hoverable/interactive content, not disappear before interaction. Additional Notes: This occurs in VS Code Insiders. Screenshot attached showing the pop-over with the `Allow` and `Skip` controls disappearing when attempting to move toward it.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the Copilot Chat extension in VS Code (with default settings).
2. Ask the model to output or paste a line containing repeated periods (for example: `.........`) or view model outputs that include such lines.

input.body:
## Problem

The Source Control **Changes** list can only be sorted by **Name**, **Path**, or **Status** (`scm.defaultViewSortKey`). There is no way to give the files in a resource group a **custom / arbitrary order**.

Internally, `SCMTreeSorter` always sorts resources within a group by one of those three keys and discards the extension-provided `resourceStates` array order. Two resource *groups* preserve their creation order (the comparator returns `0` when comparing two groups), but resources *within* a group are always force-sorted ΓÇö there is no opt-out, and no way for an extension or the built-in Git provider to supply a custom comparator or explicit order.

## What I want

I want the files in the Changes list to be displayed in a **custom order that I (or a tool) define** ΓÇö not alphabetical, not by path, not by status ΓÇö so the most important / highest-consequence changes appear at the top and I review them first.

Concrete motivating use case: I have an AI assistant rank a diff from **most ΓåÆ least consequential** (e.g. security rules and core data-layer changes first; tests, config, and docs last). I want that ranking to drive the order of the built-in Changes list so my review reads top-to-bottom in importance order. Today this only works in the terminal via `git diff -O<orderfile>` (`diff.orderFile`); the GUI Source Control view ignores it entirely.

## Proposed solutions (any one of these would solve it)

1. **Extension API** ΓÇö allow a `SourceControl` / `SourceControlResourceGroup` to provide a custom sort comparator, or an explicit ordering (e.g. a priority index per `SourceControlResourceState`), that the SCM view honors instead of `SCMTreeSorter`'s fixed keys. This is the most powerful option: it lets tooling (AI review assistants, linters, risk scanners) drive the order programmatically.

2. **New "Custom" sort mode** ΓÇö add `scm.defaultViewSortKey: "custom"` (or `"manual"`) that either:
   - preserves the provider's `resourceStates` array order, and/or
   - supports drag-to-reorder in the view, persisted in workspace storage.

3. **Honor `diff.orderFile` in the built-in Git provider** ΓÇö when git's `diff.orderFile` is configured, order the built-in Git **Changes** / **Staged Changes** lists to match, giving GUI/CLI parity. Simplest option; reuses an existing, well-understood Git mechanism.

Option 1 is the most powerful (lets tooling drive the order); option 3 is the simplest.

## Current behavior (verified on latest `main`)

- `scm.defaultViewSortKey` enum is still only `name | path | status` (default `path`) ΓÇö there is no custom/manual mode.
- `SCMTreeSorter.compare()` force-sorts resources within a group; it returns `0` for two groups, so groups keep their creation order but resources within a group never do.
- The `resourceStates` array order provided by an extension is not used for display.

## Prior art / why this isn't already covered

- **#141319** (Γ£à completed) ΓÇö added the sort-*mode* setting (`name`/`path`/`status`) + workspace persistence. This is mode *switching*, not custom ordering.
- **#127952** (Γ£à completed) ΓÇö made the chosen sort mode persist across restarts.
- **#146507** (Γ£à completed) ΓÇö repository ordering in a multi-root workspace (a different concern: ordering repos, not files within a group).
- **#235493** (Γ¥î closed as not planned) ΓÇö the closest prior "add a sort order option" request. This issue differs by asking for an **arbitrary/custom order** (ideally programmable via an extension API), not another fixed preset mode.

None of the completed work provides a way to define an arbitrary per-file order in the Changes list.
input.title: Allow a custom/arbitrary sort order for files in the Source Control 'Changes' list

input.body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

* VS Code Version: 1.125.1
* OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Start a local web app protected with HTTP Basic Authentication.
   Example: OpenCode web/server running on `http://127.0.0.1:4096` with password authentication enabled.

2. Open the same URL in a normal browser such as Chrome or Edge.

3. The browser correctly shows the HTTP Basic Auth username/password prompt and the app loads normally after entering the credentials.

4. Open the same URL inside VS Code using Integrated Browser or Simple Browser.

5. Instead of showing the HTTP Basic Auth prompt, the browser view renders a completely blank white page.

Expected behavior:

VS CodeΓÇÖs Integrated Browser / Simple Browser should display the HTTP Basic Auth prompt, or provide another way to enter the username and password.

Actual behavior:

The page stays blank white. No authentication prompt or input is shown, so the local web app cannot be used inside VS Code.

Additional information:

The issue does not happen in external browsers. It only happens inside VS CodeΓÇÖs browser view.

This makes VS CodeΓÇÖs internal browser unusable for localhost developer tools that are protected with HTTP Basic Authentication.

A workaround is to run a local proxy that injects the `Authorization` header before forwarding the request to the protected app, but this should not be necessary.
input.title: Integrated Browser shows blank page instead of HTTP Basic Auth prompt for localhost web apps

input.body:
* Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest
* VS Code Version: latest
* OS Version: latest
* Feature: Copilot Chat / existing chat session after VS Code restart
* Selected model: GPT 5.5 High 272k

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set Copilot Chat to use GPT 5.5 High with 272k context.
2. Start or continue a Copilot Chat session.
3. Restart VS Code.
4. Continue the existing Copilot Chat session.
5. Check the model/context shown in the Copilot UI.

## Expected Behavior

Copilot should respect the currently selected model/context setting after VS Code restart.

If I selected GPT 5.5 High 272k, continuing an existing session should keep using GPT 5.5 High 272k, unless I explicitly choose another model/context.

## Actual Behavior

After restarting VS Code and continuing an existing session, Copilot appears to ignore the selected setting and silently switches to the 1M context option.

This is especially serious because 1M is much more expensive. The switch is not explicitly confirmed by the user, but the user is still charged for the more expensive option.

## Impact

This caused unexpected billing charges for me. In one case, I paid around $10 because Copilot silently switched to the 1M option.

This makes Copilot unsafe to use with paid models/context tiers, because the UI setting is not reliably respected across VS Code restarts.

## Request

Please investigate why continuing an existing session after VS Code restart can override the user-selected model/context setting.

At minimum, Copilot should never silently switch to a more expensive model/context tier without explicit user confirmation.
input.title: Copilot Chat auto-switches to 1M context after VS Code restart, ignoring selected model setting

Extensions are not restored from Settings Sync. VS Code is in a contradictory authentication state. The Accounts menu shows my GitHub account as signed in, but the Settings Sync / Backup and Sync panel still says: ΓÇ£Please sign in to backup and sync your data across devices.ΓÇ¥ This prevents Settings Sync from restoring my extensions. Environment: OS: Ubuntu-based Linux, fresh installation VS Code path: /usr/bin/code VS Code version: 1.125.1 Commit: fcf604774b9f2674b473065736ee75077e256353 Architecture: x64 Authentication provider: GitHub Terminal checks: which code ΓåÆ /usr/bin/code code --version ΓåÆ 1.125.1 ΓåÆ fcf604774b9f2674b473065736ee75077e256353 ΓåÆ x64 xdg-mime query default x-scheme-handler/vscode ΓåÆ code-url-handler.desktop gnome-keyring, libsecret, libpam-gnome-keyring and seahorse are installed. Expected behavior: If VS Code shows my GitHub account as signed in, Settings Sync should either use that GitHub session or clearly explain why it cannot. Actual behavior: VS Code shows GitHub signed in, but Settings Sync still asks me to sign in and does not restore extensions. input.body:
When using Copilot Chat with a BYOK model on a Custom Endpoint that exposes an OpenAI-compatible /responses API, compaction appears to occur, but the next user turn still sends a large serialized local history payload instead of a compact continuation.

In contrast, I tested the same general workflow with chat-completions and the next-turn request shrank as expected after compaction.

The strongest symptom on the failing path is:
* the next request succeeds
* the next request includes a valid previous_response_id
* but the next request still contains a large serialized local history input

I have only reproduced this on the Custom Endpoint path so far. My Custom Endpoint is fronted by Bifrost or LiteLLM, but the oversized payload is already present in the request before any upstream rewriting, so this does not appear to be caused by the gateway.

# Environment
VS Code: Version: 1.125.1 (user setup)
Commit: fcf604774b9f2674b473065736ee75077e256353
Date: 2026-06-19T08:39:34Z
Electron: 42.2.0
ElectronBuildId: 14159160
Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
Node.js: 24.15.0
V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Failing path: BYOK Custom Endpoint using an OpenAI-compatible /responses API
Working comparison: BYOK chat-completions


# Observed Comparison
I tested two BYOK paths:

1. Custom Endpoint BYOK
* uses an OpenAI-compatible /responses endpoint
* in my setup this is fronted by Bifrost (custom to forward compaction/context_management params) or LiteLLM (custom to enable Azure compaction support)
* after compaction, the next request still carried a large serialized local history payload

2. BYOK with chat-completions
* after compaction from Custom Endpoint BYOK, the next request with Azure BYOK shrank as expected

So the behavior difference I am reporting is:
* Custom Endpoint BYOK /responses: next-turn request remains unexpectedly large
* BYOK chat-completions : next-turn request becomes compact as expected

I have not proven from source why the two paths differ, only that this difference is observable at runtime.

# Steps to Reproduce
1. Configure a BYOK Custom Endpoint that uses the Responses API (/responses).
2. Start a conversation long enough to trigger compaction / summarization.
3. Send the next user message in the same conversation.
4. Inspect the outgoing request body for that next turn.
5. Observe that:
* previous_response_id is present
* the request succeeds
* but the request still contains a large serialized local history payload

For comparison, repeat the same general workflow with BYOK chat-completions and observe that the next-turn request becomes smaller after compaction.

# Expected Behavior
After compaction, when a valid previous_response_id is available, the next request should send only the minimal continuation history required by the Responses API, plus any necessary compaction round-trip item.

That is the behavior I observed with chat-completions BYOK, and what I expected from the /responses BYOK path as well.

# Actual Behavior
On Custom Endpoint /responses BYOK, the next request includes previous_response_id, but also re-sends a large local serialized history blob.

# Why I think this is client-side
I captured the failing request at my upstream gateway. The large serialized local history was already present before the request was forwarded upstream. That rules out the gateway as the origin of the oversized payload.

I also reviewed the relevant VS Code source and found logic that appears intended to shrink history once continuation state is available:
```
if (webSocketStatefulMarker) {
    markerIndex = getIndexOfStatefulMarker(webSocketStatefulMarker, messages);
    if (markerIndex !== undefined) {
        previousResponseId = webSocketStatefulMarker;
    }
} else if (!ignoreStatefulMarker) {
    const statefulMarkerAndIndex = getStatefulMarkerAndIndex(modelId, messages);
    if (statefulMarkerAndIndex) {
        previousResponseId = statefulMarkerAndIndex.statefulMarker;
        markerIndex = statefulMarkerAndIndex.index;
    }
}

...

if (markerIndex !== undefined) {
    messages = messages.slice(markerIndex + 1);
    ...
} else if (latestCompactionMessageIndex !== undefined) {
    messages = messages.slice(latestCompactionMessageIndex);
}
```

That suggests the next request should shrink when continuation state is available.

# Sanitized Evidence
I am not attaching raw logs because they contain private conversation contents, local paths, shell history, endpoint/provider metadata, and request identifiers.

The relevant redacted shape of the failing Custom Endpoint request is:

```
{
  "status": "success",
  "params": {
    "previous_response_id": "resp_REDACTED",
    "store": true
  },
  "responses_input_history": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": [
        {
          "type": "input_text",
          "text": "<context> ...large serialized prior conversation / environment blob... </context>"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
The important part is:
* request succeeded
* previous_response_id was present
* a large serialized local history payload was still sent

# Related Issues
I found some related issues, but none that seem identical:
* #316747 BYOK Responses retry for previous_response_not_found re-sends previous_response_id
* #316576 customendpoint provider sends invalid item type in Responses input
* #318125 Responses API requests should include explicit message item types
* #321200 foreground compaction stores raw summary output incorrectly

This report seems different because the request here is successful and already carries previous_response_id, but the post-compaction next-turn payload still appears much larger than expected on the Custom Endpoint path.
input.title: Copilot Chat BYOK /responses appears to resend large local history after compaction

input.body:
After selecting a custom agent mode (WorktreeCoordinator, TestArchitect, ManualTester), Copilot Chat switches back to default Agent during an active conversation.

Environment
- OS: Windows
- VS Code: 1.126.0-insider
- GitHub Copilot extension: 0.54.2026061605

Steps to reproduce
1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code Insiders.
2. Select a custom agent mode (for example WorktreeCoordinator).
3. Start a longer task with multiple turns/tool calls.
4. Observe mode reverting to Agent mid-session.

Expected behaviour
Selected custom agent mode remains active unless manually changed.

Actual behaviour
Mode reverts to Agent unexpectedly during the session.

Regression
Started after updating VS Code Insiders/Copilot extension.

Impact
High workflow disruption; requires restarting chats to restore the intended persona.
input.title: Copilot Chat custom agent mode reverts to default Agent mid-session (Insiders)

input.body:
## Description

The VS Code Remote SSH server logs an `Unexpected SIGPIPE` error when a client disconnects gracefully. The error occurs at the moment the `ManagementConnection` reports a graceful disconnect, and the Extension Host Process exits immediately after.

## Log excerpt

```
2026-06-22 03:46:04.152 [info] [<unknown>][7dc6d1df][ManagementConnection] The client has disconnected gracefully, so the connection will be disposed.
2026-06-22 03:46:04.157 [error] Error: Unexpected SIGPIPE
    at process.<anonymous> (file:///.../server/out/server-main.js:607:1019)
    at process.emit (node:events:521:24)
2026-06-22 03:46:04.192 [info] [<unknown>][ed4d3dd7][ExtensionHostConnection] <5971> Extension Host Process exited with code: 0, signal: null.
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Connect to a remote host via Remote-SSH.
2. Work in the remote session for a few minutes.
3. Disconnect/close the VS Code window (graceful disconnect).
4. Check the remote server log at `~/.vscode-server/data/logs/<timestamp>/remoteagent.log`.

## Expected behavior

Graceful client disconnect should not produce an error. The SIGPIPE should be handled silently (or the write that causes it should be guarded).

## Actual behavior

An `Unexpected SIGPIPE` error is logged every time the client disconnects, even though the disconnect is graceful and the extension host exits cleanly (code 0).

## Environment

- VS Code: 1.123.0 (Stable, commit 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8)
- Remote SSH server: Linux x64
- Node.js: (bundled with VS Code server)
- Platform: Linux (RHEL 8.10)

## Analysis

The SIGPIPE likely occurs because the server attempts to write to the management connection's IPC channel after the client has already closed its end. On Linux, writing to a closed pipe/socket raises SIGPIPE. The error is caught by a generic `process` error handler that logs it as `Unexpected SIGPIPE`, but since the disconnect is graceful, this should be suppressed or handled explicitly.

The error is non-fatal (extension host exits cleanly with code 0), but it pollutes the logs on every disconnect and can be alarming to users reviewing server logs.

## Suggested fix

Ignore SIGPIPE during connection teardown, or check if the connection is still writable before writing. A `process.on('SIGPIPE', () => {})` handler or using `Stream#write` with error callbacks would prevent the uncaught error.
input.title: SIGPIPE error in Remote SSH Extension Host on client disconnect

input.body: The built-in Git Blame editor decoration allows customizing the template, but not the formatting of date values. For example: `{ "git.blame.editorDecoration.template": "${authorName} (${authorDate})" }` The value of ${authorDate} is generated using the runtime locale and formatting, which cannot be customized. Depending on the selected locale, this may produce undesirable output (for example, localized relative time strings or date formats that don't match the user's preference). ### Proposed solution Allow configuring the formatting of date variables independently from the template. ### Some possible approaches: Add settings such as: git.blame.dateFormat git.blame.relativeDateStyle Support formatting options in template variables, for example: ${authorDate:yyyy-MM-dd} ${authorDate:short} ${authorDateAgo:short} ### Why Many developers prefer a consistent date format regardless of the UI locale, or would like to choose between different relative date styles without changing the entire application locale. This would make the built-in Git Blame decoration significantly more flexible while keeping the existing template mechanism.
input.title: Allow customizing Git Blame date formatting

input.body:
## Summary

When a git repository is re-cloned (or undergoes a large checkout) while a VS Code window is open on that workspace, VS Code's built-in git extension captures a **transient, momentary state** where all files appear as deleted (`D`). The `git.statusLimit` guard then permanently sets `isRepositoryHuge = true`, which **freezes all subsequent `git status` refreshes** ΓÇö including manual "Refresh" from the Source Control panel ΓÇö until VS Code is restarted.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a large git repository (e.g., 10,000+ tracked files) in VS Code
2. From the integrated terminal, delete the repo directory and re-clone it (e.g., `rm -rf . && git clone <url> .`)
3. Wait for the clone to complete
4. Observe the Source Control panel ΓÇö many files show as `D` (deleted) even though they exist on disk
5. Click the "Refresh" button in Source Control ΓÇö the stale `D` decorations persist
6. Only restarting VS Code (or reloading the window) clears the stale state

## Expected Behavior

After the clone completes and files are present on disk, VS Code should re-run `git status` and clear the stale `D` decorations ΓÇö either automatically (once the transient state resolves) or at minimum when the user manually clicks "Refresh" in the Source Control panel.

## Actual Behavior

- During the clone, the file watcher fires and triggers `git status -z`
- At that instant, all tracked files are missing from the working tree ΓåÆ git reports them all as deleted
- `git.statusLimit` (default: 10,000) is hit, so only the first 10,000 entries are captured (early-alphabetical files like `.config`, `Jenkinsfile`, `README`, etc.)
- The `didHitLimit` flag sets `isRepositoryHuge = true`
- From that point on, **all** file-watch events and manual refresh attempts are short-circuited by the huge-repository guard
- Stale `D` state is frozen until the extension host restarts

## Evidence from logs

The VS Code remote server log shows the file watcher shutting down during the re-clone:

```
[warning] [File Watcher (node.js)] Watcher shutdown because watched path got deleted
[warning] [File Watcher (node.js)] Watcher shutdown because watched path got deleted
[warning] [File Watcher (node.js)] Watcher shutdown because watched path got deleted
```

## Root Cause

The `isRepositoryHuge` flag was designed to protect against repositories that *persistently* have an enormous number of changes (e.g., Linux kernel). However, it gets permanently set from a **transient spike** during clone/checkout ΓÇö when the working tree is momentarily empty. Once set, there is **no recovery path**:

- Γ¥î Manual "Refresh" in Source Control ΓåÆ ignored (guard short-circuits)
- Γ¥î Subsequent file events ΓåÆ ignored
- Γ¥î Only a full window reload / extension host restart clears it

## Suggested Fixes

Any of the following would resolve the issue:

1. **Re-evaluate `isRepositoryHuge`**: On the next `git status` call, if the result count is below the limit, reset `isRepositoryHuge = false` so automatic refresh resumes.
2. **Allow manual "Refresh" to bypass the guard**: The user explicitly clicking "Refresh" should always trigger a fresh `git status`, regardless of the huge-repo flag.
3. **Detect transient clone state**: If `.git/index.lock` exists or `.git/` is rapidly changing, suppress `git status` until the operation settles ΓÇö rather than capturing a snapshot of an incomplete working tree.
4. **Increase default `git.statusLimit`** or make the limit configurable per-repository.

## Environment

- VS Code: 1.123.0 (commit 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8)
- OS: RHEL 8.10 (Remote - SSH)
- Git: 2.x (shallow clone, `--depth 1`)
- Repository size: ~15 GB, ~74,000 tracked files, 150+ submodules

## Workaround

Restart VS Code or reload the window (`Developer: Reload Window`). Alternatively, increase `git.statusLimit` in settings:

```json
"git.statusLimit": 100000
```

Or close VS Code before performing large re-clones and reopen after completion.
input.title: Git: `isRepositoryHuge` flag set during transient clone/checkout permanently freezes stale git status with no recovery short of restart

Type: Feature Request ## Feature Request ### Problem I occasionally trigger the "Close All Editors" command (Ctrl+K, W) by mistake. When this happens, all open editor tabs are closed immediately without any confirmation prompt. While tabs can often be restored, it can be disruptive, especially when working with many open files. ### Proposed Solution Add an optional setting that displays a confirmation dialog before executing the "Close All Editors" command. Example: "Are you sure you want to close all open editors?" * Close All * Cancel ### Suggested Setting ```json "workbench.editor.confirmCloseAllEditors": true ``` The setting could be disabled by default to preserve the current behavior and enabled by users who prefer additional protection against accidental tab closure. ### Benefits * Prevents accidental closure of all editor tabs. * Improves usability for users who frequently use Ctrl+K shortcuts. * Provides consistency with other confirmation dialogs available in VS Code. * Optional setting ensures no impact on users who prefer the current workflow. ### Additional Context This is particularly useful when working with many open files across multiple editor groups, where accidentally triggering "Close All Editors" can interrupt the current workflow. Type: Bug The following `files.exclude` pattern does not work correctly: ``` "files.exclude": { "Source[^M/]*": true } ``` Even the documented `!` syntax is not working. "Source"-folder is displayed and can be expanded, but remains empty. A small test-repo can be found here: https://github.com/bwsnico/issue-glob-pattern Maybe a hint: On Windows the test-Repo is working fine if the pattern is changed to: ``` "files.exclude": { "Source[^M\\/]*": true } ``` Thanks and regards, Nico H. Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/8095

- [x] anyOS @ulugbekna
- [x] anyOS @dmitrivMS

Complexity: 3

Authors: @digitarald


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322330%0A%0A&assignees=digitarald)

---

## Context

Copilot PMF surveys now open as a native VS Code editor pane instead of sending users to an external survey URL. The first migrated survey requires only the score question and submits any optional fixed-choice answers through structured telemetry.

## Test Steps

1. Open a Code OSS development build.
2. Run `Developer: Open Survey` from the Command Palette.
	- Verify a survey opens in an editor tab.
	- Verify no external browser window opens.
3. Try to submit the survey before answering the first score question.
	- Verify submission is blocked.
4. Answer the first score question.
	- Verify the survey now has enough required information to submit.
5. Answer some optional questions and leave at least one optional question unanswered.
	- Verify optional answers can be selected and changed.
	- Verify unanswered optional questions do not show as required.
6. Navigate the survey using only the keyboard.
	- Use Tab to move between question groups.
	- Use arrow keys to change radio options.
	- Verify focus is visible and selection changes are clear.
7. Press `Alt+F1` while focus is in the survey.
	- Verify accessibility help opens with survey navigation guidance.
	- Close the help and verify focus returns to the survey.
8. Resize the editor group to a narrow width.
	- Verify the segmented choices stack cleanly.
	- Verify list options collapse to one column.
	- Verify the submit button remains reachable.
9. Submit the survey.
	- Verify a success state appears.
	- Verify the survey editor closes automatically after about 5 seconds.
10. Reopen the survey with `Developer: Open Survey`.
	- Verify the same editor-style survey opens again and remains usable.
input.title: Test: In-editor Copilot PMF survey

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒò« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ≡ƒöÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ≡ƒº¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒÆí Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ≡ƒöº Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ≡ƒ¬ô If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ≡ƒôú Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.124.2
- OS Version: Ubuntu 26.04 kernel 7.0.0 (compiled from source).

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Find a example C program that demonstrates the use of argp (there are lots around).
2. Using gdb from a command line, debug that app using the "--args" flag plus various args for the app.
   for example  **/usr/bin/gdb --args ./testapp --device=/dev/xyz --syslog --ver=9 --show --quiet --config=/etc/xyz.conf**
2. Now try debugging that using Visual Code, without using the launch setting "miDebuggerArgs".
   You will find the test app won't process its args corrrectly.
4. Now try debugging again but using the launch setting "miDebuggerArgs": "--args".
   You will find the test app still won't process its args correctly.

The problem (this took me an entire day to figure out because the problem wasn't in my test app at all!).
(The following example outputs are from my test app using the different ways of launching gdb described above) .

Debugging my test app using gdb from the command line works perfectly because the "--args" option appears just before the target executable, and so gdb passes all the following arguments to the target main().
**ARGP_KEY_INIT
parse_opt() key:64 char:d  arg:/dev/xyz
parse_opt() key:6C char:l  arg:(null)
parse_opt() key:76 char:v  arg:9
parse_opt() key:73 char:s  arg:(null)
parse_opt() key:71 char:q  arg:(null)
parse_opt() key:63 char:c  arg:/etc/xyz.conf
ARGP_KEY_NO_ARGS
ARGP_KEY_END
ARGP_KEY_SUCCESS
ARGP_KEY_FINI
argp_parse() done**
The above is the expected output with all app arguments correctly processed.

However using Visual Code debugging without the  "miDebuggerArgs": "--args" launch property  -
**ARGP_KEY_INIT
parse_opt() key:64 char:d  arg:/dev/xyz --syslog --verbose=9 --showkey --quiet --config=/etc/xyz.conf
ARGP_KEY_NO_ARGS
ARGP_KEY_END
ARGP_KEY_SUCCESS
ARGP_KEY_FINI
argp_parse() done**
This is incorrect. The app only processed a single argument that was the entire argument string given to the app.

And using Visual Code debugging with the "miDebuggerArgs": "--args" launch property  -
**ARGP_KEY_INIT
parse_opt() key:64 char:d  arg:/dev/xyz --syslog --verbose=9 --showkey --quiet --config=/etc/xyz.conf
ARGP_KEY_NO_ARGS
ARGP_KEY_END
ARGP_KEY_SUCCESS
ARGP_KEY_FINI
argp_parse() done**
This is still incorrect, adding the "--arg" option via  the "miDebuggerArgs" launch property didn't do anything as it was not placed in the correct position when invoking gdb.
I get this in my Visual Code terminal window after a run using the "--arg" property -
**[1] + Done                       "/usr/bin/gdb" --interpreter=mi --args --tty=${DbgTerm} 0<"/tmp/Microsoft-MIEngine-In-u5ai3hek.04x" 1>"/tmp/Microsoft-MIEngine-Out-lot3j3jx.fqc"**

Perhaps Visual Code should have placed "--args" after the "--tty" ????
input.title: Incorrect position of "--args" gdb argument prevents correct processing of target program arguments.

<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒò« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ≡ƒöÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ≡ƒº¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒÆí Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ≡ƒöº Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ≡ƒ¬ô If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ≡ƒôú Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
  Environment:
  - OS: Windows 10 22H2 (19045)
  - VS Code version: 1.125.1 (happens every update, across multiple versions)
  - Install type: System Installer, custom path E:\Program Files\ide\Microsoft VS Code

  Steps to reproduce:
  1. Pin VS Code to the Start menu (tile area). The tile displays correctly.
  2. Wait for an auto-update, or trigger one via "Check for Updates".
  3. After the update completes, open the Start menu.

  Observed behavior:
  The pinned VS Code tile becomes completely invisible/transparent (not a white square ΓÇö
  truly empty, nothing renders). The shortcut itself is intact, Code.exe exists at the
  correct path, and clicking the invisible tile still launches VS Code normally.

  Workaround that works every time:
  Right-click the blank tile ΓåÆ "Unpin from Start" ΓåÆ re-pin from Code.exe. Or, right-click
  the .lnk shortcut ΓåÆ Properties ΓåÆ Shortcut tab ΓåÆ "Change Icon..." ΓåÆ OK ΓåÆ Apply (without
  actually changing the icon). Both restore the tile immediately.

  What has been ruled out:
  - Γ¥î Shortcut target path is NOT broken ΓÇö TargetPath still points to correct Code.exe
  and IconLocation is Code.exe,0
  - Γ¥î No _ (underscore) subfolder issue ΓÇö Code.exe is directly in install directory, not
  moved
  - Γ¥î Icon cache corruption ΓÇö ie4uinit.exe -show alone doesn't always fix it; restarting
  StartMenuExperienceHost.exe does
  - Γ¥î Not specific to custom install path ΓÇö the Code.VisualElementsManifest.xml and its
  referenced hash-named PNG folder are both intact post-update



  Possible root cause (AI-generated analysis):
  The root cause appears to be that VS Code's Inno Setup-based updater replaces Code.exe
  in-place during the update but does not call SHChangeNotify(SHCNE_ASSOCCHANGED, ...) or
  ie4uinit.exe -ClearIconCache after the file replacement. Windows' Start menu tile host
  process (StartMenuExperienceHost.exe) holds a cached rendering of the old Code.exe's
  icon resources in memory. Because the file path hasn't changed, Windows does not
  automatically invalidate the cache, and the stale cached data fails to map to the new
  binary's icon resources ΓÇö resulting in the tile rendering nothing (invisible rather than
  a white fallback).

  Additionally, Code.VisualElementsManifest.xml references icon PNGs via a hash-named
  subfolder (e.g. <hash>\resources\app\resources\win32\code_150x150.png). Even though the
  manifest is updated to the new hash during installation, if StartMenuExperienceHost.exe
  has cached the manifest content from before the update, it may still reference the old
  (now-deleted) hash folder path. This would also cause a transparent tile.

  Suggested fix:
  In the Inno Setup installer script, add a post-install step:

  [Code]
  procedure CurStepChanged(CurStep: TSetupStep);
  begin
    if CurStep = ssPostInstall then
      SHChangeNotify(SHCNE_ASSOCCHANGED, SHCNF_IDLIST, 0, 0);
  end;

  Or in the [Run] section:

  [Run]
  Filename: "{cmd}"; Parameters: "/c ie4uinit.exe -ClearIconCache"; Flags: runhidden
input.title: Start Menu pinned tile becomes invisible (transparent) after every auto-update on   Windows

input.body:
## Feature request

Please add a way to keep Git worktree entries collapsed by default in the Source Control Repositories view.

## Current behavior

When a workspace has Git worktrees detected, the Source Control Repositories view shows them under the parent repository. Even if the repository nodes are manually collapsed, opening the Source Control sidebar again can expand the parent repository and reveal the worktree entries.

This makes the Repositories view noisy in multi-root workspaces where each root repository has many worktrees.

## Expected behavior

There should be a way to keep worktree entries collapsed by default while still showing the parent repositories.

For example, one of these would work:

- A setting that makes Git worktree entries default collapsed in the Source Control Repositories view
- A setting that makes repository nodes with worktrees default collapsed
- Preserving the collapsed state of repository nodes when reopening the Source Control sidebar

## Why existing settings do not solve this

`git.detectWorktrees: false` hides worktrees entirely, which is not the desired behavior. I still want worktrees to be available in the Repositories view, just not expanded by default.

`scm.autoReveal: false` does not appear to prevent the repository node from reopening in this case.

I also observed that the workspace storage state can contain an empty expanded list, for example:

```json
{"expanded":[]}
```

but reopening the Source Control sidebar can still show the worktree entries expanded.

## Use case

In a workspace with several repositories and many worktrees, the desired default view is compact:

```text
Repositories
  > app
  > website
  > api
  > tools
```

The worktrees should still be accessible by expanding a repository, but they should not make the top-level Repositories view tall every time the Source Control sidebar is opened.
input.title: Allow Git worktree entries in Source Control Repositories view to default collapsed

var
   contador, soma, nota, media: inteiro
   i: inteiro

inicio

contador <- 0
soma <- 0

para i de 1 ate 10 passo 1 faca
   leia(nota)

   soma <- soma + nota

fimpara

media <- soma / 10

escreval("Quantidade de aprovados: ", contador)
escreval("Media: ", media)

fimalgoritmo

input.body:
## Impact

A background agent session in the Agents window can stay showing as **running** in the session list indefinitely, even though the agent has finished its work and gone idle. The badge never clears, and in the affected case it stayed stuck across multiple subsequent messages. This is misleading ΓÇö the user can't tell which sessions are actually still working, and a "completed" session looks like it's hung.

## What's happening

When a session goes idle, the final status/summary updates that should flip the badge from **running ΓåÆ idle** are dispatched against a session that has already been **evicted from the agent host state manager** (`AgentHostStateManager._sessionStates`). The state manager doesn't recognize the session anymore, so it **drops the update** and logs a warning. The badge is left at whatever it last showed ΓÇö `running`.

This is visible directly in the logs. The moment the agent finishes (`Session idle`), the very next status/meta updates are rejected as belonging to an "unknown session":

```
2026-06-20 10:26:43.633 [info] [Copilot:e67a002f-192f-4835-98e3-828d13d824ef] Session idle
2026-06-20 10:26:43.636 [info] [Copilot] Listing sessions...
2026-06-20 10:26:44.302 [warning] [AgentHostStateManager] Action for unknown session: copilotcli:/e67a002f-192f-4835-98e3-828d13d824ef, type=session/metaChanged
2026-06-20 10:26:45.792 [warning] [AgentHostStateManager] setSessionSummaryChanges: unknown session copilotcli:/e67a002f-192f-4835-98e3-828d13d824ef
```

The `session/metaChanged` and `setSessionSummaryChanges` actions that carry the post-turn state are the ones being dropped, so the session summary (which drives the list badge) is never updated to `idle`.

The same pattern keeps repeating afterwards for the affected session every time something tries to refresh its summary, which is why the badge never recovers on its own.

## Who is pushing the dropped updates

In a later trace-level capture, the producer is clearly the changeset service. It periodically recomputes git/branch changes for sessions and pushes a summary update keyed by the chat-channel URI (`ahp-chat://default/<base64>`) ΓÇö but the underlying session has already been evicted, so the update is dropped as "unknown session":

```
2026-06-21 15:22:54.297 [debug] [AgentHostChangesetService] Using _meta.git base branch fallback for Branch Changes in ahp-chat://default/Y29waWxvdGNsaTovMjU5NTEwNjItZjlhZi00NDM0LWExMWYtMzg5M2EyNDI4ZmFh: main
2026-06-21 15:22:54.307 [warning] [AgentHostStateManager] setSessionSummaryChanges: unknown session ahp-chat://default/Y29waWxvdGNsaTovMjU5NTEwNjItZjlhZi00NDM0LWExMWYtMzg5M2EyNDI4ZmFh
2026-06-21 15:22:54.307 [warning] [AgentHostStateManager] setSessionSummaryChanges: unknown session ahp-chat://default/Y29waWxvdGNsaTovZDk4NDI5MmItNzllNy00MGI3LWEzN2ItYzRiYjA2NjM1YzRk
```

(The two base64 values decode to `copilotcli:/25951062-ΓÇª` and `copilotcli:/d984292b-ΓÇª` ΓÇö two different background sessions, so this is not specific to one session.)

## Reproduction

I have not been able to capture the exact failure transition (see caveat below), but based on the logs the trigger is a background session being evicted while idle. Best-known repro:

1. Run several agent sessions in the Agents window so some are in the background (not the focused/subscribed session).
2. Let a background session finish its work and go idle while it is **not** the focused session (so it is unsubscribed and eligible for idle eviction).
3. Observe the session in the list: it can remain showing **running** even though the agent has completed.

In the capture the session was evicted from in-memory state (`_maybeEvictIdleSession` ΓåÆ `removeSession`) while idle; once evicted, the end-of-turn status update lands on an unknown session and is dropped.

## Caveat: the original transition was hard to capture

In a second capture taken specifically to catch this, the window started **after** the session was already stuck. Opening the stuck session and sending it a new message just **restored** it into state and ran new turns:

```
2026-06-21 15:22:28.092 [info] [AgentService] Restored session copilotcli:/e67a002f-192f-4835-98e3-828d13d824ef with 7 turns
```

So that capture shows the recovery, not the failure. To catch the original transition you need logging running **while the session is still in the background going idle/evicted**, before it is touched again.

---

## Analysis / where to look (not a prescribed fix)

There appears to be a **key-form inconsistency** around eviction and summary updates that is worth investigating:

- `AgentHostStateManager._sessionStates` is keyed by the **raw session URI** (`copilotcli:/ΓÇª`).
- `getSessionState()` normalizes a **chat-channel URI** (`ahp-chat://default/<base64>`) down to the session URI before lookup, so it accepts both forms.
- `removeSession()` does a **raw lookup with no normalization**, so it only accepts the session-URI form.
- `AgentHostChangesetService` pushes summary updates keyed by the **chat-channel URI**.

The eviction path can be invoked with either form. In the trace capture the same session was "evicted" twice with different keys ΓÇö once with a chat-channel key that produced **no** removal, and once with a session key that did:

```
2026-06-21 15:22:27.347 [trace] [AgentService] Evicting idle session: ahp-chat://default/Y29waWxvdGNsaTovNDJlZjk4NDQtYmE0Yi00OWQwLWE0YmYtMzY3NDdhZjhlMmJi (triggered by unsubscribe of ahp-chat://default/Y29waWxvdGNsaTovNDJlZjk4NDQtYmE0Yi00OWQwLWE0YmYtMzY3NDdhZjhlMmJi)
2026-06-21 15:22:44.609 [trace] [AgentService] Evicting idle session: copilotcli:/42ef9844-ba4b-49d0-a4bf-36747af8e2bb (triggered by unsubscribe of copilotcli:/42ef9844-ba4b-49d0-a4bf-36747af8e2bb)
2026-06-21 15:22:44.609 [trace] [AgentHostStateManager] Removed session: copilotcli:/42ef9844-ba4b-49d0-a4bf-36747af8e2bb
```

Two directions worth considering when fixing:
- A producer (e.g. `AgentHostChangesetService`) should not keep computing and pushing summaries for a session that has been evicted ΓÇö or a summary update for an unknown-but-known-on-disk session should re-register/restore it rather than being silently dropped.
- The eviction/removal and lookup paths should normalize chat-channel vs session URIs consistently so eviction can't half-apply.

Relevant code: `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentHostStateManager.ts` (`getSessionState`, `removeSession`, `setSessionSummaryChanges`, the "Action for unknown session" path), `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/agentService.ts` (`_maybeEvictIdleSession`), and `AgentHostChangesetService`.

Note: PR microsoft/vscode#322309 promotes the idle-eviction log to `info` and adds a turn-start desync log to make the next capture of this easier to read; it does not fix the underlying drop.

_(Written by Copilot)_
input.title: Agent session stuck showing "running" in the UI after the agent has finished

input.title: Claude not working

input.body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: [Please fill from the Extensions tab, e.g., v0.23.0]
- VS Code Version: [Please fill from VS Code -> About, e.g., 1.90.1]
- OS Version: macOS (MacBook Air)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Custom Language Model Integration (chatLanguageModels.json)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Custom Endpoint (minimaxai/minimax-m3 via NVIDIA NIM)
- Logs: See below

### Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure a custom endpoint in `chatLanguageModels.json` with `"toolCalling": true` and `"maxInputTokens": 16000` (or 8000 to match typical trial API constraints).
2. Install any agent extensions that register multiple tools/schemas (e.g., extensions that register dozens of custom tools, bloating the prompt payload).
3. Open Copilot Chat, select the custom model, and send a message.
4. The client-side token-pruning algorithm crashes with a fatal exception.

---

### Description and Root Cause

I am configuring a custom language model (`minimaxai/minimax-m3` on NVIDIA NIM) using the `chatLanguageModels.json` configuration in VS Code.

When using the model with `"toolCalling": true`, the extension crashes locally with the following error:
`Error: No lowest priority node found (path: Xre) at eEt (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:173:4513)`

**Detailed Root Cause:**
1. **Excessive Tool Injection:** VS Code automatically injects the JSON schemas of all available agent/extension tools (in my case, over 70 tools totaling ~86KB) into the payload.
2. **Pruning Algorithm Failure:** When trying to compress the prompt payload to fit a lower `"maxInputTokens"` constraint (such as 8000 or 16000) to respect the custom API limits, the token-pruning algorithm fails to safely discard low-priority nodes and throws a fatal client-side exception: `No lowest priority node found`.
3. **UI Selection Disappearance:** If I try to work around this by setting `"toolCalling": false` in `chatLanguageModels.json`, the custom model completely disappears from the Copilot Chat model selection dropdown UI, making it impossible to use without tool injection.

---

### Call Stack / Error Logs:

```text
Error: No lowest priority node found (path: Xre)
    at eEt (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:173:4513)
    at n.removeLowestPriorityChild (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:171:10945)
    at on._getFinalElementTree (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:174:30965)
    at async on.renderRaw (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:174:29509)
    at async on.render (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:174:29172)
    at async on.render (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1686:16979)
    at async FG._provideLanguageModelResponse (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:11644)
    at async FG.provideLanguageModelResponse (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:15097)
```

---

### Related Bug: Plain-text `apiKey` silently ignored in configuration

During troubleshooting, we also found that if an API key is provided as a plain string in `chatLanguageModels.json`:
`"apiKey": "nvapi-SK1VhLO..."`

VS Code does not raise a configuration error. Instead, it silently drops/ignores the key and sends the HTTP request to the custom endpoint with **no `Authorization` header at all**, leading to authorization failures on the receiving server. 

The header is only populated if we force VS Code to use the secret store reference syntax:
`"apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.nvidia-api-key}"`

---

### Expected Behavior
1. **Tool Control**: Setting `"toolCalling": false` in `chatLanguageModels.json` should successfully disable tool schema injection, but the model must remain selectable in the UI dropdown.
2. **Robust Pruning**: The token-pruning engine should degrade gracefully (e.g., truncation or dropping systemic messages) without raising fatal runtime exceptions when payload constraints are tight.
3. **Consistent Auth**: VS Code should support plain-text API keys in `chatLanguageModels.json` as fallback, or at least throw a validation warning/error instead of silently omitting the Authorization header in the outgoing HTTP requests.
input.title: Bug: Custom endpoint crashes with "No lowest priority node found" under context limits and silently ignores plain-text apiKey

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ≡ƒò« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ≡ƒöÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ≡ƒº¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->

## Summary

Clicking the model picker button in VS Code Chat throws `TypeError: a.charAt is not a function` and the dropdown never appears. The crash happens in `getCategoryLabel()` in `chatModelPicker.ts` when it receives a non-string `category` value (including `null` or omitted). The function only guards against `undefined` and `''` in the switch, then unconditionally calls `.charAt(0)` in the default branch.

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **No**

This is important: the crash is in VS Code's code, not an extension. With all extensions disabled, only Copilot's built-in models register, and those always provide string categories like `'lightweight'`, so the bug doesn't manifest. But the picker still crashes on any third-party provider that omits `category` or passes `null`, which is valid per the API spec (`category?: string`).

I filed this here (not on the extension publisher) because the root defect is the unguarded `.charAt` call in VS Code's picker code. The extension already shipped a workaround in v2.1.6, but the upstream bug means the next extension that doesn't guard `category` will hit the exact same crash.

## Version

- VS Code Version: **Insiders 1.126** (bug introduced here; **Stable 1.125 not affected** ΓÇö same provider, same account, same quota state, picker works fine there)
- OS Version: **macOS 26.5**
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: built-in (bundled with VS Code)
- Feature: model picker (clicking the model picker button in the Chat input)
- Selected model: N/A ΓÇö crash happens before any model is selected

## Extensions

The bug requires a BYOK provider registered via `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` to trigger. I have one extension installed: `gethnet.litellm-connector-copilot` (LiteLLM-backed OpenAI-compatible proxy, vendor: `litellm-connector`). With that extension disabled (or all extensions disabled), the picker opens normally because the only models registered are Copilot's own, which all carry a known string `category`.

Extension Bisect was not used because the crash is in VS Code's code, not the extension's. The extension simply provides a `LanguageModelChatInformation` entry whose `category` field was set to a grouping object `{ label: <string>, order: <number> }` ΓÇö a non-string value. VS Code's picker iterates all registered models, hits `getCategoryLabel` with that value, and throws.

## Steps to Reproduce

The bug appears when:
1. A language model provider (built-in or third-party) registers a model via `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` where `LanguageModelChatInformation.category` is not a string (e.g., omitted, `null`, or an object).
2. You click the model picker button in the Chat input (or invoke `workbench.action.chat.openModelPicker`).

**Why quota exhaustion in the original report:** I discovered this with Copilot's quota exhausted because that's when the picker starts showing "Other Models" (third-party vendors). On an active Copilot subscription, the only models listed are Copilot's own, which all use valid string categories, so the bad code path never runs. The bug exists regardless of quota stateΓÇöit's just more visible (and discoverable) once Copilot models are unavailable.

## Expected Behavior

The picker dropdown should appear and list all registered models. If `category` is non-string, missing, `null`, or empty, the picker should treat it the same way it already handles `undefined` and `''` ΓÇö omit the category tag and move on.

## Actual Behavior

The dropdown does not appear. No error toast, no log channel entry. The console records one `TypeError: a.charAt is not a function` per click, one per registered model.

## Console Output

Three identical traces on three consecutive clicks:

```
chatModelPicker.ts:331 Uncaught TypeError: a.charAt is not a function
    at TSn (chatModelPicker.ts:331:1)
    at L0i (chatModelPicker.ts:1547:1)
    at fbe (chatModelPicker.ts:223:1)
    at RSn (chatModelPicker.ts:612:1)
    at IWe.show (chatModelPicker.ts:1146:1)
    at chatModelPicker.ts:1065:1
    at HTMLAnchorElement.<anonymous> (chatModelPicker.ts:1085:1)
getCategoryLabel     @ chatModelPicker.ts:331
getModelHoverContent @ chatModelPicker.ts:1547
createModelItem      @ chatModelPicker.ts:223
buildModelPickerItems @ chatModelPicker.ts:612
show                 @ chatModelPicker.ts:1146
(anonymous)          @ chatModelPicker.ts:1065
(anonymous)          @ chatModelPicker.ts:1085
```

Quota state in the same session:

```
log.ts:117 ERR You've reached your monthly credit limit. Upgrade to Copilot Pro or wait until your credits reset on June 30, 2026 at 7:00 PM.
    ChatQuotaExceeded: You've reached your monthly credit limit.
        at OG._provideLanguageModelResponse (.../copilot/dist/extension.js:1690:14186)
```

## Root Cause

In `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelPicker.ts`, the function `getCategoryLabel` has a type signature that says `category: string | undefined`, but the code doesn't match:

```ts
function getCategoryLabel(category: string | undefined): string | undefined {
    switch (category) {
        case undefined:
        case '':
            return undefined;
        case 'lightweight': return localize(...);
        case 'versatile':   return localize(...);
        case 'powerful':    return localize(...);
        default:
            return category.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + category.slice(1);  // ΓåÉ crashes here
    }
}
```

The problem: JavaScript `switch` uses **strict equality**, so `case undefined` only matches `undefined`, not `null`. If `category` is `null`, an object, a number, or any other non-string value, it falls through to `default:` where `.charAt(0)` is called unconditionally. That throws.

The public API for `LanguageModelChatInformation` declares `category?: string`, meaning providers are allowed to omit it entirely or pass `null`. VS Code should handle that gracefully, but instead one bad model aborts the entire picker dropdown.

The same `.charAt` pattern appears in two other functions in the same file:
- `getPriceCategoryLabel`
- `getVendorDisplayName`

Both have the same unguarded fallback that will throw on non-string input.

## Suggested Fix

Add a `typeof` guard before the switch, treating non-strings the same way undefined and empty string are already handled:

```ts
function getCategoryLabel(category: string | undefined): string | undefined {
    if (typeof category !== 'string' || category.length === 0) {
        return undefined;
    }
    switch (category) {
        case 'lightweight': return localize('chat.category.lightweight', "Lightweight");
        case 'versatile':   return localize('chat.category.versatile',   "Versatile");
        case 'powerful':    return localize('chat.category.powerful',    "Powerful");
        default:
            return category.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + category.slice(1);
    }
}
```

Apply the same guard to `getPriceCategoryLabel` and `getVendorDisplayName` in the same file. This is behavior-preserving: existing translations stay the same, and any non-string category just gets omitted from the UI instead of crashing.

Optionally (for better diagnostics): add a `logService.warn` in the guard so silent picker crashes show up in the Log (Window) output instead of being invisible outside DevTools.

## Additional Context

**Silent failure.** The crash is invisible in the UI ΓÇö no error toast, no log channel entry, no status bar hint. Only DevTools console shows the exception. This makes it hard for users to diagnose why the picker won't open.

**Why it's rare today.** Most users see Copilot models first, which all use correct string categories, so the bug path doesn't run. But the picker should be defensive against *any* model with a missing or malformed `category`, not just happen to work because Copilot's descriptors are well-formed.

**Why this is VS Code's responsibility, not the extension's.** The API spec for `LanguageModelChatInformation` declares `category?: string` ΓÇö it's optional, and it's optional for a reason (not every provider needs or wants to categorize their models). Code that consumes optional fields should always guard against their absence. VS Code's picker iterates and renders models from multiple vendors; it's responsible for handling any value an extension can legally supply under the API contract. This isn't about a misconfigured extensionΓÇöit's about defensive programming in code that has to work with many different providers.

**Our workaround.** The LiteLLM Connector extension (v2.1.6, already shipped) fixed its side by ensuring `category` is always a valid string. This issue exists to harden the picker itself so future extensions don't need the same workaround.
input.title: Chat model picker crashes with 'a.charAt is not a function'

input.body:
- [x] anyOS @aiday-mar
- [x] anyOS @benvillalobos

Complexity: 4

Authors: @sandy081


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322285%0A%0A&assignees=sandy081)

---

An Agent Host **Copilot CLI** session in the **Agents window** can now hold **multiple chats** ("peer" chats) sideΓÇæbyΓÇæside, each with its own conversation, title and status. Peer chats are now **persisted and restored across a window reload**, and deleting a chat asks for **confirmation** first.

This test covers creating, switching, renaming, deleting and restoring multiple chats within a single Copilot CLI session.

## Set up

1. Open the **Agents window**
2. This test is specifically about a **Copilot CLI session created from the Local Agent Host** (the builtΓÇæin, locallyΓÇærunning Copilot CLI agent host ΓÇö **not** a Copilot Chat session, and **not** a remote/tunnel agent host). Make sure you are signed in to Copilot and the local Copilot CLI agent host is available.
3. Start a **new session** and, in the sessionΓÇætype picker, choose **Copilot CLI** (the entry provided by the Local Agent Host). MultiΓÇæchat is only enabled for these **Copilot CLI** sessions ΓÇö other session types intentionally show a single chat.
4. Send a first message so the session is created (no longer "untitled").

## Test steps

### 1. Create additional (peer) chats
- With the **Local Agent Host** Copilot CLI session active, click the **New Chat** (`+`) button in the session toolbar.
- Γ£à A new chat **tab** appears in the chat bar next to the existing one and becomes active, showing an empty/newΓÇæchat input.
- Send a message in the new chat and confirm it gets its own response, independent of the first chat.
- Add a second peer chat the same way.
- Γ£à The session now shows 3 tabs, each with independent conversations.

### 2. Switch between chats
- Click each tab in turn.
- Γ£à Switching shows that chat's own request/response history immediately (no perpetual loading spinner), and the input/scroll position is perΓÇæchat.
- Γ£à The inΓÇæprogress spinner / unread indicator on a tab reflects only that chat's state (e.g. start a long request in one chat, switch away ΓÇö only that tab shows the spinner).

### 3. Rename a chat tab (independent of the session)
- DoubleΓÇæclick a chat tab (or rightΓÇæclick the tab ΓåÆ **Rename**), type a new name, press **Enter**.
- Γ£à Only that tab's title changes. The **session title** (in the sessions list / header) is unchanged.
- Press **Escape** while editing ΓåÆ rename is cancelled, the old title stays.

### 4. Rename the session does NOT rename the chats
- Rename the **session** (doubleΓÇæclick the session title in the header / sessions list), give it a new name.
- Γ£à The session title updates.
- Γ£à Any chat tab you renamed in step 3 keeps its own name.
- Γ£à The **default** (first) chat tab ΓÇö if you never renamed it ΓÇö follows the session title; if you did rename it, it keeps its independent name.

### 5. Delete a chat asks for confirmation
- Hover a **peer** chat tab and click its **Γ£ò (Close)** button.
- Γ£à A confirmation dialog appears: *"Are you sure you want to delete this chat?"* with detail *"This action cannot be undone."* and a **Delete** primary button.
- Click **Cancel** ΓåÆ Γ£à the chat is **not** deleted, the tab stays.
- Click the **Γ£ò** again ΓåÆ **Delete** ΓåÆ Γ£à the chat tab is removed and the remaining chats are unaffected.
- Note: the **default** (first) chat has no close button ΓÇö it lives and dies with the session.

### 6. Persistence across reload (the main fix)
- In the session, make sure you have the default chat plus **at least one peer chat**, each with some conversation history.
- Run **Developer: Reload Window**.
- After reload, reΓÇæopen the same Copilot CLI session.
- Γ£à **All** chats are restored ΓÇö the peer chat tabs are still present (not just the default chat).
- Click into the **restored peer chat**.
- Γ£à Each one loads its own history reliably.
input.title: Test: Multi-chat in a Local Agent Host Copilot CLI session (create, switch, rename, delete, restore)

input.body:
## Summary

When a user adjusts model parameters (e.g. **Reasoning Effort**, **Context Size**, **Service Tier**) via the **Chat Model Picker dropdown**, the values are correctly persisted for the current chat editor, but third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions receive the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default ΓÇö not the user-selected value ΓÇö in `modelConfiguration` at request time. The user-visible settings do not flow into the API surface used by non-Copilot providers.

---

## Regression Window

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Last Known Good** | VS Code **1.124.2** (2026-06-10) |
| **First Known Bad** | VS Code **1.125.0** (2026-06-17) ΓÇö also **1.125.1** |
| **Root Cause PR** | [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) ΓÇö *Fix model configurations syncing across editors and locations* |
| **Underlying Issue** | [#320393](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/320393) ΓÇö *Context length selector changes on all open editors* |
| **Milestone** | [1.125.0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/milestone/424) |
| **Files Changed** | `+725 / ΓêÆ58` across ~8 files |

### What the PR Did

PR [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) introduced **per-editor scoped model configuration** to fix a bug where changing context size in one editor affected all other open editors. The key additions were:

- **`chatModelConfigurationStore.ts`** ΓÇö Per-editor `IStorageService` bucket (resolves per `(location, sessionType)` key)
- **`chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts`** ΓÇö Resolution logic: `resolveModelConfiguration()`, `computeStoredConfiguration()`, `filterConfigurationToSchema()`, `extractSchemaDefaults()`
- **`modelPickerActionItem.ts`** ΓÇö `IModelConfigurationAccess` interface (read/write/actions contract)
- **Modifications to `chatInputPart.ts`** ΓÇö Routes model picker reads/writes through `ChatModelConfigurationStore`
- **Modifications to `chatService.ts` / `chatServiceImpl.ts`** ΓÇö Passes `userSelectedModelConfiguration` through to requests
- **Modifications to `languageModels.ts`** ΓÇö Extracted `createModelConfigurationActions()` helper

### What Broke

Before 1.125.0, model picker wrote configuration **directly** via `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` ΓåÆ both the global `_modelConfigurations` Map and `chatLanguageModels.json`. After the change, the primary write targets the per-editor `IStorageService` bucket, and only *mirrors* to the global service as a secondary operation. The mirror write (`ChatModelConfigurationStore.setModelConfiguration()` ΓåÆ `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`) has a **hidden dependency**: it requires the model's metadata to already exist in `_modelCache`. When `_modelCache` is not yet populated (e.g. provider registered asynchronously), `setModelConfiguration()` silently exits without writing anything.

| | **1.124.2 (Good)** | **1.125.0 (Bad)** |
|---|---|---|
| **Primary write** | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` ΓåÆ per-editor bucket |
| **Global mirror** | N/A (it was the primary) | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` ΓåÆ ΓÜá∩╕Å depends on `_modelCache` hit |
| **Copilot reads** | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ΓåÉ ChatInputPart | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ΓåÉ `ChatModelConfigurationStore` Γ£à |
| **3rd-party provider reads** | `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` ΓåÉ `_modelConfigurations` Γ£à | Same path, but `_modelConfigurations` may be empty ΓÜá∩╕Å |

---

## Environment

- **VS Code Version**: ΓëÑ 1.125.0 (Insiders / Stable)
- **OS**: All platforms
- **Affected APIs**: `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` / `LanguageModelChatProvider`

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### The Silent Failure Path

| | **Channel Γæá: Per-Editor Bucket** | **Channel Γæí: Global `_modelConfigurations`** | **Channel Γæó: `chatLanguageModels.json`** |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Storage** | `IStorageService` KV store | In-memory `Map<string, IStringDictionary>` | Filesystem JSON |
| **Written by** | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsConfigurationService` |
| **Write reliability** | Γ£à Always succeeds | ΓÜá∩╕Å Depends on `_modelCache` hit | ΓÜá∩╕Å Depends on Channel Γæí |
| **Read by Copilot** | Γ£à `userSelectedModelConfiguration` | Γ¥î Not used | Γ¥î Not used |
| **Read by 3rd-party LM providers** | Γ¥î Not accessible | Γ£à `getModelConfiguration()` | Γ¥î Not read directly |

### The Silent Failure

The critical issue is in [`LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts):

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        return;  // ΓÜá∩╕Å SILENT RETURN ΓÇö no write, no error, no warning!
    }
    // ...
    // 1. Update _modelConfigurations (in-memory)
    // 2. Write to chatLanguageModels.json
    // 3. Fire onDidChangeLanguageModels event
}
```

When `_modelCache` does not contain the model metadata (e.g., because the extension provider registered asynchronously and the cache was not yet populated, or was cleared during model re-resolution), the method **silently exits** without:
- Updating `_modelConfigurations` (the cache from which third-party providers read)  
- Writing to `chatLanguageModels.json`  
- Throwing or logging any error  

### Why Copilot Is Unaffected

Copilot participates via the `ChatParticipant` API. Its request path receives configuration through `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration`, which is sourced from `ChatInputPart.getModelConfiguration()` ΓåÆ `ChatModelConfigurationStore.getModelConfiguration()` ΓÇö the **per-editor bucket (Channel Γæá)**:

```typescript
// chatServiceImpl.ts
modelConfiguration: options?.userSelectedModelConfiguration
    ?? (options?.userSelectedModelId
        ? this.languageModelsService.getModelConfiguration(options.userSelectedModelId)
        : undefined),
```

The primary value comes from `userSelectedModelConfiguration` (Channel Γæá), which is always available. The fallback to `getModelConfiguration()` (Channel Γæí) is only used when that primary source is absent.

### Why Third-Party Providers Are Affected

Third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations receive configuration through `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`, which is populated from `LanguageModelsService.getModelConfiguration()` ΓÇö the **global `_modelConfigurations` cache (Channel Γæí)**:

```typescript
// languageModelsService.sendChatRequest()
async sendChatRequest(modelId, from, messages, options, token) {
    const configuration = this.getModelConfiguration(modelId);
    const mergedOptions = configuration
        ? { ...options, configuration: { ...configuration, ...options.configuration } }
        : options;
    return provider.sendChatRequest(modelId, messages, from, mergedOptions, token);
}
```

If Channel Γæí was never populated with the user's latest picker selection (due to the silent failure above), `modelConfiguration` arrives as the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default.

---

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install a third-party extension that registers a `LanguageModelChatProvider` with a `configurationSchema` (e.g. one providing `reasoningEffort` and `contextSize` properties).
2. Open the Chat view and select the third-party model from the model picker.
3. In the model picker dropdown, change **Reasoning Effort** or **Context Size** to a non-default value (e.g., `max`).
4. Send a chat request using this model.
5. Observe in the extension's handler that `options.modelConfiguration` contains the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default ΓÇö never the value just selected in the picker.

### Expected

- Third-party providers should receive the user-selected configuration values from the model picker in `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`.

### Actual

- `options.modelConfiguration` at request time contains the value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default, not the value the user just selected in the model picker.
- The model picker UI shows the correct selected value (because it reads from the per-editor bucket), but the actual request uses the persisted/default value visible to third-party providers.

---

## Proposed Fix

### Short-term

At minimum, `setModelConfiguration()` should **log a warning** when `_modelCache` is missing a model, so the silent failure is visible. Additionally, consider retrying the write when the model becomes available:

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        this._logService.warn(`[LM] Cannot persist configuration for '${modelId}': model not yet resolved in cache`);
        // Optionally: queue the write for when the model resolves
        return;
    }
    // ...
}
```

### Long-term

1. **Unify the two configuration channels** so there is a single source of truth for per-model configuration, accessible equally to Copilot and third-party providers.
2. **Expose per-editor model configuration** to extensions through a stable API, so `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations can access the user's current selection without relying on the global cache.
3. **Make `chatLanguageModels.json` or a unified service the canonical persistence layer** for all model configuration, ensuring that changes from the model picker are always written to the file/service and are consistently read by all consumers, including third-party providers.

---

## Related Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationStore.ts` | Per-editor scoped storage (Channel Γæá) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts` | Resolution, filtering, schema extraction |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts` | `setModelConfiguration()` / `getModelConfiguration()` / `sendChatRequest()` (Channel Γæí) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/languageModelsConfigurationService.ts` | `chatLanguageModels.json` read/write (Channel Γæó) |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostLanguageModels.ts` | Extension host: `$startChatRequest` ΓÇö bridges `modelConfiguration` to provider |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguageModels.ts` | Main thread: registers provider & proxies `sendChatRequest` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | Chat service: merges `userSelectedModelConfiguration` ΓåÆ `modelConfiguration` |

---

## Impact

This bug affects **all third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions** that declare a `configurationSchema` and rely on `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` to receive user-configured model parameters (reasoning effort, context size, service tier, etc.).

input.body:
My workspace has a lot of Git repos (clone of AOSP codebase). As of recently (few weeks ago) I'm getting frequent Copilot Chat session interruptions due to my GitHub API rate limit being exhausted. Previously I had worked on the same codebase without these issues for a few months. My understanding is that this limit does not come from Copilot, but from regular GitHub API usage. I wonder if it might be caused by large number of queries for content exclusion rules (these can be observed in the log).

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.53.1
- VS Code Version: 1.125.1
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (remote SSH session started from VS Code running on Windows 11 Home, 25H2, build 26200.8655)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Auto
- Logs: [GitHub Copilot Chat.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29177899/GitHub.Copilot.Chat.log)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. `sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install repo && mkdir aosp-source && cd aosp-source` 
2. `repo init -u https://android.googlesource.com/platform/manifest -b android-15.0.0_r36`
3. Start Copilot Chat session (submit a prompt)
4. After a long time of seemingly no activity (other than the "Waiting..." message), and potentially a few prompts and responses (typically a single digit number) an error like this comes up in chat window:
```
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: 7b3b42d5-f545-4ab3-841a-01c3e1106848

Reason: key is missing
```
5. After waiting sufficiently (around 35 minutes), the session can be resumed (GitHub login succeeds in the background). Content exclusion rules are then refreshed (taking another up to 10 minutes). After that, new prompts can be submitted, but after only a few prompts/responses, the error reappears.
input.title: GitHub API limit frequently exhausted

input.body: Deleted

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
**Problem**
There's currently no easy way to copy a code block from the rendered Markdown preview ΓÇö you have to manually select the text, which is fiddly and easy to get wrong (e.g. accidentally including line numbers or surrounding text).

**Proposal**
Add a hover-triggered copy icon/button on each fenced code block (` ``` `) in the Markdown preview, similar to how GitHub, GitLab, and most documentation sites handle it. Clicking it copies the raw code content to the clipboard.

**Scope**
This applies specifically to the built-in **Markdown preview** (`markdown-language-features` extension) ΓÇö not the Markdown source editor itself.

**Additional context**
Happy to put together a prototype/PR for this if the team is open to it.
input.title: Add copy button to code blocks in Markdown preview
