input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Extensions should be able to register **new custom section types** in the Agent Customizations management editor, alongside the built-in ones (Agents, Skills, Instructions, Prompts, Hooks, MCP Servers, Plugins, Models). Currently, sections are hardcoded in VS Code's source with no extension point.

---

## Current Architecture

The sections are defined as a hardcoded const object:

**`src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts`**
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts

```typescript
export const AICustomizationManagementSection = {
    Agents: 'agents',
    Skills: 'skills',
    Instructions: 'instructions',
    Prompts: 'prompts',
    Hooks: 'hooks',
    McpServers: 'mcpServers',
    Plugins: 'plugins',
    Models: 'models',
} as const;
```

The UI metadata (label, icon, description) is also hardcoded in:

**`src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationManagementEditor.ts`**
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationManagementEditor.ts

```typescript
const sectionInfo: Record<string, { label: string; icon: ThemeIcon; description: string }> = {
    [AICustomizationManagementSection.Agents]: { label: 'Agents', icon: agentIcon, ... },
    [AICustomizationManagementSection.Skills]: { label: 'Skills', icon: skillIcon, ... },
    // ... all sections hardcoded
};
```

The visible sections are determined by `IAICustomizationWorkspaceService.managementSections`, which is also hardcoded in both implementations:

- **Core VS Code**  https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts
- **Sessions window**  https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts

The **create button menu** (`chat/customizations/create`) and **item context menu** (`chat/customizations/item`) are already exposed as proposed menu contribution points behind `chatSessionCustomizationProvider`:

**`src/vs/workbench/services/actions/common/menusExtensionPoint.ts`**
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/actions/common/menusExtensionPoint.ts

```typescript
{
    key: 'chat/customizations/create',
    id: MenuId.for('AICustomizationManagementCreate'),
    description: "The create button in the Chat Customizations management editor.",
    proposed: 'chatSessionCustomizationProvider',
},
{
    key: 'chat/customizations/item',
    id: MenuId.for('AICustomizationManagementEditorItem'),
    description: "The item context menu in the Chat Customizations management editor.",
    proposed: 'chatSessionCustomizationProvider',
},
```

The `ChatSessionCustomizationType` class already supports custom types via `new ChatSessionCustomizationType(id)`:

**`src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.chatSessionCustomizationProvider.d.ts`**
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.chatSessionCustomizationProvider.d.ts

```typescript
export class ChatSessionCustomizationType {
    static readonly Agent: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    static readonly Skill: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    static readonly Instructions: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    static readonly Prompt: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    static readonly Hook: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    static readonly Plugins: ChatSessionCustomizationType;
    readonly id: string;
    constructor(id: string);  // <-- already allows custom IDs!
}
```

**However**, using `new ChatSessionCustomizationType('my-custom-type')` does **not** create a UI section; the custom type ID has no matching entry in the hardcoded `sectionInfo` map, so the management editor has nothing to render.

---

## Proposed API

I see three possible approaches, from simplest to most flexible. Any of them would be valuable.

### Option A: `contributes` point in `package.json`

A declarative contribution point for extensions to register sections in their `package.json`:

```json
"contributes": {
    "customizationSections": [
        {
            "id": "memory",
            "label": "Memory",
            "description": "Persistent memory stores for AI agents",
            "icon": "$(database)",
            "fileExtensions": [".memory.md", ".memory.json"],
            "defaultCreateCommand": "myExtension.createMemory"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Option B: Programmatic API in `vscode.chat`

```typescript
export namespace chat {
    /**
     * Register a custom section in the Agent Customizations management editor.
     * @param id Unique section identifier (e.g. `'memory'`, `'workflows'`).
     * @param metadata Display metadata for the section.
     * @param provider Optional provider for items in this section.
     */
    export function registerCustomizationSection(
        id: string,
        metadata: CustomizationSectionMetadata,
        provider?: CustomizationItemProvider
    ): Disposable;
}

export interface CustomizationSectionMetadata {
    label: string;
    description: string;
    icon?: Uri | { light: Uri; dark: Uri } | ThemeIcon;
    createCommand?: string;
    filePatterns?: string[];
}
```

### Option C: Extend existing `ChatSessionCustomizationProviderMetadata`

The existing `registerChatSessionCustomizationProvider` already has `supportedTypes`. Adding a `customSections` field would let harnesses declare entirely new sections:

```typescript
export interface ChatSessionCustomizationProviderMetadata {
    readonly label: string;
    readonly iconId?: string;
    readonly supportedTypes?: readonly ChatSessionCustomizationType[];
    
    // NEW: Declare custom sections this provider contributes
    readonly customSections?: CustomSectionDescriptor[];
}

export interface CustomSectionDescriptor {
    readonly id: string;
    readonly label: string;
    readonly description: string;
    readonly iconId?: string;
}
```

---

## Use Cases

### 1. ≡ƒºá Memory Management

Current situation: Memory/context stores (`.memory.md` files, knowledge bases) have no dedicated home in the Agent Customizations panel. Extensions that manage persistent agent memory must either:
- Reuse the Instructions section (semantically wrong : memory Γëá instructions)
- Create their own custom tree views outside the management editor
- Rely on file explorers and manual management

With custom sections, a memory management extension could register a **Memory** section in the Agent Customizations panel where users can:
- Browse all memory files across workspaces
- Create/edit memory entries directly
- Enable/disable specific memory stores
- See memory usage stats inline

### 2. ≡ƒöî Tool/MCP Configurations

Extensions that provide tool registrations (MCP servers, custom tools) currently live under the "MCP Servers" section. A section for **Custom Tools** or **API Connections** would allow better separation of concerns.

### 3. ≡ƒôï Workflow/Pipeline Definitions

Agents often need workflow definitions (DAGs, pipeline steps). A dedicated **Workflows** section would let extensions surface these alongside other customizations.

### 4. ≡ƒôè Analytics & Monitoring

A **Monitoring** section could show agent usage stats, token consumption, or performance metrics in a dedicated place.

### 5. ≡ƒöÉ Security & Permissions

Extensions managing API keys, allowed tools, or security policies could use a **Security** section.

### 6. ≡ƒöä Sync & Version Control

Extensions providing customization syncing across machines or teams could add a **Sync** section showing sync status, conflicts, and history.

---

## Implementation Notes

The changes would touch these files:

| File | What to change |
|------|---------------|
| `aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts` | Accept dynamic sections from registered extensions |
| `aiCustomizationManagementEditor.ts` | Build `sectionInfo` from both hardcoded + contributed sections |
| `customizationHarnessService.ts` / `IHarnessDescriptor` | Allow custom sections to be listed and managed |
| `mainThreadChatAgents2.ts` | Bridge extension API to the workspace service |
| `vscode.proposed.chatSessionCustomizationProvider.d.ts` | Add new API surface |
| `menusExtensionPoint.ts` | Register new contribution point if using `contributes` approach |

The key insight is that the management editor already supports:
1. Iterating over `managementSections` dynamically
2. Looking up section info from a `Record<string, {label, icon, description}>` map
3. Per-section create button overrides via `ISectionOverride`
4. Per-section `hiddenSections` filtering via harness descriptors

This means the plumbing for dynamic sections largely exists. the main gap is the lack of an extension point to **add** new entries to these maps.

---

## Related Code References

- [AICustomizationManagementSection definition](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts)
- [sectionInfo hardcoded map in management editor](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationManagementEditor.ts)
- [Core VS Code workspace service (managementSections)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts)
- [Sessions workspace service (managementSections)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomizationWorkspaceService.ts)
- [ChatSessionCustomizationType proposed API](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.chatSessionCustomizationProvider.d.ts)
- [Menu contribution points (chat/customizations/create, chat/customizations/item)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/actions/common/menusExtensionPoint.ts)
- [IHarnessDescriptor interface](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/customizationHarnessService.ts)
- [AI Customizations design doc](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/AI_CUSTOMIZATIONS.md)
input.title: Copilot : Allow extensions to register custom sections in the Agent Customizations panel

input.body:
Refs: #320393, #321405

- [x] anyOS @digitarald
- [x] anyOS @hediet
- [x] anyOS @joshspicer

Complexity: 3

Author: @pwang347


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322259%0A%0A&assignees=pwang347)

---

**Context:** Previously, we stored model configurations as globals in `chatLanguageModels.json`. This did NOT align with the behaviour for model selections themselves, which follow a hierarchy of 1. saved per session to avoid breaking cache 2. stored as default selected per location + session type and 3. using a global value.

**What Changed:** We should now expect that wherever a model is shown, the associated config should be either the appropriate default value or match the previously used value there.

## Test Cases
For the test cases here, we are focused on the Local agent. There are known issues with Copilot CLI context selection.

### 1. Default context behaviour is correct 
- Open VS Code with --transient flag
- Select the Local Agent
- Select GPT 5.5
- Verify that the picker defaults to the smaller context value
- Send a message and verify that the context widget shows the correct context value (not 1M)
- Open chat debug logs, and verify that the maxPromptTokens is 272000
  
### 2. Default value after initial change is persisted
- In chat panel, choose a model and modify the thinking effort / context size
- Select a different harness and do the same
- Reload the window
- Verify that the values for both harnesses are the same as the last set values

### 3. Configurations are persisted per session
- Send two chat messages (separate conversations), each with the same model but different model configurations (thinking effort / context size)
- Reload the window
- Clicking into each of the chats should show the previously selected configurations for each
- Reload the window and start a new chat with the model; the default config should be whatever was last set
input.title: Test Plan: Session-scoped model configurations

input.body:
I'm constantly seeing Claude indicate that part of some input or prompt is "cut off". Is this just some artifact of how Claude "thinks", or is there a bug somewhere that actually is truncating my prompt?

Here is an example with Claude Opus 4.8, with thinking effort set to "Max".

### Prompt

> So ideally we should have one 16-count semaphore for file reads, and one 50-count semaphore for S3 requests? Is it easy to poinpoint exactly those I/O operations and network requests? If we do so, it is OK to let all the other network requests and I/O operations grow unbounded?

### Thinking

(Bold emphasis added.)

> The user is asking three specific questions about semaphore configuration and implementation. First, they want me to confirm whether a 16-count semaphore for file reads and a 50-count semaphore for S3 requests is the right approach. Second, they're asking how feasible it is to identify and wrap those exact I/O operations and network requests with semaphore acquire/release calls. Third, **the question cuts off** but seems to be heading toward implementation details.

In the particular case above, the response contained a complete answer to the third question I provided, so maybe that "cut off" verbiage is just an artifact of some "thinking" technique Claude uses.

I continually see this. The pattern is always something along the lines of, "The user seems to be asking about X, but then it cuts off." I just want to make sure GitHub Copilot isn't cutting something off. Or maybe GitHub Copilot cuts off the prompt when it summarizes and gives it to some subtask or some less-capable model for feeding back into Claude? It's puzzling and I just want to make sure everything is working properly (especially when I'm paying what I'm paying for Claude Opus 4.8 on Max level).

input.body:
## Problem

When a session invokes a skill that spawns a sub-agent (e.g. `review-areas` spawning a rubber-duck sub-agent), the main agent can end its turn/session *before* the sub-agent finishes. The sub-agent's results are delivered to its child channel but never relayed back to the main channel ΓÇö the user only sees the "waiting for results" message.

## Observed behavior

1. Main agent (opus-4.6) invokes review skill ΓåÆ sub-agent (gpt-5.5) spawned on child channel
2. Main model emits `end_turn` after just "Kicked off the deep multi-angle review ΓÇö waiting for results." (17 output tokens)
3. `sessionEnd` fires for main agent
4. Sub-agent works for ~90s, produces 8294 chars of review findings on child channel
5. `chat/turnComplete` fires for the main turn
6. User sees session idle with only the "waiting" message ΓÇö review results are orphaned

## Expected behavior

Sub-agent results should be surfaced to the user on the main channel, either by:
- Keeping the parent session alive until sub-agents complete, or
- Relaying sub-agent `responsePart` content to the parent channel upon sub-agent completion

## Logs

Session `a867cc0d`, turn `request_fdfc4def`, 2026-06-20T18:32:52Z. Agent Host log shows:
```
Turn ended: 2
Hook started: sessionEnd
Hook ended: sessionEnd
...5s gap...
[sub-agent starts working on child channel]
...90s later...
Subagent completed: rubber-duck
chat/turnComplete
Session idle
```
input.title: AHP: Sub-agent results lost when parent agent ends turn before sub-agent completes

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:

- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):
- Logs:

### Describe the bug

Running chat session from VS Code, I have installed a hook that denies specific commands.

### Affected version

- VS Code `1.125.1`
- GitHub Copilot Chat Extension `v0.53.1`
- Feature: agent with bypass approvals
- Any model

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

**.github/hooks/hooks.json**

```json
{
	"hooks": {
		"PreToolUse": [
			{
				"type": "command",
				"cwd": "src",
				"windows": "bin/Debug/net10.0/hook --tee requests.txt",
				"timeout": 15
			}
		]
	}
}
```

My `hook.exe` program parses the event hook and denies commands containing `Get-Date` from `run_in_terminal` tool. The `--tee request.txt` allows to pipe the STDIN request and dump the exit code and STDOUT response from the hook handler.

<details>

<summary>You can quickly re-create `hook.exe` as thus:</summary>

**Program.cs**

```csharp
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.Json.Serialization;

var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(new Response
{
  Continue = false,
  stopReason = "Tool call denied",
  hookSpecificOutput = new
  {
    permissionDecision = "deny",
    permissionDecisionReason = "Get-Date is a dangerous command",
  }
});
Console.WriteLine(json);
Environment.Exit(2);

class Response
{
  [JsonPropertyName("continue")]
  public bool Continue { get; set; } = default!;
  public string? stopReason { get; set; }
  public object hookSpecificOutput { get; set; } = default!;
}
```

</details>

Here is a simple session prompt:

> Give me the latest powershell `Get-Date`

**requests.txt**
```txt
{"timestamp":"2026-06-20T09:02:23.792Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"189c9189-f1ea-4656-9b85-b5b31729ce41","transcript_path":"redacted.jsonl","tool_name":"run_in_terminal","tool_input":{"command":"Get-Date","explanation":"Print current date/time","goal":"Show current PowerShell date/time","mode":"sync"},"tool_use_id":"call_SCugVzemRWFMPYhoX6DKCdEE__vscode-1781945312345","cwd":"c:\\Projects\\springcomp\\hooks\\t\\src"}
```
**requests_response.txt**
```txt
2{"continue":false,"stopReason":"Tool call denied","systemMessage":null,"hookSpecificOutput":{"permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Get-Date is a dangerous command","updatedInput":null,"additionalContext":null,"hookEventName":"PreToolUse"}}
```

I tried _everything_:

- Exit code `2`
- ResponseΓÇÖs `continue: false` property
- ResponseΓÇÖs `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: deny` property

Still, the tool is called and never denied.

### Expected behavior

The tool call SHOULD be denied.

### Additional context

_No response_
input.title: preToolUse hook fails at denying tool call

input.body:
<!-- ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ΓÜá∩╕ÅΓÜá∩╕Å -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Summary

Add an option to configure a custom User-Agent for the VS Code Integrated Browser.

### Problem

The Integrated Browser already allows overriding the User-Agent through the Device Emulation toolbar or the "Browser: Emulate User Agent..." command. However, this override is temporary: it only applies to the current browser instance, resets when opening a new tab, and is not persisted across VS Code sessions.

There is currently no way to configure a persistent default User-Agent for the Integrated Browser.


### Example:

`{
  "browser.userAgent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/137.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
}`

### Benefits:

Better compatibility with websites that reject Electron User-Agents.
Improved testing and debugging capabilities.
Consistency with other Chromium-based browsers that support User-Agent overrides through DevTools or launch options.
More flexibility for developers using the Integrated Browser as part of their workflow.
input.title: Feature Request: Allow persistent Custom User-Agent in the Integrated Browser

input.body: Currently VS Code has native support for stashes in the Source Control Repositories view, and a stash can be opened in the multi-file diff editor. I would like the native Stashes tree to support expanding each stash to show the changed files inside it, similar to GitLens' Stashes view. ### Example GitLens provides this kind of workflow today: a stash can be expanded, its changed files are shown as child nodes, and each file can be opened individually. ### Expected UX - Source Control Repositories view shows `Stashes` - Expanding a stash shows its changed files as child nodes - Files can be displayed as a tree/list with SCM decorations/status - Clicking a file opens the individual diff for that file - Existing stash-level actions like View, Apply, Pop, Drop remain available This would make it possible to inspect a stash incrementally without opening the full multi-file diff first. ### Related - #279605 added Stashes nodes into the Repositories view - #280596 added click action for stashes - #203037 added the `Git: View Stash...` multi-file diff experience - #180720 tracks a broader native stash view request
input.title: SCM: Show changed files as child nodes under each stash

input.body:
VS Code Version:
1.125.1

Copilot Chat Version:
0.53.1

OS:
Windows 11

Description:
When configuring an OpenAI BYOK model through `chatLanguageModels.json` with `vendor: openai`, Copilot Chat appears to ignore the configured `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens` values.

The same configuration, when changed from `vendor: openai` to `vendor: customendpoint`, appears to honour the configured token limits correctly.

The documentation appears to imply that these model-level settings are valid for providers including `openai`, because the model configuration reference lists `vendor` examples including `azure`, `openai`, and `customendpoint`, and then lists `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens` as model-level properties. The docs also say VS Code uses the sum of these two values as the model's total context window in the Chat view.

If these settings are not intended to be supported for `vendor: openai`, the documentation should say that clearly. If they are intended to be supported, this appears to be a bug.

Model configuration:
The model is configured through `chatLanguageModels.json`, not selected from the standard built-in Copilot model list.

The relevant OpenAI provider entry uses the Responses API:

    vendor: openai
    apiType: responses
    model id: gpt-5.5
    streaming: true
    toolCalling: true
    vision: true
    thinking: true
    zeroDataRetentionEnabled: true
    reasoningEffort: medium
    maxInputTokens: 272000
    maxOutputTokens: 128000
    url: https://api.openai.com/v1/responses

Representative redacted configuration:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "OpenAI",
    "vendor": "openai",
    "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.REDACTED}",
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "gpt-5.5",
        "name": "GPT-5.5",
        "url": "https://api.openai.com/v1/responses",
        "apiType": "responses",
        "toolCalling": true,
        "vision": true,
        "thinking": true,
        "streaming": true,
        "maxInputTokens": 272000,
        "maxOutputTokens": 128000,
        "zeroDataRetentionEnabled": true,
        "supportsReasoningEffort": ["none", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"],
        "reasoningEffortFormat": "responses"
      }
    ],
    "settings": {
      "gpt-5.5": {
        "reasoningEffort": "medium"
      }
    }
  }
]
```

Expected:
The Context Window popover and context packing should use the configured model limits:

    maxInputTokens + maxOutputTokens
    272000 + 128000 = 400000 tokens

So the Chat UI should show a context window of approximately 400K tokens.

The configured `maxInputTokens` value should also be respected by context packing so that VS Code does not pack more input context than the configured cap.

Actual:
With `vendor: openai`, the Context Window popover showed a much larger context window, approximately:

    18.8K / 888K tokens

This suggests VS Code is using built-in or discovered metadata for the recognised OpenAI model instead of the manually configured `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens`.

Workaround / comparison:
Changing the same endpoint and model configuration to `vendor: customendpoint` causes the configured values to be honoured.

For example:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "OpenAI Custom Endpoint",
    "vendor": "customendpoint",
    "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.REDACTED}",
    "apiType": "responses",
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "gpt-5.5",
        "name": "GPT-5.5 Custom",
        "url": "https://api.openai.com/v1/responses",
        "apiType": "responses",
        "toolCalling": true,
        "vision": true,
        "thinking": true,
        "streaming": true,
        "maxInputTokens": 272000,
        "maxOutputTokens": 128000,
        "zeroDataRetentionEnabled": true,
        "supportsReasoningEffort": ["none", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"],
        "reasoningEffortFormat": "responses"
      }
    ],
    "settings": {
      "gpt-5.5": {
        "reasoningEffort": "medium"
      }
    }
  }
]
```

With `vendor: customendpoint`, the Context Window popover showed:

    18.8K / 400K tokens

That matches the configured total:

    272000 + 128000 = 400000 tokens

Impact:
This prevents users from reliably controlling context packing for BYOK OpenAI models.

In this specific case, the configured model has a pricing threshold based on input-token count. If VS Code ignores the configured `maxInputTokens` value and instead packs according to the model's larger maximum context window, a conversation can unexpectedly cross into the higher pricing tier.

This is especially problematic because the UI appears to advertise the larger effective context window when `vendor: openai` is used, suggesting that the larger value may be used by the context packer rather than only displayed incorrectly.

Suggested resolution:
If `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens` are intended to be supported with `vendor: openai`, ensure the OpenAI provider honours the configured model-level values and uses them for both:

- the Chat Context Window display
- context packing / prompt construction

If these settings are intentionally ignored or overridden for recognised OpenAI models when `vendor: openai` is used, please document that clearly in the BYOK / `chatLanguageModels.json` model configuration documentation.

Additional notes:
Other model-level settings may also be affected when using `vendor: openai`, but this has not been verified. The demonstrated issue is specifically with `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens`.

The current workaround is to configure the same OpenAI Responses API endpoint using `vendor: customendpoint`, where the configured token limits appear to be honoured.
input.title: Copilot Chat BYOK `vendor: openai` appears to ignore `maxInputTokens` and `maxOutputTokens`

Type: Bug When I restart VSCode, the previously opened windows disappear, move to another monitor, and resize. If I use the Developer: Reload Window option from the command palette, I sometimes get my previous windows back, but even then it is not uncommon for VSCode to come back with only 1 window. This phenomenon has been present since version 1.125.1. input.body:
VS Code Version:
1.125.1

Copilot Chat Version:
0.53.1

OS:
Windows 11

Description:
Copilot Chat/Agent mode repeatedly fails during normal conversations with:

```
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: <REDACTED_CLIENT_REQUEST_ID>

Reason: Response contained no choices.: Error: Response contained no choices.
    at FG._provideLanguageModelResponse
    (.../resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:14094)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:104:5)
    at async FG.provideLanguageModelResponse
    (.../resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:15097)
```

This happens multiple times per conversation. There does not appear to be a 100% reliable way to reproduce the problem but it inevitably happens the longer the conversation goes.

Model configuration:
The failing model is configured through `chatLanguageModels.json`, not selected from the standard built-in Copilot model list.

The relevant configuration is an OpenAI provider entry using the Responses API:

```
vendor: openai
apiType: responses
model id: gpt-5.5
streaming: true
toolCalling: true
vision: true
thinking: true
zeroDataRetentionEnabled: true
reasoningEffort: medium
maxInputTokens: 272000
maxOutputTokens: 128000
url: https://api.openai.com/v1/responses
```

This configuration is used so model requests can use zero data retention mode.

Local log correlation:
In local VS Code logs, each visible `Response contained no choices` error lines up with a Copilot Chat request summary marked `unknown` for the same configured `gpt-5.5` model via `copilotLanguageModelWrapper`.

Redacted examples:

```
<timestamp> ccreq:<REDACTED>.copilotmd | unknown | gpt-5.5 | <duration>ms | [copilotLanguageModelWrapper]
<timestamp> [CHAT] extension request ERRORED in STREAM Response contained no choices.

<timestamp> ccreq:<REDACTED>.copilotmd | unknown | gpt-5.5 | <duration>ms | [copilotLanguageModelWrapper]
<timestamp> [CHAT] extension request ERRORED in STREAM Response contained no choices.

<timestamp> ccreq:<REDACTED>.copilotmd | unknown | gpt-5.5 | <duration>ms | [copilotLanguageModelWrapper]
<timestamp> [CHAT] extension request ERRORED in STREAM Response contained no choices.
```

This pattern was found repeatedly in the same VS Code log session. The unredacted request IDs are available locally, but are redacted here because they may be uniquely trackable.

Relevant source-code path:
In current `microsoft/vscode`, this appears to flow through:

```
extensions/copilot/src/extension/conversation/vscode-node/languageModelAccess.ts
extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts
extensions/copilot/src/platform/chat/common/commonTypes.ts
```

`languageModelAccess.ts` uses `debugName: 'copilotLanguageModelWrapper'` for the language model wrapper request.

`chatMLFetcher.ts` returns `ChatFetchResponseType.Unknown` with `RESPONSE_CONTAINED_NO_CHOICES` when no successful completion is produced from the completion stream.

`commonTypes.ts` defines:

```
export const RESPONSE_CONTAINED_NO_CHOICES = 'Response contained no choices.';
```

Impact:
This is user-visible and interrupts normal chat/agent workflows repeatedly. It does not appear to be prompt-specific. The failure appears to happen when the OpenAI Responses API configured model stream produces no usable completion choice, after which Copilot Chat classifies the request as `unknown` and surfaces the generic `Response contained no choices` error.

Expected:
Copilot Chat should either produce a response or surface a more actionable provider/model/backend error. If this is a valid upstream ΓÇ£no outputΓÇ¥ or malformed Responses API stream condition, the extension should handle it gracefully and show enough information to identify whether the issue is model output, Responses API parsing, streaming, tool-calling, reasoning output, or zero data retention mode.

Possible related issues:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/318235
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/313215
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/316360
input.title: Copilot Chat frequently fails with "Response contained no choices" using OpenAI Responses API BYOK model

input.body: Type: Bug Description When using Live Preview with docked browser DevTools, normal editor interactions cause the browser layout to break. The browser view temporarily expands to fill the area occupied by the docked DevTools, causing the DevTools to disappear. Once the interaction ends, the layout returns to normal. This only affects docked browser DevTools. If the browser DevTools are undocked into a separate window, the problem disappears. Steps to reproduce Open Live Preview (Show Preview). Open Browser DevTools docked inside the browser. Interact with the editor by: switching editor tabs, typing (triggering IntelliSense/autocomplete), changing focus between editors, open command palette. Observe the browser view. Expected behaviour The browser and docked DevTools remain visible and maintain their layout. Actual behaviour The browser temporarily expands over the docked DevTools area, making the DevTools disappear until the interaction completes. The layout then snaps back. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ef9222d-20ee-49fc-b479-160e5ed21b82 Additional information Reproduces with Live Preview pages and ordinary websites (e.g. https://google.com), so it is not specific to Live Preview or my application. Does not reproduce when Browser DevTools are undocked. Developer: Toggle Developer Tools continues to inspect the VS Code workbench correctly; the issue is specific to the Integrated Browser + docked Browser DevTools. The issue began immediately after updating both macOS and VS Code to the versions listed below: VS Code: 1.124.2 (Stable) Electron: 42.2.0 Chromium: 148.0.7778.97 macOS: 15.5 (Darwin 25.5.0) VS Code version: Code 1.125.1 (Universal) (fcf604774b9f2674b473065736ee75077e256353, 2026-06-19T08:39:34Z) OS version: Darwin arm64 25.5.0 Modes:
input.title: Integrated Browser docked DevTools disappear and Browser View incorrectly resizes during editor interactions

input.body:
Type: Bug Dear VS Code Development Team,I am writing to submit an issue regarding how VS Code's extension update mechanism interacts with local, resource-intensive extensions (specifically local AI/LLM indexing tools like Kilo Code).Whenever VS Code updates itself or automatically updates an AI indexing extension, the extension's local storage and cache state are routinely orphaned or invalidated. Because I rely on local LLMs for code embeddings, this forced cache loss triggers an aggressive, automatic re-indexing loop on startup. This pegs my laptopΓÇÖs CPU/GPU at 100% compute for an extended period, destroying battery life and severely disrupting my active development workflow.The Technical IssueWhile I understand that VS Code isolates extension versions into clean directories (e.g., migrating from ~/.vscode/extensions/publisher.extension-1.1.0 to 1.2.0), the platform's current management of global/workspace storage caches (ExtensionContext.globalStorageUri and ExtensionContext.storageUri) seems to trigger aggressive purges or path disconnections during specific update lifecycles.When the extension boots up post-update, the underlying platform state fails to maintain the pointer to the previously computed database. Because VS Code handles these updates aggressively and silently in the background, users have no way to defer or prevent these destructive cache resets.Requested Enhancements / SafeguardsTo protect hardware performance and stop silent resource exhaustion, please consider the following platform safeguards:Persistent State Bridging: Ensure that updates across minor extension versions guarantee strict continuity and preservation of data stored inside globalStorageUri. Extensions should not lose access to their heavy local databases due to platform migration quirks.Resource-Aware Update Throttling: Provide a clear, explicit setting or API that allows extensions to flag themselves as "High Compute / Local Model Reliant." When flagged, VS Code should block silent background updates and instead prompt the user with a warning: "Updating this extension will require re-indexing your workspace. Proceed now?"Workspace Integrity Check: Provide a more robust way for the platform to preserve local index mappings so extensions can seamlessly reconnect to external/global caches post-migration without assuming a blank-slate state.The current "silent update followed by massive background compute spike" behavior makes developing with local AI tools incredibly disruptive. I look forward to seeing the VS Code platform evolve to better handle the heavy, stateful storage needs of modern local AI tools.Sincerely, Magnus VS Code version: Code 1.125.1 (fcf604774b9f2674b473065736ee75077e256353, 2026-06-19T08:39:34Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

input.body:
Many students and beginner developers rely on AI coding assistants such as GitHub Copilot. However, when free credits expire, users often need to search for alternative tools manually.

It would be helpful if VS Code provided a dedicated section or marketplace category that highlights free AI coding assistants and their available plans. This could include information about:

**Free AI coding tools compatible with VS Code
Student-friendly offers and GitHub Student Developer Pack benefits
Free trial availability and usage limits
Recommended alternatives when credits expire**

This feature would help developers discover useful coding assistants more easily and improve the overall VS Code experience, especially for students and new programmers.

**Benefits for VS Code:**
This feature can improve user experience, help students discover useful AI coding assistants, increase engagement with the VS Code Marketplace, and encourage more developers to use VS Code as their primary development environment.
input.title: Add a Directory of Free AI Coding Assistants and Alternatives

Type: Bug When using Claude Opus 4.6, I can set a 200k context size, but the right hand side Context Window dial presumes all chats use 1M tokens. When I'm at 78k of context, I expect the dial and metricts to be 38% full not, 8% full. Not only is the dial misleading, it actually encourages developers to submit more requests at a higher cost when instead they should be compacting, or starting new chats. As we know, more context means 'lost in the middle' problems and performance degredation. Please address. Thanks. input.body:
≡ƒñû  generated based on a failing smoke test in CI

---

## Summary

A Linux Electron smoke test failed because the **relaunched** Electron **browser (main) process crashed with SIGSEGV during startup**, before the test driver could attach over the DevTools WebSocket. The minidump shows the top of the crashing stack is in **libexpat + libfontconfig**, i.e. fontconfig parsing its XML config via expat during font initialization.

This is almost certainly **infrastructure flakiness, not a product bug** ΓÇö and it's unrelated to the PR it failed on (#322170 only touches Agents Window smoke tests). The CI already carries a workaround for expat 2.6.1 NULL-deref CVEs in fontconfig parsing, yet the crash still occurred.

## Links

- **Failing job:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/job/82429344900
- **Workflow run:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533
- **Crash-dump artifact (minidump):** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/artifacts/7759317546
- **Logs artifact:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/artifacts/7759333287
- **Minidump path inside artifact:**
  `smoke-tests-electron/4_suite_test_verifies_that_hot_exit_works_for_dirty_files_with_delay/pending/430a921f-8492-43e3-b347-df0a0fa978e5.dmp`

## Failing test

```
VSCode Smoke Tests (Electron)
  Data Loss (insiders -> insiders)
    verifies that "hot exit" works for dirty files (with delay)
    electron.launch: WebSocket error: socket hang up
```
(46 passing, 63 pending, **1 failing**; step exited 255)

The first app instance shut down cleanly (`main.log` shows `app.quit()` at `22:19:05`). The failure is on `Application.restart()` ΓåÆ the **relaunched** process at `22:19:06`:

```
- <launched> pid=7464
- [pid=7464][err] Debugger attached.
- [pid=7464][out] Found --crash-reporter-directory argument...
- [pid=7464][err] DevTools listening on ws://127.0.0.1:45603/devtools/browser/...
- <ws connecting> ws://127.0.0.1:45603/devtools/browser/...
- <ws error> ... socket hang up
- [pid=7464] <process did exit: exitCode=null, signal=SIGSEGV>
```

## Minidump analysis

Parsed from the minidump (timestamp `Fri Jun 19 22:19:06 2026`, matching the relaunch):

- **Signal:** `EXCEPTION_SIGSEGV` (0xb), `ExceptionAddress: 0x0`
- **Crashing process:** browser/main process (`code-oss`, with `libgtk-3`, `libffmpeg`, `libnss3`, full browser module set loaded)
- **RIP:** `0x7fe0a1048806` ΓåÆ `libc.so.6+0x48806`
- **RSP:** `0x7fdf7a3e9a90`, **RBP:** `0x7fdf7a3e9ac0`

**Top of the crashing-thread stack** (module+offset; no symbols available in CI build):

```
#0  RIP            libc.so.6+0x48806
#1  sp+0x0038      libexpat.so.1+0x20183
#2  sp+0x0040      code-oss+0xc7450b0
#3  sp+0x0068      libexpat.so.1+0xc056
#4  sp+0x0098      libexpat.so.1+0x11568
#5  sp+0x00c8      libfontconfig.so.1+0x2f83e
```

(Frames #1ΓÇô#5 are contiguous near RSP and consistent; deeper frames are from a naive stack scan and less reliable.) The expat ΓåÆ fontconfig chain ending in a libc fault strongly indicates a crash while **fontconfig parses font-config XML via expat** during browser-process font init.

## Environment (from job log)

- Runner: Ubuntu 24.04 (noble), x86_64, 4 CPUs
- `libexpat1: 2.6.1-2ubuntu0.4` ΓåÉ has the known NULL-deref CVEs (fixed upstream in **expat ΓëÑ 2.7.5**)
- `fontconfig / libfontconfig1: 2.15.0-1.1ubuntu2`
- `FONTCONFIG_FILE=/tmp/fonts-minimal.conf` (the CI workaround is active)

The workflow already has a step **"Workaround expat NULL deref CVEs in fontconfig parsing"** that writes a minimal `fonts.conf` with no `<include>` directives and clears the fontconfig cache, precisely because expat 2.6.1 NULL-derefs when fontconfig parses `<include>` via `XML_ExternalEntityParserCreate`. **The crash happened despite this workaround.**

## Hypothesis

The relaunched Electron browser process **SIGSEGVs inside fontconfig's expat-based XML config parsing** at startup. Even with the minimal `FONTCONFIG_FILE` workaround, the buggy `libexpat 2.6.1` is still being exercised (e.g. fontconfig rebuilding its cache after the CI cache-clear, or a parse path the minimal config doesn't avoid), hitting one of the expat NULL-deref bugs. Because it's a font-init crash in the browser process, it surfaces intermittently and only on app relaunch, presenting to Playwright as `electron.launch: WebSocket error: socket hang up`.

## Suggested next steps

- Symbolicate the dump against this build's `code-oss` + bundled libs to confirm the exact expat/fontconfig frames.
- Consider hardening the CI workaround: pin/upgrade `libexpat1` to ΓëÑ 2.7.5 on the runner, or ensure fontconfig never parses the system config (verify the minimal conf fully avoids `<include>` and pre-warm the cache so no rebuild happens mid-run).
- Treat the `Data Loss / hot exit (with delay)` failure as a flaky native crash on relaunch, not a regression in #322170.

---

Want me to also extract the renderer/main logs of the crashing window into the issue, or symbolicate the dump if you can point me at the matching symbol server/build?
input.title: Smoke test Electron crash (SIGSEGV) on relaunch ΓÇö fontconfig/expat font init in browser process

input.body:
# Test: Cloud Agent (v2) ΓÇö create a pull request on demand

- [x] anyOS @meganrogge
- [x] anyOS @vritant24 

Complexity: 3

Author: @osortega


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322177%0A%0A&assignees=osortega)

---

## Context

Cloud Agent sessions on the v2 (Task API) backend are now PR-less by default, a session no longer auto-creates a pull request. Instead, you create the PR on demand from the session toolbar once the task has settled, and then open it. This TPI validates that flow.

## Setup

1. Latest VS Code Insiders.
2. Sign in to GitHub Copilot.
3. Set `"github.copilot.chat.cloudAgentBackend.version": "v2"` and reload the window.
4. Open a repo enrolled for Copilot coding agent.

## What to test

1. Start a new cloud task that makes code changes and let it run to completion. Confirm the settled session is PR-less (no PR badge/link, no PR auto-created on GitHub).
2. On the settled, PR-less session, confirm the chat input toolbar shows Create Pull Request button.
3. Invoke Create Pull Request ΓåÆ a PR is created against the correct repo/branch. The toolbar updates and shows Open Pull Request icon appears
4. Verify the changed-files view for the settled task is populated and matches the diff in the created PR.
5. Click Open Pull Request ΓåÆ the correct PR opens in the browser. Confirm it resolves whether the session is from v1 or v2 backend.
6. Reload the window and reopen the same session ΓåÆ it still resolves to the PR, and Open Pull Request is shown.
input.title: Test: Cloud Agent - create a pull request on demand

Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/321517

- [x] anyOS @sandy081

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322171%0A%0A&assignees=kycutler)

---

Integrated browser tabs in the agents window should be treated as specific to one session.

Each session should have its own set of browser tabs that don't interfere with other sessions.

## Setup

- Ensure the setting `workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled` is enabled
- Open two sessions using either Local or Agent Host-based agents

> [!NOTE]
> There is currently an issue with agent host that causes agents to not automatically "see" browser pages that have been shared with them.
> 
> **For scenario 3 below, be sure you test using the Local agent.**

## Scenarios

1. If an agent opens a browser tab while it is **not** the active session, the tab should not be opened in the editor.
   - The tab should still be usable by the agent
   - The tab can be opened by clicking on its label in the tool calls within the chat.

2. If an agent tries to open a browser tab with the same URL that is open in another session, it should open a new tab with no prompting.
   - But if a tab with the same URL is already open in this session, it will either prompt to share the tab or just tell the agent it is already open.

3. Agents should only "see" the browser tabs that are part of their session.
   - Be sure to test this using a Local agent (see above note)

4. Browser commands should operate on the tabs for the current active session.
   - Specifically, pressing `ctrl+alt+/` (`command+option+/` on Mac) should always open a new browser tab if there are none in the current session, or list the open ones for that session if there are any.
input.title: Test: browser isolation in agents window

input.body:
Refs: meganrogge/voice-mode-integration branch

- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Linux

Complexity: 5

Authors: @meganrogge

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+voice+mode%0A%0A&assignees=meganrogge)

---

Voice Mode enables hands-free interaction with chat sessions via push-to-talk, speech recognition, and text-to-speech responses.

## Prerequisites
- Enable `agents.voice.enabled` in settings
- Microphone access available

## Connection & Basic Flow

- [ ] Connect to voice mode via the mic icon in chat input
- [ ] Verify "Connecting..." state shows, then transitions to idle with PTT keybinding hint (e.g. "Press Ctrl+Shift+Space to talk")
- [ ] Hold the PTT keybinding (Ctrl+Shift+Space) and speak ΓÇö verify transcription appears in real-time
- [ ] Release PTT ΓÇö verify request is sent and response is read aloud (TTS)
- [ ] Verify mic icon is colored blue while listening, purple while speaking
- [ ] Disconnect via the disconnect button ΓÇö verify green disconnect icon in chat input

## Aux Window

- [ ] Open voice mode aux window (floating pill)
- [ ] Verify it appears centered above the VS Code window bottom
- [ ] Verify the window can be dragged/moved
- [ ] Verify PTT works in the aux window with same keybinding (Ctrl+Shift+Space)
- [ ] Verify transcript shows in aux window (user input + assistant response)
- [ ] Verify processing animation (comet border beam) shows while waiting for response
- [ ] Verify the window does not move/jump when toggling session list expand/collapse
- [ ] Verify session list is scrollable when many sessions exist (max-height constraint)
- [ ] Verify toolbar and transcript always remain visible below session list

## Session Targeting (Aux Window)

- [ ] Expand session list in aux window ΓÇö verify sessions are listed
- [ ] Click a session ΓÇö verify it becomes the voice target AND is revealed in the chat panel
- [ ] Speak with a non-focused session targeted ΓÇö verify request goes to correct session
- [ ] Verify response is read aloud even when targeting a non-focused session
- [ ] Verify transcript in chat input is suppressed when aux window targets a different session

## Confirmations via Voice

- [ ] When a session has a pending confirmation, say "approve" or "reject"
- [ ] Verify the confirmation is handled correctly even if the session is not focused
- [ ] Verify voice backend narrates the result after approval/rejection

## Error & Edge Cases

- [ ] Disconnect network mid-session ΓÇö verify reconnection behavior
- [ ] Deny microphone permission ΓÇö verify appropriate error state
- [ ] Rapidly toggle PTT ΓÇö verify no crashes or stuck states
- [ ] Close aux window while speaking ΓÇö verify graceful cleanup
input.title: Test: Voice Mode

input.body:

input.body:
## Bug Description
Chat session history titles are not being generated. All session debug logs are empty (1 byte), resulting in no session titles appearing in the chat history UI.

## Expected Behavior
- Session debug logs should capture chat content
- Session titles should auto-generate within ~1 minute
- Chat history should display sessions with generated titles

## Actual Behavior
- All 88 indexed sessions have empty summaries/titles
- Session debug log files are created but contain only 1 byte
- No metadata captured (cwd, repository, agent_name all NULL)
- Session history UI shows no sessions

## Diagnostic Data

**VS Code Version:** 1.125.1 (fcf604774b)  
**Platform:** Windows x64  
**Affected Sessions:** 88 (all sessions since 2026-06-18)

**Session Database Query Results:**
```
Total sessions:         88
Sessions with titles:   0  (should not be zero)
Sessions with metadata: 0  (no cwd, repository, agent_name)
Latest session:         2026-06-19T18:59:03.815Z
```

**Session Debug Log Status:**
- Current session ID: `497fa7a8-11af-49b7-88d1-406459f5ffd0`
- Log file exists: Γ£à Yes
- Log file size: Γ¥î 1 byte (empty)
- Path: `C:\Users\jdickson\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\workspaceStorage\...\GitHub.copilot-chat\debug-logs\<session-id>`

**Reindex Attempt:**
- Ran `session_store_sql` reindex operation
- Result: +2 sessions indexed, but still 0 titles generated
- Issue persists after reindex

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start any chat session in VS Code
2. Wait ~1-5 minutes
3. Check chat history UI
4. Observe: No session titles appear
5. Query session database: All summary fields are empty/NULL

## Impact
- **Critical:** Users cannot navigate chat history
- Cannot resume previous conversations from UI
- Session context is lost between restarts
- Work product must be manually saved to files

## Workaround
Manually save important chat outputs to files (the saved files are preserved).

## Timeline
- **Issue started:** ~2026-06-18 (all sessions since then affected)
- **Likely trigger:** Recent VS Code or Copilot extension update around this date

## Additional Context
- Session database is successfully tracking session IDs and timestamps
- The session metadata capture/logging mechanism appears to be silently failing
- No visible errors in VS Code or Copilot extension
- Permissions appear intact (files are created, just not written to)

## Request
Please investigate why session content logging stopped working, resulting in empty debug logs and no session title generation.
input.title: Copilot Chat: Session debug logs empty (1 byte) - no chat history titles generated

input.body:
Instead of:

> "The application shall sanitize all untrusted inputs before processing."

Perhaps:

> "The application shall implement controls to ensure that processing untrusted inputs does not compromise the application's security boundaries."

This is technique-neutral. A desktop email client satisfies it by sanitizing HTML in email bodies. A browser satisfies it by pointing to sandboxing, site isolation, and same-origin┬á enforcement. A PDF reader satisfies it by validating document structure before parsing. Each application demonstrates compliance through whatever mechanism is appropriate to its threat┬á model.

The tradeoff: Outcome-based language is harder to audit than prescriptive language. "Did you sanitize inputs?" is a yes/no question a lab can test. "Did you implement controls to protect security boundaries?" requires the applicant to articulate their threat model and the assessor to evaluate whether the controls are adequate. If we need a concrete checklist, we might need to add guidance like:

"Acceptable controls include, but are not limited to: input validation and sanitization, sandboxing, process isolation, and privilege separation."

_Originally posted by @alex941115 in https://github.com/appdefensealliance/ASA-WG/pull/306#discussion_r3175301472_

Refs: #315283

- [x] any OS @chrmarti 
- [x] any OS @kycutler

Complexity: 3

Authors: @lramos15

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322128%0A%0A&assignees=lramos15)


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322128%0A%0A&assignees=cwebster-99,lramos15)

---

VS Code now surfaces **session-level token usage**, similar to the Copilot CLI's `/usage`. This supplements the existing per-turn credit consumption and context-window indicators to give a fuller picture of what a chat session has consumed.

# Task

**Validate that session-level token usage is reported and is accurate.**

- Start a new agent/chat session and run several turns.
- Open the session token usage view and confirm:
  - [ ] Total session token consumption is shown and increases as turns are added
  - [ ] The session-level numbers are consistent with the per-turn credit / context-window info already shown
- Confirm the data resets / starts fresh for a new session.
- Try across model configurations (different context sizes / reasoning efforts) and verify the usage view still behaves correctly.
input.title: Test: Session-level token usage data

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
## Add BYOK settings to Group Policies.
Some users would like to use BYOK models on personal machines using the same Github Account as their organization provided Github license. Being able to have BYOK settings added to the group policies, we can limit the use of BYOK models on company machines while allowing those users to use custom agents at home as to not burn the organizations credits.

https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/enterprise/policies#_vs-code-enterprise-policy-reference
input.title: Add BYOK settings to Group Policies

input.body:
Context:
`chat.tools.terminal.enableAutoApprove`  Probably over-stepping if this leads to enabling all other tool. 

We should consider bringing chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove over as well, especially if it's running outside of sandbox. Potentially another step we'd want runtime to go through before they call their built-in shell tool.

This is lot intuitive with our custom terminal tool for AH, but I need to look into how the flow will work with shell tool from CLI SDK.

input.body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Not Applicable The issue appears to be related to installation of a platform-specific extension into a remote Codespace environment. - VS Code Version: 1.125.1 - OS Version: Host: Windows; Remote: GitHub Codespace (Linux/Ubuntu) ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Start a Linux GitHub Codespace from a Windows machine. 2. Connect to the Codespace from an environment where Extension Marketplace access is unavailable or restricted. 3. Attempt to install C# Dev Kit using the local installation workflow ("Install from Local" / local VSIX installation). 4. Open a .NET project in the Codespace. 5. Attempt to use C# Dev Kit functionality (debugging, project operations, etc.). ## Expected Behavior When connected to a Linux Codespace, VS Code should either: - Install the Linux-compatible version of the extension, or - Detect that the locally installed extension targets a different platform and block the installation (or provide a clear warning). ## Actual Behavior VS Code appeared to accept the local installation, but the resulting environment appeared to contain a mix of Windows and Linux components. The extension subsequently produced errors consistent with a platform mismatch, including "bad image format" failures when attempting to use C# Dev Kit functionality. This made the root cause difficult to diagnose. The failure occurred at runtime rather than during installation. Had VS Code detected and blocked the platform mismatch during the installation workflow, the root cause would have been immediately obvious. ## Additional Context The following screenshot shows the BadImageFormatException observed within the Linux Codespace environment after the local extension installation workflow was used. While troubleshooting with members of the C# Dev Kit team, the symptoms were determined to be consistent with a Windows-targeted extension being used within a Linux Codespace environment. The issue was ultimately resolved by creating a fresh Codespace from an environment with access to the Extension Marketplace and installing the extensions normally. After doing so, C# Dev Kit functioned as expected. One team member suggested that VS Code should not allow local installation when a platform-specific extension package is incompatible with the target environment, as the current behavior can lead users into a difficult-to-diagnose configuration. ## Suggested Improvement During local extension installation, validate the extension's target platform against the remote execution environment (Codespaces, SSH, Dev Containers, etc.). If the extension is incompatible: - Block installation, or - Display a prominent warning explaining the mismatch and potential consequences.
input.title: VS Code allows incompatible local extension installation into Linux Codespace, leading to platform mismatch and "bad image format" errors

- VS Code Version: 1.119.0
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.5.0

Steps to Reproduce:
1.  Settings => chat.mcp.discovery.enabled
2. Enable Claude Desktop configuration (`claude_desktop_config.json`)
3. Restart vscode, go to Cmd + Shift + P > "Open Chat" > "Claude" icon
4. Add an MCP and confirm it started.
5. Prompting the MCP fails

So `tablepro` is an MCP that I've added to Claude Desktop, and wanted to reuse that in vscode, but am unable to do so. I can clearly see status of `tablepro` as running by Cmd + Shift + P > List MCP servers > Search 'tablepro' > shows "running"
input.title: settings > chat.mcp.discovery.enabled does not pick up servers

    'diff --git a/app/components/helper/card-filter.js b/app/components/helper/card-filter.js\n' +
    'new file mode 100644\n' +
    'index 0000000..6528321\n' +
    '--- /dev/null\n' +
    '+++ b/app/components/helper/card-filter.js\n' +
    '@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@\n' +
    "+import Component from '@glimmer/component';\n" +
    "+import { tracked } from '@glimmer/tracking';\n" +
    "+import { action } from '@ember/object';\n" +
    '+\n' +
    '+/**\n' +
    '+ * Reusable card filter bar component.\n' +
    '+ *\n' +
    '+ * Usage:\n' +
    '+ *   \n' +
    '+ *\n' +
    '+ * @filters      ΓÇô current active filter values (hash of key ΓåÆ value, "all" means no filter)\n' +
    '+ * @filterDefinitions ΓÇô array of { key, label, options: [{ value, label, count? }] }\n' +
    '+ * @onFilterChange    ΓÇô action called with (key, value) when a pill is clicked\n' +
    '+ */\n' +
    '+export default class HelperCardFilterComponent extends Component {\n' +
    '+  get filterGroups() {\n' +
    '+    const defs = this.args.filterDefinitions || [];\n' +
    '+    const active = this.args.filters || {};\n' +
    '+\n' +
    '+    return defs.map((def) => ({\n' +
    '+      key: def.key,\n' +
    '+      label: def.label,\n' +
    '+      options: def.options.map((opt) => ({\n' +
    '+        ...opt,\n' +
    "+        active: (active[def.key] || 'all') === opt.value,\n" +
    '+      })),\n' +
    '+    }));\n' +
    '+  }\n' +
    '+\n' +
    '+  get hasActiveFilters() {\n' +
    '+    const active = this.args.filters || {};\n' +
    "+    return Object.values(active).some((v) => v && v !== 'all');\n" +
    '+  }\n' +
    '+\n' +
    '+  @action\n' +
    '+  toggleFilter(key, value) {\n' +
    '+    if (this.args.onFilterChange) {\n' +
    '+      const current = (this.args.filters || {})[key];\n' +
    '+      // Toggle off if already selected (revert to "all")\n' +
    "+      this.args.onFilterChange(key, current === value ? 'all' : value);\n" +
    '+    }\n' +
    '+  }\n' +
    '+\n' +
    '+  @action\n' +
    '+  clearAll() {\n' +
    '+    if (this.args.onClearFilters) {\n' +
    '+      this.args.onClearFilters();\n' +
    '+    }\n' +
    '+  }\n' +
    '+}\n' +

Type: Bug ### Summary The Codex panel hangs forever on the loading spinner (logo only, chat never loads). ### Environment - Codex extension: openai.chatgpt-26.616.31447 (linux-x64) - codex-cli: 0.142.0-alpha.1 - OS: Linux 6.17 (x86_64) - VSCode: desktop ### What happened With two VSCode windows open, each window spawned its own `codex app-server` process. The extension's IPC initialize timed out and the CODEX panel stayed on the loading spinner indefinitely, with no error shown in the UI. ### Relevant log lines (exthost/openai.chatgpt/Codex.log) [info] [CodexMcpConnection] Spawning codex app-server (x2, one per window) [warning] [IpcClient] Initialize failed errorMessage=timeout at Timeout._onTimeout (.../out/extension.js:481:18725) [warning] [IpcClient] Received broadcast but no handler is configured method=client-status-changed [warning] cli: ... featured plugin request ... failed with status 401 Unauthorized ### Workaround that fixed it 1. Killed both stale `codex app-server` processes (`pkill -f 'codex app-server'`) 2. Reloaded the window (Developer: Reload Window), keeping a single VSCode window 3. Signed in to Codex ### Suspected issues 1. IpcRouter contention when multiple VSCode windows run the extension ΓåÆ duplicate app-servers and an Initialize timeout. 2. On Initialize timeout the UI shows only a spinner with no error/retry/sign-in prompt, so the user has no indication of what's wrong. input.body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **Yes** **VS Code Version:** 1.125.0 **OS Version:** Windows 11 ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open VS Code Chat. 2. Create a chat session (tested with Claude). 3. Open the chat session list. 4. Right-click the chat session. ## Expected Behavior The context menu should provide an option to delete/remove the chat session. ## Actual Behavior No delete/remove option appears in the context menu. The only available option is **Archive**, which hides the chat session but does not delete it. ## Additional Information This issue is reproducible with extensions disabled. Screenshot attached.
input.title: Delete chat session option unavailable
